english_meaning: short form for '³ada' "elder brother"—ओ़ह युहलगी आ ग्‍युर्मी माएँज्‍यी येहकें।In that village there was a man called elder brother Gyurmi.
short form for '³aʑi' "elder sister"—राहम्‍नेला आइ साम्‍देन येहबा येहना काहजे लु लेहन्‍ज्‍ये येहबा!If elder sister Samden is at the dedication festival she sings a lot!
uncle (father's brother, or husband of mother's sister, stepfather)
bend s.th. outwards—केम्‍दाङ आङज्‍ये मेहयोहङगे, मिहन्‍सीमाना छ्या़गे।You mustn't pull the bamboo tongs apart, otherwise they will break.
be in a dilemma—का़हन्‍दी का़हन्‍दी ल़ेह पेहच्‍ये, ङाह आङमे थाङमे काहल्‍सीन!Which work shall I do now, I am in dilemma!
wrong s.b—ख्‍यागी दि बानला तोहङबो तुप्‍सीमाना ङि्यहला आङ्या पेहक्‍यो यिहम्‍बा।If you cut trees in this forest, you are doing us an injustice.
ouch (used when feeling a burning or pricking pain)—आछा, मेहगी छी़सीन!Ouch, I got burned!—आथा, खापकी स़ूहसीन!Ouch, I got stung!
four-paisā coin—डिबु ज़्‍यिगी आना च्‍यी़ ओहङगे।Four paisaì make an 'þana'.
expressing admiration—लाप्‍तार सुल्‍गार केहन्‍दे, त़ेङगा कार्पु केहन्‍दे, च्‍यि सोङ ज्‍यिबा लेह! आपा आपा!Wearing a Nepali outfit and a white woolen jacket, how smart, just look at him!

come to a decision quickly (without much deliberation)—नामा लाङ डोएँ ग्‍या़ला मोहराङगीराङ आप्‍ता च्‍येती, “मेहयोहङगे” म़ेदी लो!When they went to ask for the bride, she herself made a quick decision and said, she wouldn't accept the proposal.
expressing surprise or fear—आबी! ल्‍हान्‍डी ओहङसीन!Ohhh, this is a ghost!

expressing disapproval or fear—मिक्‍या च्‍यिॱआङ फी़ मी़हखु दु, आबोउ!Oh dear, he can't even wipe his eye secretion off!—आबोउ ज्‍यी़बा! ता फ़ूला च्‍येन ओहङगु दु!How dangerous, a tiger is coming from over there!

original_word: आम च्‍याक च्‍याक/ आम प्‍लेगे/ आम प्‍लेक प्‍लेक प्‍लेक
pronunciation: ⁴am ⁴tɕak ⁴tɕak (E)/ ⁴am ⁴plege/ ⁴am ⁴plek ⁴plek ⁴plek (W)

english_meaning: get fed up (mostly used for food)—सेहन माहङबु स़ेहदी पेहजाॱयाला आम लो़हसीन दु।The children got fed up with eating corn mash all the time.
goddess residing on the top of 'Yangri Kahng'
handful-sized lump—माहइ पी़हरुला तेर्च्‍येला पोल्‍दा आम्‍ज्‍याङ ज़़ेदोङ!Make handful-sized lumps of this dough to give to the buffalo calf!
doctor who practises herbal medicine (usually a Tibetan monk)—ओहले आम्‍ज्‍यी मेहमे ङोहवाङ माहर फेप नाङगेराङ येहकेन।And grandfather Ngohwang, the doctor was on his way to come here.
dig one's teeth into food—ङि्यहगी मागी आम्‍बुर क्‍याहप्‍ती साहक्‍यो।We dug our teeth into the corn on the cob.
expressing a sudden pain—आया! लाहक्‍पा टेम्‍दी!Ouch! my hand got caught!
equipment for distilling whisky—छ्याङ तेन्‍गे आर्चाङ।the equipment for making whisky.
expressing a lasting pain—आलाउ! ङाह गो़ सुह क्‍याहप्‍सीन!O dear! I have a terrible headache!
fine s.b. for damage done by cattle in fields
hope for—ङाहला च्‍यिॱआङ ज्‍योर्के नि म़ेदी आ़से पेहक्‍यो।I hoped I would get something.
do wrong—आसात्‍या पेहक्‍योदीला छ्या़ छाल च्‍य़ूएँ।The one who has done wrong has to bow down ceremonially in the temple.
1uncle (mother's brother, or husband of father's sister)
expressing admiration or excitement—आहा! काहजे खाम्‍साङा!Oh! How much fun!
oh, (I made a mistake)—आहा, दिदी माहयोहङबा!Oh, this won't do!—ए, ङाहइ जोल्‍सीन!Oh, I made a mistake!
astonishment/ foreboding—दि स़ेह या़हबा आ़ह!Oh, how nice this is!—ख्‍येनी काहवा आ़ह!Oh, you are well off!—काहन्‍डुम पेहते डोज्‍ये आबा? ङाहनी ज्‍यी़बा आ़ह!How can I go father, I am afraid!
wide open—कोह आहङा ओहङे खा फ़ेक्‍यो दुबा!Oh, the door is wide open!—फुक्‍पा आहङे ओहङे दु!Oh, this is such a wide cave.
open—कोह आहङे दु।The door is open.—काहम खा आहङे दु।The lid of the chest is open.
expressing wonder or astonishment—ज्‍यिबुराङ खाम्‍साङबु ओहङदे, आहठे, राहम्‍नेलानी!The dedication festivities were such a lot of fun, really!
this way—ओ़ आज्‍यी, देन्‍डे ताम यिहम्‍बा!Yes sister, this is the way it is!
this way—ङि्यह पोहम्‍बो ज़ोएँ माएँदीॱगा आहन्‍डाबार नाहले, आज्‍यी त़े आ़!Look sister, this is the way we become a shaman!
intensifier for '¹tuh-, ³khyer-, ³kyaa ³khyer-, ¹tɕooh-'—दि पेला थाम्‍ज्‍यीराङ आहप–उहप ती़हदी चेल्‍बाला ल़ू!Gather up all these things swiftly and put it into the carrying basket.

asking for affirmative response—आज्‍यी त़े आ़, ङि्यह मिहॱया नेहपा डोएँ उइ!Look sister, we people become sick, don't we?—ता़ह तिहरीङ छ़ेबा ज़्‍यि उइ!Now today is the forth day of the lunar month, isn't it?
cover up—खोगी ओ़ह ओम तोहगी उप ज़्‍या़एँ लो।He said he would cover up that hole with stones.
urge s.b—खुङगी, “ख्‍ये डोॱराङ कोहएँ” म़ेदी उर्चु पेहकें।They are urging you to go.
request to come—तिहरीङ ओ़हला जोम कोहएँ लो, उर्ती पेहकु दु।He is requesting people to gather there today.
strike out—प्रेहकागी उहङडा लाङदी तेर्केन बेलानी सोदीदी याहर क्‍येर लाहङसीन दु लो!When he struck out and hit the good luck, it jumped up to his feet and stood straight up!
intensifier for '¹tiih-, ¹tuh-, ³kyaa-'—मेहनी उहप(रोहवा) ती़हदी काहल्‍दी!The fire devoured everything!—नेहपानी ज्‍युङा उहप ती़हदी काहल्‍दी!The sick person died in one moment!
fly—च्‍यिहबी-दाङ च्‍याहमु उहर्कें।Birds and chickens fly.
intensifier for '⁴thuŋ-'—थुक्‍पा उहर उहर थुङगे।I am drinking up the soup quickly.

intensifier for '¹tiih-, ³tshar-, ²sihn-'—ङि्यकाराङ उहर ती़हदे थाला फुङे छी़ दु लो।Both caught fire in one moment and burnt up to completely.—तिहरीङ सा कोज्‍येनी उहरुरु सिहन्‍सीन।Today we finished digging up the soil with gusto.
exaggerate—ङाह पोहला ओहङदी उहर्स्‍यु ज्‍यिबा ताम माएँ।She comes to me and exaggerates everything terribly.
flattish piece of wood used to pull out the warp threads alternately
exaggerate—ङाह पोहला ओहङदी उहर्स्‍यु ज्‍यिबा ताम माएँ।She comes to me and exaggerates everything terribly.
flattish piece of wood used to pull out the warp threads alternately
we (inclusive)—आबा आ़, दिनी ऊह आमा मिहन लेह!Father, this is not our mother!—ऊहदी खा डोएँ च्‍यि, ऊहदी मेननी मेहन्‍डो।Our mouth keeps working, our battery doesn't run out.
pick (flowers, leaves, fruit)—पेहजाॱयागी स्‍याउ ओ़उ दु।Oh, the children are picking apples.
expressing agreement—यिहम्‍बा ओ़ आज्‍यी!Yes, I agree with you, elder sister!
particle added to the name (or term of address) of a person called out to from a considerable distance—आमा ओउ! देला डोम्‍बो ओहङ दु, स्‍यो!Mother, mother! Guests have arrived, please come!
member (of human body)—ओहराङगी जुबुला ओङबु छि्यम्‍बुदी गो़ यिहम्‍बा।The most important member on our body is the head.
beg instently—ङाहगी किन्‍ज्‍योला ओस्‍ये स्‍युहएँ, ङाहला दि तुहक्‍पुलेगी तेन नाङदोङ!Oh God, I beg you instently, please take me out of this trouble!
come—ङाह ओहङ ओहङलानी मोहनी फेत थाल्‍दे काहल दुबा!Oh I see, while I was coming she went ahead swiftly.
1be (in the sense of general quality or existence)
commandment—साङग्‍येगी का माएँदी ओहराङ का यिहम्‍बा।The commandment of the god is our commandment.—खोला किन्‍ज्‍योगी का बाप्‍क्‍योॱराङ यिहम्‍बा!He did get the word of God.
get a shock—दि ताम थ़े–लुहङ म़ेहबा का क्‍याहल्‍दी थोमे लिहसीन।When I heard this, I got a shock and was completely perplexed.
graciousness—च्‍यि पेह ख़ूक्‍यो यिहन्‍ज्‍ये ले, किन्‍ज्‍योगी का–टेहन नाहले!Whatever I could do, it's the grace of God!

numeral group—ङि्यकार देला स्‍यो!Both of you come here!—तिङमे ङि्यहमुदी काङबा ङि्यकादीराङ एक्‍स रेइ तेन्‍सीन।The next day they made an x-ray of both legs.—च्‍याहमु सुम्‍गाराङ स्‍यिदी!All three hens died!

verb stem nominalizer—तिहरीङ ख्‍योङग्‍यो च्‍यिनी कार्का लोहङ मा़हखु।The weight of the sugar I brought today was less than what I asked for.—दि पा़लो ब़्रे ताहप्‍का लोहङसीन दु।Oh, today the rice makes up the expected quantity!
portal-like structure spanning the path, decorated with religious symbols
Kakani (village on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley, after Timbu on the way to Langgyapsa)
very winding—लाहम काक्‍ताङ कुक्‍तीङ पेहती डोएँ।It's a very winding path.
fill—दि स्‍येल्‍बुङला छ्यु काङदोङ ले!Fill this bottle with water.—दि स्‍येल्‍बुङला ओहमागी काङदोङ।This bottle you fill with milk.
drive away anticipated hardship by placating the house god/ spirit—काङसु ताङगेन बेले ली़ क्‍युर्केँ।During the 'þkaÊsu' ritual millet flour statues are thrown out.
Raithan (first Yohlmu settlement on the ridge when coming up from Melamchi Pul)
huddled up—नोहनोनी कातार कोतोर पेहती तेहकु दु!Oh, little brother is sitting there huddled up!
huddled up—रोहला कान्‍दार कुन्‍दुर पेहते ज़्‍या़एँ नाहले।The corpse is placed into a huddled up position.
thick (of sauces, mashes, milk, etc.)—बालु कान्‍दी काहल दु।Oh, this grain beer is too thick.

cover—ङ्याहल तेहकेन बेले स्‍यिराक काप्‍कें।For sleeping you cover yourself with a quilt.—खाङबा काप्‍च्‍येला तोहङले तुहङमा ज़्‍या़ कोहएँ।To build the roof of a house you first must put the horizontal rafters into place.
supporting post (in houses and temples)—ओहले ङेनगी कोहम्‍बाला काप्‍स्‍यी च्‍यी़रु येहबा।And in the old temple there are 16 supporting posts.
{receive} gift (in the sense of charisma)—खोराङला टाहम्‍ङ्येन तुहङगेन्‍गी काबाप थोप्‍क्‍यो।He received the gift of playing the string instrument.—किन्‍ज्‍योगी काबाप नाङगे।God gives gifts.
bring under control—ङि्यहगी मिह ङ्योक्‍योॱया नाहरेराङ कायाम ज़ोएँ!The people who are insane (because of spirit possession) we bring under control in one moment!
weigh—योहल्‍मुला उमालीला माहर कार्कें।In Helambu butter is weighed with scales.
lie on a hard spot—तिहरीङ ङाहला ङ्याहल्‍साला कार्ती!Last night I lay on a hard spot!—तिहरीङ ङाहला ङ्याहल्‍सा कार्ते!Last night I lay on a hard spot!
it's an odd number—छ्याप नि कार? म़े!Is it an even or an odd number? Tell me!—ओ़ह रेहमु क्‍याहप्‍च्‍येला टोहङबा कार कोहएँ।For this pattern you need an odd number of stitches.
offer a milk product instead of blood—ठा़ तेर्केन ग्‍या़ला ओहमा तोर्केँ, ओहले कार ताङगे माएँ।Instead of offering blood, milk is sprinkled about, and this is called 'ÿkar ÿtaÊgen'.
small iron pot for frying (bowl-shaped with handles)

apply dabs of butter on food or drink offered to gods or given as gifts to people—स्‍याहल्‍गार च़ूएँ बेलाले ताहम्‍बे खाला कार्क्‍येन सुम क्‍याहप कोहएँ।When you present a ceremonial gift of alcohol, you must apply three dabs of butter on the bottle's mouth.—काहङरी कार्पे खावा, कार्क्‍येन डासा मिहन्‍दु।There is white snow on the mountains, but nothing is like the butter mark.
weigh and keep record of a person's sins and merit—स्‍यिन्‍डी छ्य़ेगी क्‍याहल्‍बुगी तिहक्‍पा-दाङ फायीनगी कार्ताङ ज़्‍या़दाङ जे नाङगे लो।It is said the judge of the dead weighs their sins and merits.
scraps of cloth—पोहम्‍बोॱयागी सुहङबु छ्येकेन बेले कार्ना मार्नाॱया कोहएँ।When the shaman sacrifices an animal to the spirits some scraps of colored cloth are needed.
white (used only for white female animal)—ओहले जोमो कार्मुज्‍यी तोर्सीन दु लो।And a white 'dzomo' got lost.
{be} a drought—काहर्नाङ ओहङसीमाना बा़ली रेबे नोहङगे, ओहले मिह तो़बा लिहकें।When there is a drought the crops fail and people go hungry.

strong in taste—तिहरीङ थोङसे मार्चा काहर्पा काहल दु।Oh, today the curry is too peppery.—च्‍याह काहपा।र्strong tea.—च्‍याह छा काहर्पा।too salty tea.—च्‍याह च्‍यिनी काहर्पा।too sweet tea.—छ्याङ काहर्पा।strong beer, strong whisky
pole—ताम्राला काहस्‍यीङ तेर कोहएँ।Beens must have poles.
1inauspicious sign on the calendar—कालला पा़रती़सीमाना टोहङबा ख्‍योङज्‍ये मेहयोहङगे।You must not get married on a day which has the inauspicious sign of "kal".
load—मिन–टोहल्‍मागी ओ़ह डुल–मिह ङ्यी़ला लेहङगु काल दु लो।Min-Tohlma gave the two travelers a message to carry.
a kind of sour fruit (malla dubla)
very difficult—दि ताम लोप्‍च्‍येला काले का़हबु म़ेह।It is not very difficult to learn this language.
share of food—ख्‍यिम आमागी म़ेहमे जाम्‍मालाराङ काल्‍दो क़ेह तेर्कें।The mother of the home dishes out to each family member his share of food.
settlement in the western part of Yohlmu, in the upper Tadi Khola valley, north of Kulu
bit (for the horse)—ताबुगी काह च्‍या़गी ज़ोक्‍यो ओहङगे।The bit for the horse is made of iron.
where—दि का़हगी मिह, का़हगी मिह??Where could this person be from? ?
how big?—क़े काह–छ्योवा तेन्‍ज्‍ये?How high (or loud) shall I sing?

extremely, very much—ओहले ख्‍यिम च्‍यी़ टोहङरो ङ्यी़गी पी़हमी काहकालाराङ जामु येहकेन दु लो।And the two close neighbor ladies got along with each other extremely well.—ओहले मोहनी –च्‍याहङ पोहम्‍बोगी युहलला मेहन्‍डो’ म़ेदे काहकालाराङ तुहङाल पेह तेहके नाहले!She said, "I am not going to the village of the north shaman!" And she worried terribly.
on top of (e.g. on top of shoulder, head, etc.)—युहल्‍दुम गो़ काहङला ज़्‍या़दी डोउ दु।He is walking along with a bundle on top of his head.
at the time of "verbing"—ङि्यह डोएँ काहङला ख्‍ये लेपॱराङ माहलेप येहकें।When we left you hadn't arrived yet.—डोडोपा काहङला।after having walked for some time.—ख्‍या़बु काहङला।during the cold season.
Gangjawal (village on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River, about 1 1/2 hours' walk north of Sermathang on the way to Langgyapsa)

hold counsel—ओ़ह युहलगी गोबा-दाङ लामा थाम्‍ज्‍यीगी काहटी पेहती थाङगु प्रुहज्‍ये म़ेदी साला डी़सीन।All the leaders and lamas of that village held counsel, and they decided that they would have thangkas painted.
which one—दि स्‍या़हमा ङ्यी़ला का़हन्‍दी या़हबु ऩेगे?Which one of these two back-aprons do you like better?
bite (preceded by snapping)—दि क्‍यिबुगी मिहला काहप्‍कें।This dog bites people.
big—ओहलेनी पेहजा ङ्यी़ छार्ते काहपु काहपु काहल्‍दे लो।And the two children grew and became big.
Newari goldsmith—दि सेर या़हबु चोक्‍पा ताहक्‍स्‍यार काहबागी पेहकें।The Newari goldsmith will ascertain whether this is pure gold or not.
difficult—खोला देला तेहच्‍येला का़हबु ओहङगु दु।Oh, it is difficult for him to stay here.
hair of grain ears—च्‍याङदा-दाङ जाउला काहमा माहङबु ओहङगे।The barely and oat ears have lots of hair.

{make} written marriage contract with agreements for restitution in the case of divorce (an optional custom)—पा़हमा पेहकेन बेलाले पान्‍जाॱयागी पी़हमीगी ला़गीला काहम्‍ग्‍या प्रुहएँ।When getting married the witnesses write a marriage agreement for the woman.
obstacles on paths (rocks, etc.)—लाहमला स्‍यिङ, छेर्मा काहराक कोहरोचक ल़ेहमुॱया साल्‍सीमाना फायीन ओहङगे।If you clear the path of obstacles like fallen trees or thorny brushwood you gain merit.
rocky, uneven (ground)—काहराङ कोहरोङ नाहङले फारकी डो कोहउ दु।Oh, we have to go through this rocky area.
{get/ end up with} scales of dirt (on the skin)—काङबा लाहक्‍पाॱया ठुॱराङ मिहठुसीमाना काहल्‍बा छ्या़एँ।If you don't wash your feet and hands you end up with scales of dirt on them.
tickle—पासाङगी ङि्यहला कितीक ङो़एँ।Pasang is always tickling us.
the whole—लुहङबा किनॱराङ क्‍यासाङे ओहङगे।The whole world/ region is light.
{perform} a dance—मिह क़ेहङगेनॱया किन्‍ज्‍याम नाङगु दु।Oh, the old people are dancing.
creator God—च्‍यि पेह ख़ूक्‍यो यिहन्‍ज्‍ये ले, किन्‍ज्‍योगी का-टेहन नाहले!Whatever I could do, it's the grace of God!
flea—किस्‍यीगी आ़ ताप्‍ते नाहले!The flies bit me!
flea—ओ़हला किस्‍यीक्‍पागी ङाहला ङ्याहलॱराङ मा़हच्‍युबा, नुहप काहङॱराङ आ़ ताप्‍ती!There the fleas did not let me sleep, they bit me the whole night!
small village on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley, below Sermathang, near Kolama
Ghopʈe Pãʈi (lodge settlement between ʈhãrepãʈi and Gosainkunɖa)
have a loss—कार्लु या़हबु तेर्नानी ङाहला की़ह मेहक्‍याहप्‍केँ।If you give me good weight I will not have a loss.
in four days (four days ahead)—ङाह क्‍यी़ह ङि्यहमु खाङबाला डोएँ।I will go home in four days.
{make} an idol; statue—ओहले कुन्‍डा ज़्‍याङगेन्‍गी ल़ेह बाँकी येहकेन। तिङले कुॱया़ङ ज़्‍याङ छार्सीन।Then the making of the idols was left to do. Later the idols were also finished.
verb stem nominalizer—ङाहगी माएँगी ताम ङि्यहङगु ज़्‍यो़!Take note of what I am saying!—ओहराङ ङ्यी़दी तेर्कु लेहङगु म़ेह।We don't owe each other anything.

be able to keep up with the activity of vs—दि पेहजा डोगु छ्येपा?– छ्येसीन।Was this child able to keep up with you on the way? - Yes.—दि पेहजा लाहमला डोगु छ्येकोॱवा?Will this child be able to keep up with us? (when walking).—खोगीॱआङ रो़ह ङ्याम्‍बु टा़हगु छ्येको ले?Do you think he will be able to keep up with his friend in reading?—खोगी तेर कोहएँ पेलाॱया तेर्कु छ्येकोॱवा?Will he be able to give the things he needs to give?

imperfect aspect—खो ओहङगु दु।He is just coming.—ङाह डोउ येह।I am about to go.—यिबीलानी पेताम ओहङगो येहके।Grandmother used to know stories.—तोहमगी साहवाराङ मागी नोहङ दु।Because the bears came to feed on it the corn harvest is spoiled.—ता़ह ओहराङ खोगी पेहकोॱवाह ङाहगी पेहकोॱवाह म़ेदी क़ूहदी तेहकें।Now we are waiting for each other to act (and nobody is doing anything).
swing—ङाहॱआङ कुकु चाज्‍ये, टो़हबु!I also want to swing, my special friend!

bent (e.g. of a tree, not at the top but at the middle or lower part, stick, path)—ताहङ कुक्‍तीङ पेहती ख्‍येर कोहएँ।You must speak with contour tones.—तोहङबो रिन कुक्‍तीङ दु।Oh, the tree trunk is crooked.
fast—छ़ेबा क्‍य़ेहला ङि्यह जाम्‍माराङ कुक्‍पा तेहकेन।On the eighth day of the lunar month we all fast.
common fund—दि पा़लो राहङ राहङ मिहम्‍बा कुङगी खार्चा पेहकें।This time we will pay the expenses from the common fund, not each one separately.
pull up limbs—काङबा कुङदी तेहकेन।sit with knees pulled up.—लाहक्‍पा फार कुङ पेहतोङ!Bend your arm towards the other side!
lamp stand for butter lamp—कुङुला काबे टिहल्‍दी च़ू कोहएँ।The raw cotton wool must be twisted and put into the lamp stands

crippled—ङि्यह आज्‍यीइ काङबा कुच्‍या काहल्‍दी येहबा माना आज्‍यीइ सेमला फो़गे, खोटो काहल्‍दी येहबा मा कोहएँ।If you say "our sister's foot is crippled" she feels hurt, you must say "her foot is incomplete".
burn a piece of paper which carries the name of the deceased person
picture of a venerated person—छ्येटीला दाले लामागी कुबार ज़्‍या़गे।On the house altar we put a picture of the Dalai Lama.

{give} reward for ritual services  (in cash)—लामा, पोहम्‍बोगी कुरीम पेहतीले च्‍यिहन्‍दागी कुयेन क्‍याहप्‍कें।When the lamas or shamans have performed rituals the sponsors give them a reward in money.—ओहले थाङगु प्रुहक्‍योगी कुयेन खुङगी ङाहला फुल्‍सीन।And then they gave me the reward (pay) for painting the thangkas.
fall over—लोपॱस़े पेहती फेप, मेहमे, कुर–ताप स्‍योरॱवा!Walk slowly, grandfather, you might fall over!
big hoe with short handle—कुरीइ सा क़ेदी क्‍याहगार च़ूएँ।The big hoe is used to dig up the fields for planting millet.

an individual or a family sponsor a ceremony for monks, nuns, lamas, or shamans—ल्‍हा, लु, ती़ह, चेनॱया क्‍योङना, दि जाम्‍माराङ कुरीम पेहक्‍यो यिहम्‍बा।When we placate gods, forest spirits, demons and other evil spirits, this all is called 'þkurim pehken'.

take turns in giving house parties—लोहसारला योहल्‍मु मिहॱया कुरीम्‍बा पेहकें।At the beginning of the New Year the people in Yohlmu take turns in giving house parties.—तिहरीङ ङि्यह ग्‍या़ला कुरीम्‍बा येहबा।Today we have a house party at our place.
joke—ङाहगी मेहमेला कुर्स्‍या स्‍युहक्‍यो।I am just telling you a joke.—मेहमेगी कुर्स्‍या सुङगे/ जेकेँ।Grandfather is joking.

order—ल़ेह कुल्‍गे मिह नाहक्‍पु नाहक्‍पु ओहङगु दु।The people who give the orders for the work are very black.—ओहले ङाहइ ल़ेहमी कुल ज़्‍या़दी ग्‍यो़बा ग्‍यो़बा लो़हदी ओहङदी!And then, having arranged for field workers, I came back quickly!
village in the western part of Yohlmu, in a corner below Gosainkunɖa range; outsiders call it Kulu, the inhabitants talk of Tsyang-Sahzyu and Kyah-Thang

yearly celebration performed for the upkeep of religious monuments, especially to repaint them—कुवार सोल्‍गेन बेलाले माहनीॱयाला सागार कार्पु क्‍याहप्‍कें।When the religious monuments are renovated the prayer walls are whitewashed.
clear up in the evening after a rainy day (usually red clouds can be seen)—तिहरीङ कुहङ–साङ तेन दु।Oh, today it has cleared up.
effigy representing the dead person during the '¹kyehwa' rituals—ओहले क्‍येहवा ङि्यहमुदी कुहर ज़ोएँ काहल्‍सीन।And on the day on which the death rites are performed and effigy has to be made.
slowly—ल़ेह कुहल्‍बु पेहकु दु।Oh, he is working slowly—खो कुहल्‍बु डोउ दु।He is going with slow movements.
1bend s.th. down (e.g. a branch)—ङाहगी याहल्‍गाला क़ूसीन।I bent down the branch.
corner which is the result of bending s.th. (e.g. bamboo when weaving it)
call for a visit—देला याहरकी मिहॱया जाम्‍माराङ डोम्‍बु केती लो!I heard that they invited all the people from up here!

person hired to look after a temple—कोहम्‍बा सुङगेन्‍गी क़ेङ्येरला च्‍यिहन्‍दागी फुल्‍ग्‍यो स़ेहॱयागी फोवा च्‍याहङगे।The person who looks after the temple lives from the things the adherents offer to the temple.

{talk about} a matter—ओहले ङाह काहल्‍सीम्‍बाराङ ङाहइ ची़री कोहम्‍बागी केच्‍याॱया जाम्‍माराङ क्‍याहल्‍बुला स्‍येक्‍यो यिहम्‍बा।And then I went and told the king everything about the temple of Tsiiri.—ख्‍या म़ेहमेॱयागी च्‍यि केच्‍या लाहप तेहक्‍यो?What are you ladies talking about?
dress s.b. else—पेहजाॱयाला काप्‍च्‍या केन तेर/ केन्‍दोङ!Please dress the children!

{climb} steep incline—योहल्‍मुला डोज्‍ये यिहन्‍सीमाना केमा माहङबु जा़ कोहएँ।If you go to Helambu, you have to climb a lot of steep paths.—दि लाहम ज्‍यिबुराङ केमा दु।Oh, this path goes ascends very steeply.

concomitant action participle 1—ओहले कोहम्‍बा ज़्‍याङगेमुॱआङ छ्या़ने क्‍याहप्‍केमुॱआङ बा़न्‍ज्‍याम क्‍याहप्‍कें।And when the building of a temple is started, as well as when it is dedicated, masked dances are performed.—कोहरेला ता डोएँमुनी केहपुनी मिहन्‍दु लो।When she went to look in the cattle shed the old man was not there, she said.
decided future—ङ्यहगी पोहमो तेर्केम्‍बा।We have decided to give our daughter.—दि पा़लो पोखराला ङि्यहॱआङ डोगेम्‍बा/ डोएँम्‍बा।We have decided that we too are going to Pokhara this time.

imperfect continuous aspect—आमा बालु नुर्केराङ येह।Mother is just mixing beer.—आदागी छ्य़े लोङगेराङ येह।Older brother is just searching in the books.—ओहले क्‍येप–ख्‍योवादीनी तोहङ–तोङ तोहङ–तोङ डोएँराङ येहके लो।And the man kept going ahead (of her).
thickening of sauces with flour—क्‍याहगार चाम्‍बा क़ेस्‍यीङ छि्यम्‍बु ओहङगे।Millet flour thickens (a soup) well.
clothes—याहप्‍च्‍येन ङ्याहम्‍दी फेप्‍सोङ, क़ेहला ल्‍हेम्‍बा फो़सोङ!My honoured father, dying has gone, and I had to patch my clothes!
clean—दि ग्‍या़ क़ेह–चाङमा दु।This is a very clean place!—नो़हगी पेहजाॱया म़ेदीनी आदागी पेहजाॱया क़ेह–चाङा!The children of the older brother have cleaner habits than the ones of the younger brother!
evil spirit which particularly troubles people in relation to their wedding and marriage
rejoice—खो नाहम्‍माना क़ेहनाङ पेहकें।He always rejoices.
terrace wall (steep slope of terraced fields)

religious merit—स्‍यिम्‍बुगी छेदीला ल़ेह केहवा तिहक्‍पा ङोह मा़हस्‍येबा येहना तिहक्‍पा ता़हज्‍येगी छि्यरला मिह माहङबुगी माहनी टाहङ कोहएँ।The deceased person may not have been much aware of works of merit or sin when he was alive; so in order that his sin may be wiped out many people must recite ritual prayers.
three quarters full or long—माना को़।3/4 of a "maana".—ठु को़-दाङ ङ्यी़।1 and 3/4 cubits.
small rake (used to pull together dry leaves)
red colour applied to the rice statues
rare—ङाहनी ज्‍यिबुराङ टेहम्‍बा कोम्‍बु येहबा!I am very forgetful!

reciprocal system of eating at each others' death rites and other festivals sponsored by individual families—दि ङि्यहराङ क्‍येहवादी ङाहगी सेमला क्‍येएँ ल़ेहमु यिहन्‍सीमाना तोहङले ङि्यहराङ छ्यो़ थाताला काहल्‍दी ङि्यहराङगी कोर तुहसीन। ओहले येहपाराङ दि क्‍येहवा ताङसीन। क्‍येहवाला कोर लो़सीनIf it's the way I think with our death rites, we go to different places (to eat), and we get ritually indebted. Then again we perform death rites (ourselves) and so we reciprocate.
talk deviantly—कोर–ताम माज्‍येला खो ज्‍यिबुराङ ख़ेबु दु।He is very clever in distorting the truth.
walk—आमा छ्योर्तेन कोराला काहल्‍ग्‍यो।Mother has gone to the circle of Bauddha.
make a trip for a special purpose—ङि्यह लाङडाङला कोर्याङला डोज्‍ये।We want to go and meet somebody in LangÍang.
village on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley, located between Syuhnggan-Che and Nuhrpu-Kahng

divide s.th—ओहले आदा-दाङ नो़ह ङ्यी़इ फास्‍यी कोहसीन दु लो।And then the two brothers devided up the inheritance.—खाङबाला तो येहबा, ख्‍याराङ पिनॱया क़ेहदी सोह!There is food at home, divide it up among yourselves and eat it!
need—युहल–ठिम ज़ोज्‍याला ओहजे मिह कोहएँ।To establish the village laws you need that many people.
must—ता़हनी ङाह खाङबाला डो कोहसीन।I have to go home now.—ख्‍येगी ङाहला च्‍यिॱआङ तेर मेहकोहएँ।You don't need to give me anything.
fall asleep before the meal—दि पेहजाॱया कोह–ङि्य लो़हगे!These children fall asleep before the meal!
lama settlement with temple on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley (above the path between Kahng-Yuhl and Langgyapsa)
back apron of firm cotton material
firepit—ङाहगी कोहगा सासागी थेल्‍ग्‍यो।I plastered the firepit with mud.

hope—ङाह मिह लुहङबाला डो ङ्युहङज्‍यु म़ेदी कोहङबा पेहकें।I am hoping to get to go to a foreign country.—ल़ेह जाम्‍माराङ तेहन्‍दा डुप्‍च्‍यु म़ेदी ङाहगी कोहङबा पेहकें।I hope that all my plans will be fulfilled.rare
be upset—कोहङबा माहसुङ!Please don't get angry!/ Please don't mind!—आमोमो, काहन्‍डुमु कोहङबा सुङदे!Oh, he became very upset!

take one's leave—ओहले ङि्यह फ़ेबे सुम कुल्‍लु आस्‍पातालले डाक्‍टार ङ्याम्‍बु कोहङबा स्‍यी़हदी याहर याहम्‍बुला लो़ह ओहङसीन।And the three of us took leave from the doctor of the hospital in Kullu and returned up to Kathmandu.
apologize—दि ल़ेह जोल्‍ग्‍योगी ला़गीला ङाहगी कोहङबा स्‍युहक्‍यो, ओहले कोहङबा नाङग्‍यो।I apologized for my mistake, and he forgave me.
year (used when asking for people's ages)—मेहमेगी कोहङ–लोह काहजे काहल्‍बा?How old is grandfather?
vegetable garden near the house—कोहन्‍दुमला छ्याबी, कोहक्‍पा, छ़ेमा–ऱे च़ूगे।In the vegetable garden near the house we plant small onions, garlic, and spinach-like vegetables.
divide up—टाङगा कोहप्‍स्‍या क्‍याहप्‍ती खुङ ङ्यी़ दि दावा यिहन्‍ज्‍यु छोङ सो–सो पेहकें लो।They said they would divide up the money, and each one would pursue his own business starting this month.
sling across shoulders—जो़ला कोहम्‍दा क्‍याहप्‍केन।sling shoulder bag across shoulders.
Chitre (small settlement on the western slope of the Melamchi River valley, high above Kyahldum, near Chipling and Thodung)

1temple—दी़जो कोहम्‍बाला क़ेङ्येर युहल्‍बागी ज़्‍या़गे।For the community temple the villagers hire a person to look after the temple.—लामा खोराङगी ज़्‍याङग्‍यो कोहम्‍बाॱआङ ओहङगे।There are also temples which have been built by a lama personally.
cattle shed—याङरी काहङगी रिनला जोमो कोहरे माहङबु दु।Oh, at the foot of the Yangri peak there are many dzomo cattle sheds.
scales of dirt {accumulate}—मा़हठीबा पेहती तेहसीमाना ङ्योक्‍पागी को़हरे छ्या़गे।If you don't wash yourself, scales of dirt will accumulate.
lock—कोहल्‍ज्‍या क्‍याहप्‍केन।put on the lock.—कोहल्‍ज्‍या (खा) फ़ेगेन।unlock.
gate which is closed with sliding bars (for enclosure)—कोहल्‍दाङ क्‍याहप्‍तोङ!Close the gate!—कोहल्‍दाङ फ़ेदोङ!Open the gate!

original_word: क्‍या र्‍हाबा र्‍हिबे/ क्‍या र्‍हाबा र्‍हेबे
pronunciation: ³kya ³rhaba ³rhibe/ ³kya ³rhaba ³rhebe
english_meaning: at dawn (partly light)—खो क्‍या र्‍हाबा र्‍हिबेलाराङ लाहङदे काहल्‍सीन दु।I saw him getting up and leaving when is was just getting light.
flash of light {appears}—चिलीम क्‍याहप्‍केँ बेले नामला क्‍यासाङ/ क्‍या–हाङ पेहकेन थोङसीन।When it was lightning I saw the flashing in the sky.
concomitant action participle 2—ङाहनी पाता म़ेहबा छेर्के नाहले, छुर ओहङग्‍या। फार डोक्‍यानी क़ेहनाङ लाहङसीन!Coming back (from festivals) I get very sad; going over there I was rejoicing!

pronunciation: ³kyaakya/ ³kyaaya/³ kyakya/ ³kyaya/ ³kyaye {³ma-/ ²peh-k}

english_meaning: stretch one's limbs—ङाटु लाहङ फान्‍ज्‍यो ङाहगी लाहक्‍पा क्‍याङगे।In the morning when I get up I stretch my arms.
cupboard and shelves beside the left side of the fireplace
cultivate s.th. the first time—खुङगी हे क्‍याङबा साज्‍यी कोसीन।They broke the ground and weeded the first time in the potato fields.
perform personal worship—छ्य़े स्‍य़ेगेन मिहॱयागी क्‍याम्‍रु ताङगे।The people who know the books perform this personal worship.
take s.b./ s.th. to some other place
last (of things)—खाङबा च्‍यी़गी छे काहङ मेहक्‍याल दु।One house does not last for the whole of your life.
1purse (leather bag to store money and precious metals)
squander—खोगी ङाहगी टाङगा फोक क्‍या़हसीन दु!He squandered my money just like that!
stretch s.th—दि थाक्‍पा क्‍याहङ कोहएँ।This rope has to be stretched.
stretch one's limbs apart—काङबा क्‍याहङसीम्‍बाराङ लाहङदी तेहतोङ!Stand with legs astride!—प्राहङज्‍या ताप्‍च्‍येला लाहक्‍पा क्‍याहङ कोहएँ।In order to hug somebody you have to spread out your arms.
Pheedi? (lodge settlement between ʈhãrepãʈi and Gosainkunɖa)
1plains—क्‍याहदाङला क्‍याह–लाहम ओहङगे।In the plains there are big roads.
throw—खुङगी थो़ले माहर बासोला तोह क्‍याहप्‍सीन।They threw stones at the cattle from above.—खुङ बासोला ङि्यहइ च्‍यिबे तोह क्‍याहप्‍च्‍ये?Why should we throw stones at their animals?
kingdom—ताहङबु ताहङबु प़ेह क्‍याहल–खापला दे–खाम ट़ूह येहबा लो।Formerly there were six districts in the kingdom of Tibet.
crown of cardboard with coloured pictures

{perform} funeral rites—क्‍येहवाला लामागी ऩे पार्ती स्‍यिम्‍बुला लाहम ताङगे।During the funeral rites the lama lights the ceremonial paper with the deceased persons name on it, and thus he sends the spirit (soul of the deceased) on his way.
crossing of paths on the ridge above Chhimi where the path from Chhimi and Sermathang meet
either... or—क्‍यि छे काहङ ओहङडो क्‍यि स्‍या़ह सुम ओहङडो।Either it will be for the whole life, or it will be for a few days only.

iron hook which is used to pull shut the inside door bar from outside—खाङबा निङबा येहकेन ग्‍या़ला कोहला क्‍यिन्‍ज्‍यी चाहङगे ओम ओहङगे।In older houses the door has a hole, through which the iron hook key can be pushed.
practise borrowing and lending—युहलगी मिहॱयागी टोहङरो नाहङदोङ क्‍यिबे यार्पे माहङबु पेहकेँ।Among neighbours the villagers practise a lot of borrowing and lending.
surround—क्‍यिबुॱयागी ङाहला क्‍यिल्‍दी काहप ओहङसीन!The dogs surrounded me and snapped at me!

middle—लाहम क्‍यिललाराङ ख़ेबु लोहक क्‍याहप्‍ती रिहल दु!In the middle of the road he fell flat on his nose!—कोटागी पाहर्क्‍यीलाराङ टेबुल ज़्‍या़ दुबा।Oh, they put a table into the very middle of the room.—ङि्यह ङ्यी़गी पाहर्क्‍यी/ क्‍यिलला तेह!हामी दुइको बीचमा बस!—दि क्‍याहल्‍बु क्‍याहल्‍मु ङ्यी़गी पाहर्क्‍यीला टाङगा ङा तेरक्‍यो यिहन दे।di ʼkyahlbu ʼkyahlmu  yii̤ɡi pahrkyi̤la ʼa ɡa ʼ a ter̤ʼkyo ʼyihn ʼde.For these brooches of the king and queen I paid five mohars for both together.
{bar} door with a wooden bar—ओहजेला कोह(ला) क्‍यिली क्‍याहप्‍ती येहकेन।At that time the door was barred.
cross one's legs—क्‍यिल्‍दुम च्‍य़े म़ेहस्‍ये दु, पी़मु च़ू तेहकु दु।Oh, she is not able to cross her legs, she sits kneeling on the floor.
Keldang (cattle shed settlement north of Yangri Kahng)
1bind—ओ़ह लुहन्‍दी च्‍यिपुदीला या़हबु पेहते क्‍यी़दे तुहङ दु लो।This one jackal he bound firmly and beat him up.
give no peace—दि पेहजाॱयागी ङाहला क्‍यी़राङ माहतेर!These children gave me no peace at all!—ओ़ह ल्‍हागी ङाहला क्‍यी़/ क्‍यिपु माहतेर।That god gave me no peace.
be about to vomit—क्‍य़ूज्‍ये म़ेदी क्‍यु लोहक लोहक पेहकु दु!He is about to throw up!
1throw away—खोगी काप्‍च्‍या पितेॱआङ फे क्‍युर्सीन दु।He took off his clothes and just threw them down.
money given to villagers when a new festival is established
1n.long, heavy stick—काहर्ता क्‍याहप्‍च्‍ये म़ेदी क्‍युहक्‍पा रिहङबु रिहङबु ख्‍योङ दु।Oh, he brought poles to build a fence.

{prepare} food supplies for festivals and other special occasions—दि पा़लो ङि्यहगी ना़हरागी क्‍युहप्‍च्‍ये ज़ो कोहज्‍ये येहबा।This year we have to prepare the food supplies for the 'ünaahra' festival.
intestines—माहइ क्‍युहमा साहएँ।The intestines of buffalos are eaten.
1sell (used in the context of carrying on trade)
chisel—ओम कोहर्मु फ्रुज्‍येला क्‍य़ून्‍दी कोहएँ।To carve round holes you need a chisel with a roundish blade.
move away—खुङ दि खाङबालेगी क्‍य़ेसीन।They moved away from this house.
move s.th—खुङगी कोहरे क्‍य़ेसीन।They moved the cattle shed.
distribute—ता़हनी डोम्‍बुॱयाला ज़्‍य़ेरो क्‍य़ेज्‍ये बेला काहल्‍सीन।Now it's time to distribute the fried wheat bread.
give birth—दि माहइगी पी़हरु सुम क्‍येसीन।This buffalo cow has given birth to three calves.
defilement caused by birth—सिउ येहकेन मिह क्‍ये-रिप (पाहङ-रिप)नाहङला स्‍य़ूहज्‍ये मेहयोहङगे।A person who wears rudraksha seeds as an amulet must not enter into a place defiled by birth.
weaving belt—था़ था़गेमु क्‍येपाला क्‍येदा क्‍याहप कोहएँ।For weaving you must put the weaving belt around your waist.
cow or buffalo which has recently given birth to a calf—दि पाहलाङ क्‍य़ेमा यिहम्‍बा।This cow has recently given birth to a calf.
get up (intensified)—सोदीदी याहर क्‍येर लाहङसीन दु लो!The good fortune jumped right up onto his feet!
straight and stiff—क्‍येरे लाहङदेॱआङ तेहकु दु।He remains standing straight up.
very small settlement north of Chhimi in the forest
enter into a different form of life—ला़ खासे मिहगी क्‍यिबुगी क्‍येवा माहलेहन्‍ज्‍यु!If possible, people should not take the life of a dog.

adorn—पी़हम–पेहजाॱया ज़ाप्‍कें बेले पेलाॱया क्‍येहन्‍दी येहना ज्‍यार्पु ओहङगे।When the women dress up, it looks nice if they adorn themselves with all sorts of ornaments.—क्‍याहल्‍बुला राहना रिहने क्‍येहन्‍दी दु।The king is adorned with all sorts of ornaments.

cast lots—खुङगी स्‍यिहङ काहन्‍डुमु पेहती कोहक्‍यो माना, क्‍येहन क्‍याहप्‍ती राहङ राहङ मिहनला थोप्‍क्‍यो।To tell you how they allotted the fields, they cast lots, and each one received his portion according to the lot he received.
festive belt-like silver and gold ornament
small lama settlement with a temple above Prahwal, on the western slope of the upper Indrawati valley
deviate from the original course—ङाह थोङगोराङ खुङ क्‍यो़क्‍यो।They saw me and avoided me.
oval (ball-shaped, not flat)—दि च्‍याहमु कोहङा काहजे क्‍योङमो दु।Oh, this is a very longish egg.
support s.b./ s.th—छ्याङगी ज़िक्‍यो मिहला क्‍योर्ती ख्‍येर कोहएँ, लेहमेननी प्रा़हला रिहल्‍गे।When leading along a drunk person, you have to support him, otherwise he will fall down a precipice.
Ghwakang (settlement on the western slope of the Melamchi River valley, opposite of Phaadungdung Chhyu
kingdom—ताहङबु ताहङबु प़ेह क्‍याहल–खापला दे–खाम ट़ूह येहबा लो।Formerly there were six districts in the kingdom of Tibet.

on top of—लुहन्‍दीदी य़ूह केहपा खाला तेह दु लो।The jackal was sitting up there on the wall of the terraced field.—गा़री खावा खाला डोएँमु रिहल्‍ज्‍ये पेहक्‍यो।Running over the snow the car was about to turn over.
eat s.th. more substantial after a eating a snack—हे जारा स़ेह फान्‍ज्‍यो तिङले ङि्यहगी खा–नेम साहक्‍यो।After eating potatoes for a snack we also eat some more substantial food.
bind evil spiritual powers/ forces of nature—लामाॱयागी सिम्‍बु खा–नेन जेकेन।The lamas bind the giants.
stuttering—खो खाक्‍ले दु।He is a stutterer.—खो खाक्‍ले पेहती ताम माउ दु।He stutters.
{give} a house-warming party—खाङदेन साह डोगेन।go to a house-warming party.—खाङदेनला डोगेन।go to a house-warming party.
property (house and fields)—खाङसा स्‍यिहङसा पाङदी छ्य़ेला डोएँ।I will give up house and fields and become a monk.
be quiet—खाजुम तेह, पेहजाॱया!Children, keep quiet!
give last instructions (a dying person)—लाहला स्‍यिज्‍ये पेहकें मिहगी राहङगी ङ्योहवाला खाज्‍येम ज़्‍या़एँ।Some people who are about to die give last instructions to their relatives.
brimful—ब़्रे पादी खाप-च्‍ये ताहप्‍कें।(He) makes the measuring container brimful when he measures out the rice.

{put on} lid—हायाङ(ला) खाज्‍यु क्‍याहप्‍तोङ ले!Please put a lid onto the aluminium pot!—फुम्‍बाला तोह येम्‍बा खाप्‍च्‍यु क्‍याहप्‍कें।For a lid I am going to put a flat stone onto the big earthen pot.
talkative—खो खाप्‍तारच्‍यी दु!He is very talkative!
opposite—खोगी खाप्‍तुर(ला) तेहकेन।sit opposite of him.—दि खाङबा ङ्यी़नी खाप्‍तुरॱटाङ ज़ोसीन दु।These two houses were built just opposite of each other.
board supporting the wall above the door or window frame
fragrant weed of the wormwood family with little yellow flowers
{come upon} a carcass—नाहम नाहम ङि्यहगी रिहला खाराल चोक्‍पा थ़ूएँ।Sometimes we come upon ghastly carcasses in the forest, and we take it as a bad omen.
village on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley, south-west of Kolama,
spin—ङि्यह यिबीगी पाहल खाल्‍गो येह।Our grandmother spins wool.
counting by twenties: twenty, forty, etc
race of men—दि फार–ङ़े खालाकॱया चोक्‍पु यिहन!The people over there are a bad lot!—छि्यमीगी खालाक या़हबु येहबा।The people of TÐhimi are good people.
pleura (body part)—खाल्‍मा लावागी रो़ह यिहम्‍बा।The pleura is the companion of the lungs.
snow {fall}—खावा युलु मुहलु क्‍याहप्‍केन।everything is covered with snow.—ङि्यहमा ङ्यी़ नाहङलानी ओ़ह युहलला ठु च्‍यु थुम्‍बु खावा क्‍याहप्‍सीन।Within two days ten cubits of snow fell in that region.
fumble around—खोगी खाङबा नाहङला खासार खुसुर पेहकु दु।He is fumbling around inside the house.
settlement below the ridge behind (the former) Yangrima School, Sermathang
on the edge (of)—दि खाङबा प्रा़ह खासोलाराङ दु।यो घर भीरको छेउमै रहेछ।θhis house is on the very edɡe of the abyss.
some—लामागी दिला छ्य़ेगी तेम्‍बा च़ूदी लोह खास्‍य़े ज़्‍य़ू दु लो।Establishing buddhism the lama stayed here for some years.
bend a body part—खोगी काङबा खुङ पेहकु दु।He pulls up his legs.
sigh—दाङ तिहरीङ ङाहगी खुम्‍बु माहङबुराङ ताङ कोहएँ!Nowadays I have to sigh a lot!—ङाह खुम्‍बु स्‍योर्सीन।I sighed just like that.
carry on back, head, or shoulders
take responsibility—ओ़ह खाङबा ज़ोज्‍येला ङाहगी ख़े लेहन्‍ग्‍यो।I took the responsibility for building that house.—खोगी ख़े लेहन्‍दी थिन ज़ुङग्‍यो।Having taken a vow he devotes himself to meditation.
kind (what kind? this kind)—दि काहन्‍डुमु खेनगी छोङ यिहम्‍बा?What kind of business is this?
very difficult—लामा छ्य़े लोप्‍च्‍येला ङाहला ज्‍यिबुराङ खेन्‍दा का़हबु ओहङसीन।I found it very difficult to learn the buddhist scriptures.
1show off with a skill or one's cleverness
person skilled in art or handicrafts
by heart—खुङगी लुॱया ख़ेलो लेहन्‍गु दु।Oh, they are singing the songs by heart.
be irritated—दि तामला खो खो़न्‍रो लाहङदी!He was irritated by this matter!
1wheel-like things (e.g. the round stone at the top of a prayer flag pole)

wander around without finding rest (soul in '²pahrto')—तेहकेन मिह-दाङ नोहर, काप्‍च्‍याॱयाला सेम छ्या़नानी ख्‍येराङ खोर्वा ख्‍याम्‍गे यिहम्‍बा।I your heart-mind remains attached to the living people, to your possessions and clothes your soul will wander around without finding rest.
boil (water, milk)—छ्यु ब्‍लोक ब्‍लोक खोल्‍गु दु।Oh, the water is boiling briskly.
be cold (of weather)/ feel cold—दाङ तिहरीङ ख्‍या़उ दु।It's cold these days.—ङाह तिहरीङ ख्‍या़सीन।I am cold today.—ङाहगी काङबा ख्‍या़उ दु।Oh my feet are cold.
not lift a finger (to help with work)—मिहरोनी ख्‍याक ख्‍योक च्‍यी़ॱआङ मेहबे दु!He is not lifting a finger!
grease on liquids or pots—छाज्‍याला ख्‍याक्‍पा छ्या़ दु।Oh, there are globules of butter floating on the salt tea.
solidify (of things which melt)—ज़्‍युएँ जति रेबेराङ ख्‍याङगे।Anything which can be melted solidifies afterwards.
such quarrelsome people (m.)—ख्‍या़बा ङ्याम्‍बु च्‍यिबे काहल्‍ग्‍यो?Why did you go with such quarrelsome people?
wander around aimlessly—तिहरीङ खो ङि्यहमा काहङ ख्‍याम्‍सीन।Today he walked around aimlessly all day.
such quarrelsome people (f.)—फिला मेहन्‍डो, ज्‍यी़गे! – ख्‍या़मा दुबा?I am not going outside, I am afraid! - Do you think there is a ghost?
feel weakness in one's limbs—च्‍यि ताम मेहङ्येम्‍बाज्‍यी थ़े कोहसीन, ज़ुबुराङ ख्‍याला ख्‍योले काहल्‍सीन!These are very disturbing news, I just feel week in my limbs!
inexpertly—खोगी ख्‍यासार ख्‍योसुर प्रुहउ दु।Oh, he is writing like a beginner.
fill up (with liquid)—नुहपकी खावा मिहस्‍युहना छो मिहख्‍यील येह।If the snow in the west does not melt, the lakes will not fill up.
pool (of water, small or biggish)—यार्का रिहला तोह खाला ख्‍यिल्‍ज्‍यु माहङबु च्‍य़ेह सिहन्‍गे।During the rainy season you find many little pools of water on the rocks in the forest.
know—छ्य़े ओहराङगी नोम्‍दोङ ला़ह, ओहराङगी ख्‍येन्‍गेन।You take the book, you know (how to read) it.
receive one's share—खुङ म़ेहमेॱयाला ज़्‍य़ेरो माहख्‍येप दु।Oh, these ladies didn't get their share of fried wheat bread.
{there isn't/ is} a difference—दि ङ्यी़ला च्‍यि ख्‍येपार येहबा?What is the difference between these two?
take along/ with/ to—ओहले खोनी बेतकी प्रेहका ख्‍येर्ती काहल्‍दी मिहन्‍दु लेह।And he took a cane stick with him!
intensifier used in cursing talk—थाला ख्‍येरे सोङ!/ थाला ख्‍येरे डो कोहज्‍ये!May you become all ashes!—पि-चार ख्‍येरे क्‍योहप!May you fall right upside down!
bring—ता़हनी ङि्यहगी पुहला नामा ख्‍योङ कोहएँ।Now we must get a wife for our son.
queer—ए़, ज्‍यिमी ख्‍योसोककी पेहजा फि क्‍युर ज़्‍या़ दु लो।Oh, they said Jimi, this queer fellow just left the child behind.

burp—ता़हनी पेहजाॱया टेहपा काहङबा छ़े दु, गाउ माउ दु।Now the children seem to have had enough, they are burping.
uneven (in size or arrangement)—स्‍यिङ गान्‍द्राङ गोन्‍द्रोङ दु।There are small and big pieces of wood scattered around.—सो गान्‍द्राङ गोन्‍द्रोङ दु।His teeth are out of row.
hidden away—लामा छ्य़ेलादी ताहङबुइ ताम गाप्‍टु ज़्‍या़दी येहबा।The ancient myths are hidden away in the buddhist scriptures.
throw into mouth—दि थोङसी ची़बु माहताङ येह, ङाहगी फोक फोक गाम्‍ग्‍यो!This curry is not defiled, I just threw some pieces into my mouth!
uneven (ground, piles)—तोह गाराङ गोरोङ माहङबु ओहङगे।This is uneven stony ground.—स्‍यिङॱया गाराङ गोरोङ पेहती दु, मा़हची दु।This is a disorderly pile of wood, it's not stacked up properly.
at the time—ओहराङ मुता चाएँ गारीले मुता च़ेदे डोडोपा काहङले सा़च्‍यी ज़्‍या़ कोहकेनॱग्‍यिले।When we have a court case, as we go to the court, we obviously must have a witness with us.
bear a bad reputation—टोहल्‍मानी ओ़ह रोहवा पेहती गा़ल खुर्ती काहल्‍सीन।Because Íolma behaved like that she left, bearing a bad reputation.
piece of wood (for fire)—दि गाल्‍बा च्‍यूदोङ लेह!Please put this piece of wood onto the fire!
mumps {get/ have}—दि पेहजाला गाल्‍बुरी थेन दु।Oh, this child has the mumps.
{make} loop on the rope for tying up cattle, bow of a loop—माहइगी रेहल(गी) गुमी छ्य़ेमु काहल दुबा।The loop on the cord for tying up the buffalo is too small.
move limbs—पेहजा गुल्‍गु दु!Oh, the baby is kicking (in my tommy)!
{give} wedding gifts in kind and money—पा़हमाला जोम्‍ग्‍यो मिहॱयागी गुल्‍दार ल़ूगे।At the wedding ceremony the people give the couple a turban, a 'þkhada', and money.
blunt point—क्‍येल तोहङबो च्‍योदो मेहयोहङगे, गुल्‍दुम्‍बा ओहङगे।The mountain cedar has no pointed top, it is roundish.
impose on s.b—मिहला च्‍यिबे गेङगो तेर तेहच्‍ये?Why should we keep imposing on people?
get caught in s.th—खुर्पु तोहङबोला गेन्‍गु दु।Oh, the load got caught in the tree.
stick to—दि पेहजागी डाम्‍बाला दाक्‍पा गोसीन दु।This child has mud on his cheeks.
1sit around idly—आदानी ख्‍यिमलाराङ गो़गेन, ङ्योहप्‍च्‍येॱया चोङगेन।My elder brother sits around idly at home, and he sells some goods.
do s.th. intently—थोङदेॱआङ माहथोङबा रोहवा पेहती गोक ताङदी डोउ दु।Even though he sees us, he proceeds intently on his way as if he didn't see us.
quite big and roundish (of animate beings)—पेहजा गोक–कोक च्‍यी़ दु।The child is quite chubby.
quite big and roundish (of inanimate things)—लाङगो मोएँ बेले डाल्‍लो गोक–गोक थेन्‍गु दु।When ploughing the field quite big clods of earth are turned up.
big and ball-shaped—गो़ला ठा़म्‍बुर गोगे च्‍य़ेदी!I have a big round bump on my head!
big and bouncy, fluffy (e.g. cumulus clouds)—दि तोहङबोनी त़ेदे त़ेदेराङ गोङोङोङ छार्सीन!This tree grew big and bouncy quickly!
{give} gifts of money—पा़हमाला खादा ङि्यम्‍बु गोदार लूएँ।At weddings you give a gift of money with the ceremonial scarf.

call the dead soul—माहल्‍सा ज़्‍या़गेमु स्‍यिक्‍यो मिहगी सेम लामागी गोप्‍स्‍यु क्‍याहप्‍ती ख्‍योङगे।When the cloth dummy (for a person who just died) is prepared, the lama calls the spirit of the deceased person into it.
leader—नाहङबारकी ल़ेहॱया तिहरीङ गोबाॱयागी था़ च्‍येसीन।The leaders decided today what needs to be done tomorrow.
jump over—खो क्‍याहल-ठिमला गोम्‍सीन दु।He broke the law.
lid made of mountain bamboo strips (used to cover the pot for making curds)
settlement on the western ridge of Yohlmu, after Golbhanjyāng
change—ख्‍ये छेन्‍दा ग्‍युर्सीन दु।Oh, your appearance has changed!—खोगी ओ़ह ताम ग्‍युर्सीन।He changed what he said.
regret s.th—ङाह इस्‍कुल पा़र मा़हतीबा, नाहरेॱगा ग्‍येपा स्‍यार्सीन।I did not go to school, but now I regret it.—टाङगा तोर्ते, ङाहला ग्‍येपा लाहङगु दु।I lost some money, I regret it so much.

say mantras—लामाॱयागी फ्राङा लाहक्‍पाला ज़्‍या़दी ङा़ टाहङगे।Holding the prayer beads in their hands the lamas murmur mantras.
early (has comparative conn.)—ता़ह यिहन्‍ज्‍यु ङाटु याहम्‍बी ङासाङ स्‍यो!From now on come a little bit earlier in the morning!

original_word: ङास्‍याङ स्‍येक्‍याङ ज़ाङबु/ ङाज्‍याङ स्‍याक्‍या ज़ाङबु
pronunciation: ³ŋaɕaŋ ⁴ɕekyaŋ ²zaŋbu/ ³ŋaɕaŋ ³ɕakya ²zaŋbu
english_meaning: founder of '³tsiiri ²kohmba' near Langgyapsa—ङि्यह ङास्‍याङ स्‍येक्‍याङ ज़ाङबुगी क्‍यी़ह यिहन।We are of the line of Ngasyang Syekyang Zangbu.
sleep—मोहगी ङि्य नाहङला ताम माएँ।She talks in her sleep.
act in drowsiness (because of disturbed sleep)
swallow—ख्‍येनी तोप्‍च्‍या मुहरॱआ़ माहमुहर्पा ङि्यहप्‍कु दु।Oh, you are swallowing your food without chewing it first.
(Bhundurghyang?) settlement with a temple, high up on the western slope of the Melamchi River valley
day (a specific day)—ओहले तिङमे ङि्यहमु ‘दोमाङ च्‍यी़ टा़ह कोहसीन’ म़ेदी टा़हसीन।And the next day I said, that we should read a religious book, and I read it.
perspire—ङाहला ङुल्‍ज्‍यु माहङबु थेन्‍दी काप्‍च्‍याराङ पाहङसीन।I perspired so much that my clothes are just soaked—खोला ङुल्‍ज्‍यु थी़बाॱराङ ताहप्‍कु दु।Oh, he is dripping with perspiration.
wail—रोह ख्‍येर्केन बेलाले मिहॱया ङुहप्रा छ्याप्रा पेहती ङुहएँ।When a corpse is taken (for cremation) the people will wail.
real (as opposed to dream)—दि मिलाम मिहम्‍बा, ङुहम्‍बु यिहम्‍बा!This is not a dream, this is reality!
spoil s.th. (concrete things)—फा़गी स्‍यिहङला ङेङग्‍युल पेहती हे रेबेराङ लोङ ज़्‍या़ दु।The wild pigs spoiled the potato field; they turned over everything!
flour made from parched grains—तिहरीङ जारा ङ़ेबी चाम्‍बा साहएँ।Today we are going to have parched flour for our snack.

former—ता़ह तोहङमा – ङेमागी किन्‍ज्‍योनी च्‍यी़राङ येहबाॱग्‍यिले।Now as to the first - the original God, there is only one.—ताहङबु ओहराङ छे ङेमा या़हबु पेहक्‍यो सोङगोराङ मिह मिहली थोप्‍सीन।We received human life, because formerly, in our former life we did good works.
wedding gift of the groom's family to the bride {give}
intimidating—छ्यु माहपुङ माना डाम्‍बा र्‍होप रोप तेर्कें ओहले ङ़ेह!I told you not to spill the water. Just wait, I am going to slap you on the cheek!
scabies—ङोगी स्‍योवा दाल्‍सीन दु।The sores of the scabies have spread quite a bit.
1irritate s.th—स्‍योवाला ङो़ना जेन याएँ।If you irritate the wound it will become even more itchy.
towards—खो ङो़ले सुङ!Go towards him!—ङाह ङो़ले स्‍यो!Come towards me!
generic word for blue and green
1give the spirit of the deceased person instructions
real (primarily used for kinship terms)—आज्‍यी ङोहदोराङ यिहम्‍बा?Is she your real sister?

(W) cherish (e.g. food or possessions)—पेहजा फेमाॱयागी, ‘ङाहलानी प्राहङ ज्‍योर्सीन’ म़ेदी ङोहम्‍गे, ओहले ङोहम्‍ज्‍यार तेन्‍दी साहएँ।ंसानो बच्‍चाहरूले ‘मैले चक्‍लेट पाएँ’ भनेर भन्‍छन्‌, अनि देखाएर खान्‍छन्‌।ɮittle children cherish the sweets they have received, and they eat them displayinɡ them before the others.
adhere to—ख्‍येगी तो ङ्या़सीन?Why are you eating with such bad manners?
white sediments in water—छ्युला ङ्या–ऊ दु।Oh, there are white sediments in the water.
stretch oneself (as in stretch and yawn)
stretch (arm or body) to reach for s.th—ङाहलानी ङ्याप्‍ती ङ्याप्‍ती ताउ दु!Stretching round her body she looked and looked at me!
taste s.th—ङाहगी छ्याङगी प़्रेहज्‍यीॱआङ ङ्याम माहङ्युहङ!I have never even tasted whisky.
with—ङि्यह रो़हॱया ङ्याम्‍बु काहल्‍ग्‍यो।We went with our friends.—ख्‍येनी ङाह ङि्यम्‍बु थितेराङ डो कोहएँ!You must come with me keeping close to me!
with—थ़ूदे! खो ङि्यह ङ्याम्‍बु ओहङग्‍यो लेह!We met him! He came with us!—ओहले आछामॱआङ याम्‍बु याम्‍बु ताहदा क्‍याहप्‍तेराङ येहकें।And Atsham was also following them.
keep begging—पेहजाॱयागी आबा आमाला ङ्याम्‍सेर पेहकें।Children keep begging for things from their parents.
female forest spirit—रिहला च्‍यी़राङ लिहक्‍यो बेलेनी ङ्याल्‍मुॱगा खो पोहला फोक थेन ओहङ दु लो।When he was left behind alone in the forest, suddenly a female forest spirit appeared before him.
slip through—खो मिह नाहङले फार ङ्याहलाक ङ्योहलोक पेहती डोउ दु।Oh, he is just slipping through the crowd.

expressing strong wish—आउगी ङि्यहला काप्‍च्‍या तेरक्‍यो, ओहले आउगी ङि्यहला काप्‍च्‍या तेरॱङ्यी म़ेदी मेहमेगी आउला यिह था़गे।Our uncle gave us clothes, and now grandfather (who is staying with the uncle) is bringing this up again and again.
get to—च्‍यिबे ल़े ओहङबा? ख्‍या लुहङबाला तो साह मिहङ्युहङगे?Why did you come here? Do you not get enough to eat in your country?

dagger—चुबीगी क्‍याहप्‍ती ख्‍याङे से ज़्‍या़ दु!He stabbed him to death with a dagger!

sew—काप्‍च्‍या छुल ती़हदी चेम्‍गे।Sowing clothes one gathers folds.
play games—फेमा येहकेन बेले ङि्यहगी चेमे माहङबु चेक्‍यो।We played a lot when we were small.
seam—चेम्‍बु स्‍यी़गेन।undo a seam.—चेम्‍बु स्‍यी़हगेन।seam comes undone.—चेम्‍बु क्‍याहप्‍केन।make a seam.—चेम्‍बु चेम्‍गेन।sew a seam.

ferment—छ्याङनी ङि्यहमा ट़ेहगी चोर्ती थेन्‍सीन दु।The beer fermented in the warmth of the sun, and it spilled over.
cause to ferment—तिहरीङनी ङाहगी छ्याङ चोर कोहज्‍ये येहबा।Today I have to add the fermenting agent (ÿphap) to the grain mash for the whisky.
cubit with making fist instead of outstretched fingers—च्‍याक्‍टु काहङ।one small cubit.
settlement in the western part of Yohlmu, in the upper Tadi Khola valley, south-west part of Kulu
mountain barley—च्‍याङदा-दाङ जावला काहमा माहङबु ओहङगे।Mountain barley and regular barley heads have lots of bristly fibres.
heavy—खुर्पु च्‍यान्‍दी दु।The load is heavy.
mushroom which looks like the antlers of a deer
fish hook—ङ्याहला च्‍यायु क्‍याहप्‍कें।The fish hook is thrown to the fish.
fall/ throw down forcefully—खो च्‍यार–ताप क्‍याहप्‍सीन।He fell down in a fit.—खोगी मिहला च्‍यार–ताप क्‍याहप्‍सीन।He threw the man down forcefully.
tea—च्‍याह कोल्‍गेन।make tea.—च्‍याह लोहमा।tea leaves on tea bushes.

dissolve s.th—तोहङगी ठिम(गी)ला री़हबाॱया ती़हदी छ्युला च्‍याहङगे।According to the former custom, the bones (of a cremated person) were gathered up and disposed of in the river.—दि मेन छ्युला च्‍याहङदी थुङ कोहएँ।This medicine must be drunk dissolved in water.
sheet of paper with the name of deceased person (this is burnt during the '⁴neebar' ceremony which is also called '⁴nee ⁴taŋ-gen/ ⁴par-ken')
toddle—दि पेहजा ताहप्‍सेॱगा च्‍या़हच्‍या डोज्‍ये पेहकु दु।This child it just now starting to toddle around.
wind around—कारा/ प़ेटी च्‍यिङगेन।put on cummerbund/ belt.
crown of the head—पेहजाॱयाला तुहङगेमु गो़गी च्‍यिर्चुपला क्‍याहप्‍च्‍ये मेहयोहङगे।When you are beating the children, you mustn't hit them on the crown of the head.

why—तेम्रेल च्‍यिले ता कोहएँ?Why does one need to look up the auspicious time?

bird—च्‍यिहबी छाङला कोहङदी तेहकु दु।The bird is hatching eggs.

become too small—खार्का च्‍युङसीन/ छ्युङसीन।The grazing grounds have become too small.
leafy buds (before they unfold)—केरा तोहङबोगी च्‍युली पा़ साहज्‍ये ओहङगे।The leaf buds of the banana bush may be used to make curry.
push onto—स्‍यिङ च्‍य़ूगेन।add wood (to the fire).
1allow to—ङि्यह खाङबाला चुङबेगी डु च्‍यिॱआङ लिह मी़हच्‍युए।In our house the rats don't spare any grain.
be formed—ङाह लाहक्‍पाला टाङबुर थेन्‍ज्‍ये म़ेदी च्‍यार्का च्‍य़ेदी!A hard lump has formed on my hand; it's going to be a boil!—दि थाक्‍पाला गोर्तो च्‍य़े दु।This rope has become knotted.
tongue—ङाहगी च्‍येले ठिल्‍गु दु।I can't pronounce it clearly.

shake back and forth—ब़्रे च्‍योक्‍कोल पेहती ज्‍यागी तेन्‍गे।Shaking the raw rice grains back and forth I pick out the broken grains.

elder brother—ङाहला च्‍योहज्‍यो ख़ेबागी ताङ चाहङग्‍यो यिहम्‍बा, याहम्‍बुले याहर।Elder brother, the thangka painter has sent me from Kathmandu up here.

leafy buds (before they unfold)—केरा तोहङबोगी च्‍युली पा़ साहज्‍ये ओहङगे।The leaf buds of the banana bush may be used to make curry.
push onto—स्‍यिङ च्‍य़ूगेन।add wood (to the fire).
1allow to—ङि्यह खाङबाला चुङबेगी डु च्‍यिॱआङ लिह मी़हच्‍युए।In our house the rats don't spare any grain.
be formed—ङाह लाहक्‍पाला टाङबुर थेन्‍ज्‍ये म़ेदी च्‍यार्का च्‍य़ेदी!A hard lump has formed on my hand; it's going to be a boil!—दि थाक्‍पाला गोर्तो च्‍य़े दु।This rope has become knotted.
tongue—ङाहगी च्‍येले ठिल्‍गु दु।I can't pronounce it clearly.
exchange (barter)—हे आम्‍बा ङ्याम्‍बु च्‍य़ेहगेन।Potatoes are exchanged for guavas.
{leave} footprint—काङबा साला क्‍याहप्‍ती च्‍येहलाप म़ेहछ्या दु!If I put my foot on the ground it leaves no traces!
shake back and forth—ब़्रे च्‍योक्‍कोल पेहती ज्‍यागी तेन्‍गे।Shaking the raw rice grains back and forth I pick out the broken grains.

1work through with fingers—तो च्‍योप्‍केन।break up/ mix rice with fingers.—काप्‍च्‍या च्‍योप्‍केन।work soap and water through clothes with fingers.—चाम्‍बा च्‍योप्‍केन।work through flour with fingers.

elder brother—ङाहला च्‍योहज्‍यो ख़ेबागी ताङ चाहङग्‍यो यिहम्‍बा, याहम्‍बुले याहर।Elder brother, the thangka painter has sent me from Kathmandu up here.
salt—यार्का छा ङुहएँ।During rainy season salt becomes wet.
become disabled—केहपु केहमुॱयाला छा़ तुहएँमु रो़ह पेहना फायीन ओहङगे।You gain merit if you help old people who can't help themselves anymore.
be complete—ओहजेलानी मोहराङ ज़ुबु चोक्‍पा येहकेन दावा छाङसीन।At that time the term of her pregnancy was coming to an end.
umbrella—ताहन्‍दा नाम क्‍याहप्‍च्‍ये ठि मिहन्‍दु, छा़ता मेहख्‍येरॱना़ डी़रो।It doesn't look like rain now, it's probably alright to go without an umbrella.
hut for retreat or seclusion (for lamas, monks or nuns)
time (counting times)—ओ़ह छ्य़ेदी खोगी ङि्यहमा टाहङमुराङ छार माहङबु टा़हदी तेहसीन।He kept reading that book every day many times.

finish an action—ओहले कोहम्‍बा नाहङगी छ्या़लेनॱया़ छार, फिगी छ्या़लेनॱया़ छार, ख़ेबाॱला़ कुयीन फुल छार्सीन।And then both, the work inside and outside the temple was finished, and they finished with giving the reward to the artists.
offspring (used only for humans)—खुङ पा़हमा पेहती लोह ङा काहल्‍देॱआङ (खुङला) छार्क्‍यीन माहयोहङ।Even though they are married already for five years, they have no offspring yet.
word—ओ़ह छ्य़ेदी जाम्‍बुलीङगी मिहॱयाला छिका च्‍यिॱआङ माहच्‍येपा पेहती स्‍येतोङ!Tell the contents of this book to the people of this world without leaving out any single word!
end up (in a location; negative conn.)—ज्‍येलला छि्यकेन।end up in prison.—ङ्याल्‍वाला छि्यकेन।end up in hell.
village about 20 minutes walk north of Sermathang (Chhimighyang)
festivities and offerings performed for religious merit
a little way over here—छ्युनी खो छुच्‍ची लेप्‍कें फोक जा़एँ लो, छुच्‍ची लेप्‍कें फोक जा़उ दु लो।As to the water, as he moved over this side little by little it kept leaking out.
coming this way—छुन्‍डोनी रो़ह क्‍याहक्‍चेराङ ज्‍योर्सीन।Coming back I found a lot of friends.
one moment—छुप च्‍यी़ क़ूहदोङ, ङाहॱआ़ डोज्‍ये!Just wait a moment, I want to go with you.
1shape (of a building, piece of clothing, etc.)
begin to—सोमबार यिहन्‍ज्‍यु छ्य़े प्रुहज्‍ये छ़ूगेन।I am intending to start writing the book on Monday.—ओहले ङाहगी स्‍यिह–ठो थाङगु प्रुहगेन छ़ूसीन।And then I started to paint "Ðih-Ìho" thangkas.
up to—छ्यु काङबा–छी़ छ़े लेप्‍कु दुबा।The water is coming up to the knees.—ओ़ह फार–ङ़े छ़े डोज्‍ये म़ेदी!I want to go up to over there.
measure s.th./ s.b—काप्‍च्‍या चेम्‍ज्‍येला चेम्‍गेन मिहगी ओहराङगी जुबुला छेकें।To sow clothes the tailor take the measures from our body.
fate caused by the deeds of one's former life—ताहन्‍दा तुहक्‍पु ङ्युहङना छे-ङेनगी ल़ेह यिहम्‍बा।If you endure hardship now, that is because of your fate.
be spread over tautly (e.g. skin over a drum )
spread over tautly—ङाहगी को़बा छेङग्‍यो।I fixed the skin tautly.
be lightning  (with extra loud peals of thunder)
let loose a bird or chicken, promising not to kill it, or make some other free will gift

1special amulet—ओहले क्‍याहमीगी यिहस्‍यी नुहर्पुगी सिपाइॱयागी ज़ुबुला छेन्‍सुङ येहबा म़ेदी क्‍यी़ह ज्‍योर्सीन।Then the Chinese got to know that the soldiers of the Dalai Lama were wearing special amulets on their bodies.
be hot (of weather)—तिहरीङ छेपा छाउ दु।Oh, it's hot today.
be/ feel hot (of people)—तिहरीङ ङाहला छेपा छाउ दु।Oh, I am so hot today.

{there is a} full moon—छ़ेबा च्‍यी़/ ङ्यी़one/ two days after new moon, etc.—छ़ेबा ङि्यहस्‍यु।20 days after new moon.—छ़ेबा ङि्यहस्‍यु–कुह।29 days after new moon.—स्‍यिक्‍यो मेहमेॱयागी च्‍य़ेङा नाम्‍गाङला ङि्यहला काहकाला तुहक्‍पु तेर्के।The spirits of the ancestors bother us a lot at the times of full moon and new moon.
leafy, green vegetables—लाहबु छ़ेमा।radish leaves.—राइ छ़ेमा।Nepali spinach.—प्राहउ छ़ेमा।buckwheat greens.

day of lunar calendar—तिहरीङ छ़ेराङ काहजे यिहम्‍बा?What day of the lunar calendar is it today?—तिहरीङ छ़ेबा ङि्यहस्‍यु कुह नाहले!It's the twenty-ninth!—तिहरीङ छ़ेराङ या़हबु दुबा, ओहले छ्योर्तेन याहर छ्य़ेमी फुल डोज्‍ये म़ेदी!Today is an auspicious day, so I want to go to the stupa to offer butter lamps.
thorn—ङाहला छेर्माङ(गी)स़ूहसीन।I got a thorn (in my finger).
have a dream—मेहमेगी छेलाम ज़ी़ नाङसीन।Grandfather had a dream.
herd cattle (take for grazing)—ओहले आदादीनी राह ल़ूह छो डोएँ दु लो।And the elder brother would take the goats and sheep for grazing.
1rice statues to be offered to the gods
pond and open space on the ridge above Sermathang with army barracks
group—योहल्‍मो मिहॱयादी राहङ राहङगी छोक्‍पा येहबा।Among the Yohlmo people each one is part of his own group.

fontanel—पेहजाॱया छि्यम्‍बु डोवाराङ छो़मा काहङगे।As the children become bigger the fontanel closes up.—पेहजाॱयाला तुहङगेमु छो़मा क्‍यिलला क्‍याहप्‍च्‍ये मेहयोहङगे।When you are beating the children, you mustn't hit them on the fontanel.
1look for an opportunity to perform an action
hand—योहल्‍मु लुहङबागी मेहन्‍दो नामे नाजेॱया थो़दी क्‍याहल्‍बु क्‍याहल्‍मुगी छ्या़ला फुल्‍सीन।The people of Helambu brought all sorts of flowers and presented them to the king and queen.
crease s.th. thoroughly by treading on it—च्‍यिबे काप्‍च्‍या खाला छ्या़ क्‍याहप तेहकें?Why are you trampling around on the bed-clothes?
bow down to honour gods or venerated persons
god who has a human skin wrapped around him and a snake in his mouth—छ्या़दोर मिहगी को़बागी टिल्‍ग्‍यो ओहङगे।"tÐhaador" is wrapped with human skin.
dedication ceremony performed to protect from evil influences
small settlement between ʈasyi-Thang and Kahng-Yuhl
arrange neatly—ओहले टोहल्‍माइ चा ङाहइ छ्याप पेह तेरक्‍यो।And I arranged Dolma's (load of) cattle fodder neatly.
it's an even number—छ्याप छ्याप टाहङदोङ, जोल्‍दी कार माहटाहङ याहङ!Count by twos, without making any mistakes!
sprinkle ritually pure water onto the house altar, etc
defile ritually clean things with unclean ones—ची़बु छ्याप छ्योप पेहकेन।put unclean and clean things together.—तोप्‍च्‍या छ्याप छ्योप साहगेन।eat from other people's plates.
the coming into existence (of inanimate things)
possess (ancestral or other spirits possessing people)
rain water falls down from a roof—छ्यार ताहप्‍केन ग्‍या़ला तोह तिङ कोहएँ।Where the rain water from the roof falls down you must lay stone slabs.
longlasting heavy rainfall—तिहरीङ छ्यार्पा चोक्‍पा क्‍याहप्‍सीन!Today it keeps raining heavily!
work—याहम्‍बुला च्‍यि छ्या़लेनगी फेप नाङक्‍यो लेह?For what purpose did you come to Kathmandu?
watery pus {come out/ gather}—मेह छी़क्‍यो ग्‍या़ला छ्यी़सेर थेन्‍गेन।In burns watery pus appears.

tree twigs with leaves for animal fodder—नुहमुगी छ्य़े तुप्‍कु दु।Oh, little sister is cutting animal fodder!

barley—जाउला काहमा माहङबु ओहङगे।The ears of barley have many hairs.
one peak after another—लाह जाङा जुङे दु।There is a row of peaks.—मुक्‍पा जाङा जुङे थेन दु।There are cumulus clouds.
dress up—ओहले माएँ, ज़ाप्‍केन तोहरोङ, कोहम्‍बा मिहॱयानी उइ!Indeed, they do dress up, the people of Langgyapsa, don't they!
world—ख्‍या जाम्‍बुलीङगी मिहॱया ङाह ल़ेहमु पेहती किन्‍ज्‍योला सोल्‍वाङ या़हबु तोप!You people of the world pray fervently to God as I do.
snack—जारा थुक्‍पा (कोल्‍दी)साहगेन।have soup for a snack.
get intoxicated—खो छ्याङगी ज़िउ दु।Oh, he is drunk!
quarrel—जिङग्‍यो ग्‍या़ला क्‍याहल–ठिम पेहकेन मिहगी ठिम पेहकेँ।When there are quarrels the government authorities judge the case.
do—लामाॱयागी ताज्‍ये माएँगी सेम्‍बा जेकु दुबा।The lamas said they would like to have a look at it.
big bamboo basket for storing grain—ज़ेपला मा़हथाबा डु ल़ूदी ज़्‍या़गे।The 'üzep' is used to store non-ground grain.
come off in small pieces—सेमु जेल्‍गेन।fingernails breaking off.—स्‍या जेल्‍गेन।callous skin coming off in small pieces.
small splinter—ङाह लाहक्‍पाला स्‍यिङ जेल्‍माङगी स़ूहदी।I got a wood splinter in my hand!
{make} an idol/ statue—ख़ेबागी जोगु ज़्‍याङगे।The artists make the idols.
scoopful (using both hands to scoop up s.th.)—हे लाहक्‍पा ङ्यी़गी जोप काहङ तुहएँ।With both hands I will scoop up some potatoes.
post to which animals are tied—रेहलला गुमी ज़ोएँ, ओहले जो़बुरला केन्‍गे।I am going to make a loop which can be pulled together on the cattle tying rope, and then I slip it over the post.
gather—ङ्युङनेला छ़ेबा तिहनला मिह जोम्‍गेन।At the fasting festival the people gather on the seventh day of the lunar month.
kind of cow (cross-breed of cow and yak)
jab with elbow—खोगी ङाहला जोक्‍स्‍यु क्‍याहप्‍सीन।He jabbed me with his elbow.
18 ānās or 75 paisā—आना च्‍याप्‍क्‍येला ज्‍याउ ट़ूह माएँ।Eighteen aìnaìs are called six 'þdÑaw'.
build—ओ़ह यी़–साम्‍ये कोहम्‍बा ज़्‍याङ मा़हखु लो।And they could not build the Yii-Samye temple.
uneven in length, but neatly arranged (of things hanging)—काप्‍च्‍या ज्‍याना ज्‍यिने च्‍याङ ज़्‍या़ दु, मिहनी सुॱआ़ मिहन्‍दु!The clothes are hanging there neatly arranged, but nobody is around!
sloppy (clothes)—च्‍यि ज़ोप्‍ता म़ेहबागी काप्‍च्‍या ज्‍यान्‍दाङ ज्‍योन्‍दोङ केहन्‍ग्‍यो यिहम्‍बा, दि पेहजा!This child is wearing such uncomely sloppy clothes!
gold or silver ornament hanging from center of '²tɕuhru' necklace
purifying rituals performed by shaman or lama—दि पेहजाला ज्‍याप-ठी़ पेह कोहज्‍ये दु, ओहले टा़हगे।We have to perform a purifying ritual for this child, and then he will get well.
scoop off (particles swimming on top of a liquid)—आमागी माहरकी ङ्योहक्‍तोङ ज़्‍याम्‍ग्‍यो।Mother scooped the dirt off the butter (when boiling it).
god who carries a kind of a wooden sword called '²rahlɖi' in one hand
make s.th. even (e.g. with a plane)
1pay homage—ओहले ‘क्‍याहल्‍बु दुबा’ म़ेदी क्‍याहल्‍बुला ज्‍याल्‍सीन।When they said that it was the king, I payed homage to him.
gift offered when paying homage to a person (e.g. a '¹rihmbur-³tɕhee')
→atanthalā (settlement high on the western slope of the upper Indrawati valley, just below the ridge, about 1 hour before Sermathang
lady's black coat made of cotton cloth
suck—पेहजा ल़ेनुॱयागी ओहमा ज्‍यिप्‍केन।Babies suck mother's milk.
be afraid—ङाह मेहन्‍डो, ङाहनी ज्‍यी़उ दु!I am not going, I am afraid!—क्‍यिबुगी च्‍येनला ज्‍यी़एँ।Dogs are afraid of tigers.

undertake s.th. without being completely successful—ओ़ह पेहजागी मेह फुएँज्‍यी पेहकु दु।That child is trying hard to light the fire.—मोहगी लु लेहन्‍गेनजी पेहकु दु।She is trying to sing a song.—तोहमगीनी “आया, आया!” माएँज्‍यी पेहकें लो।"Ouch, ouch" the bear would shout out loudly each time.—ङाहनी “मेहन्‍डो, डोॱराङ मेहन्‍डो” माएँज्‍यी पेहक्‍यो!I made a big show, saying, "I am not going, I am not going!"
melt s.th. (e.g. butter)—ङि्यहमा छेपागी ताहर्ताङ ज़्‍यु-ज़्‍युबा काहङला कुहङसेला स्‍युह सिहन्‍सीन दु लो।As the sun was melting the ice the whole day, it had melted away in the evening.
twist threads together (with a spindle-like device)—यिबीगी पाहल ज़्‍यु नाङसीन।Grandmother twisted the threads together.
bow (for shooting)—दि तुहङमा ज़्‍युर्कुजेबाराङ कोहर्मु दु।Oh, this beam is curved like a bow for shooting.

arrive at the moment of death—ओ़ह नेपागी ज्‍युङा उहप ती़हदीॱआङ ख्‍येर्ती!The spirit of sickness took him in one moment!—तिहरीङ टेहपा तो़रा़ ख्‍येर्ती ङाह ज्‍युङाराङ तुहज्‍ये पेहसीन!Today I almost died of hunger!
youthful—दि पी़हमीनी काहन्‍डुमा ज़्‍योम्‍बो दु!What a youthful young lady!
drain off—नेक्‍यीॱया छ्यु ज़्‍यर्च्‍यु म़ेदी ओ़हला ज़्‍या़सीन।I put the pots there to drain.
strip off—ख्‍येगी दि याहल्‍गागी लाहप्‍ती ज़्‍युर्तोङ!Strip off the leaves of this branch!
crouch down—खुङ(गी) रिहल्‍ग्‍यो तोहङबोगी चो़ फार ज्‍युल्‍दी काहल्‍ग्‍यो।They crouched down to pass under the fallen tree.
spoil (of cooked foods)—हे नाहङबार साम्‍मा ज़्‍या़ना ज्‍य़ूगेन।I we leave the potatoes until tomorrow they will be spoiled.
have the know-how—दि ल़ेह खोगी ज्‍येन्‍गे।This is the type of work he can do.—काहन्‍डुमुज्‍यी माज्‍ये ज्‍येन्‍गे!He really knows how to talk!
quite big (for roundish things)—ज्‍या़न्‍दुमॱआ़ ज्‍येन ल़ेहमुज्‍यी ता़ तेर दु रो़ह, मोहलानी!Oh look friend, what a biggish ornament they have put around her neck!
expert singer and dancer—मा़हङगुगी ज़्‍येबा खो यिहम्‍बा।He is the expert singer and dancer of Maahngu.—ज़्‍य़ेबा ज़्‍य़ेमा, देला खाम्‍साङ जे स्‍यो!Singers and dancers, come and have fun here!

youthful—ख्‍या आहन्‍डुमु मिनाम म़ेहबा माहबे, केहम्‍बा केहम्‍बागी टाहलला ज़्‍य़ू, ज़्‍येम्‍बा ज़्‍येम्‍बागी टाहलला तेहतोङ!Don't do things without using proper etiquette! You elder ones, please sit on the places assigned to the elders; you younger ones, sit on the places assigned to the younger ones!
bread of whole wheat flour, deep-fried in oil or butter
decrease in volume—दाङ लाहक्‍पा दि साम्‍मा टाङदी येहकें, तिहरीङ ज्‍योम्‍सीन।Yesterday the hand was swollen this much, but today the swelling has gone back.
find—ओहलेनी ङि्यहमा च्‍य़ूज्‍यी डोएँ ङि्यहमुदी छोल–छोल्‍बा काहङला युहलगी ङि्यहगी मेहमेला ज्‍योर्सीन।And after having searched for him a long time, we finally found the old man on the eleventh day.
bow (for shooting)—दि तुहङमा ज़्‍युर्कुजेबाराङ कोहर्मु दु।Oh, this beam is curved like a bow for shooting.

original_word: टाङ टाङ टोङ टोङ पेह्‌ती ता-
pronunciation: ³ʈaŋ ³ʈaŋ ³ʈoŋ ³ʈoŋ ²peh-ti ⁴ta-

english_meaning: pull each other's hair—पेहजाॱया टादु कुकु चेउ दु।Oh, the children are pulling each other's hair!
winnow—डु ना थाम्‍ज्‍यीराङ लोमागी टाप्‍कें।All kind of grains are winnowed with the bamboo tray.
small settlement on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley on the upper way to Langgyapsa after Phaa-Dungdung Chhyu
enemy—आदा नो़ह टाहजेबाराङ जिङदेॱआङ तेहकु दु।Even though they are brothers they keep fighting with each other like enemies.
cut cloth (for sewing clothes)—काप्‍च्‍या टाहएँ ग्‍या़ला कार्ना मार्नाॱया थेन्‍गे।When cutting up cloth for sowing something we get scraps of cloth.
become cold—दाङ ङाहइ काङबा ज्‍यिबुराङ टाहङसीन।Yesterday I had terribly cold feet!
spread (word, news, liquids)—ताम टाहम्‍सीन दु।The news spread.—छोगी छ्यु रेबेराङ टाहम्‍सीन दु।The water of the pond is spilling onto the shore all around.
small lama settlement below Maahngu, in the western area of Yohlmu
pull apart—छी़ टाहल कोहएँ।The joints have to be pulled apart. (of a calf which died in the womb of its mother.)—ख्‍येगी ज्‍यिङबा ङाहइ टाहल्‍लु स्‍य़ेदी येहकें नाह!I am going to pull your head off!

sweet-smelling herb growing at high altitudes—लामा-दाङ पोहम्‍बोगी टाहलीङ पुहम्‍बागी छ्युला ल़ूगे, छ्यु चाङमा ज़ोज्‍येला।The lamas and shamans put the sweet-smelling herb into the water of the ceremonial urn, in oder to ritually purify the water.

{throw away} small statues made of millet flourनेहपा च्‍य़ेगेन बेले पोहम्‍बोगी नाहम नाहम टाहसोर क्‍याहप्‍केँ।While curing a sick person the shaman sometimes performs the ritual of throwing away small statues made of millet flour.

the share which the '³ʈiga ⁴lama' gets—कोहम्‍बाला टिगा लामा ओहङगे, ओहले टिगा लामा का़हलेॱआङ डो मी़हखुसीमाना लेहमेन मिहला ताङगे, ओहले खोगी टिगा लामाला टिगा तेर कोहएँ।Each temple has its own head lama, and if for any reason the head lama cannot go (when people call him), he sends somebody else, and this person has to give a share of the reward to the head lama.
pull (out/ along)—नोहनोगी छ्य़ेरो टिहती ख्‍योङसीन।The little boy brought the brushwood pulling it along.
close to each other (things arranged)—पेलाॱया टिहक टिहक टिहक पेहती टी़हदी दु।The pots and pans are arranged close together.

{be affected} by spirit of defilement acting at birth or death—पेहजा क्‍येसीमाॱनाङ, मिह स्‍यिसीमाॱनाङ, ओ़ह खाङबाला टिहप ओहङगे।The birth of a child, and the death of a person defiles a house.—टिहप नाहङला स्‍य़ूहज्‍ये मेहयोहङगे मासीमाना, सिउ येहकेन मिह टिहप नाहङला स्‍य़ूहज्‍ये मेहयोहङगे।As to not entering a place defiled by a 'üÌihp', a person wearing a 'rudraksha' seed amulet must not enter such a place.
twist (thread with hands)—नो़हगी थाक्‍पा टिहम्‍सीन।Younger brother twisted the rope strands together.
make up (repay) for the care your parents have given to you by caring for them adequately when they get old
pull together to close—खोगी छा़ता टुप्‍कु दु।He is closing his umbrella.—खुमा खा टुप्‍तोङ!Close the purse (by pulling the drawstrings).
change into another form of existence—दि मिहगी ती़ह टुल्‍सीन।This person changed into the form of a demon.
real (relative)—टुहङ-आस्‍याङ।real uncle (i.e. mother's real brother).—टुहङ-मेहमे।real grandfather.
Dhukpu (cattle shed settlement north of Yangri Kahng)

fix the warp ends of the loom—था़ प्रेहन्‍दी ऩे तुह कोहएँ, ओहले टुहबा क्‍याहप्‍ती था़ कोहएँ।Having put the warp onto the loom you must fix the threads of the loom so they alternate, and then fix the warp ends, and then you can start weaving.
apply s.th—पेहजाॱया दाक्‍पा टुहल्‍दी चेमु चेउ दु।Oh, the children are smearing each other with mud as they play.
stir s.th—छोगी सुहरॱयाला खुङगी प्रेहकागी ट़ू ताएँ।They are stirring up the water at the edges of the pond to see what's there.
get caught in s.th—ङाह लाहक्‍पा कोहगी टेम्‍सीन।My hand got caught in the door.—आच्‍या, लाहक्‍पा टेम्‍दी!Sorry, your hand (finger) got pinched!—दि तामगी खोला टेम्‍सीन।This utterance caught him.
lean against s.th./ s.b—ङाहगी आनीला टेहती तेहक्‍यो।I was sitting leaning against aunty.
long for s.b—खाङबा ताहक्‍पुगी छामुगी आमा टेहन्‍गु दु।The granddaughter of the landlord is longing to be with her mother.
recall s.th—यिबीगी ट़ेहन्‍साल जेकु दु।Oh, grandmother remembers it.

probable future—मिहरोॱआङ मानपारती़दी जोम फान्‍ज्‍योला या़हबुराङ ओहङडो ले च्‍यि!If he also likes her it will probably be alright for them to get together!—तोप्‍च्‍ये ल़ूज्‍ये पा़र पेहतोङ, तोप्‍च्‍ये तेर कोहटो मिहन?Think about giving food, we will probably have to give them something to eat, don't we?—खुङगी सेहन साहरो।They will probably eat corn mash.
make a high and soft beating noise—ङा़ टोङ–टोङ माउ दु।The drum is making a high and softly beating noise.

original_word: टो़टे पेह्‌जा/ ताह्‌चे पेह्‌जा/ टो़टे ताह्‌चे पेह्‌जा

english_meaning: {put on} earrings (ear ornaments set with stones)—टोपासला ठा ल़ूगे।This kind of earring is set with precious stones.
1take out of joint—माहइ स्‍याम्‍बा पेहकेन बेले ङाहइ माहइगी काङबा टोल्‍ग्‍यो।When taking off the skin of the buffalo I broke apart the joints of its legs.
feather—च्‍याहमु टोह।hen's feather.—स्‍योक्‍पागी टोह।feather of wing.—ङामा ङो़गी टोह।feather of tail.
very small settlement on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley, south-west below Syuhnggan-Che
1household—टोहङबा काहजे दुबा?How many households are there?
ant—टो़हमाङॱया ओमले याहर सिहक सिहक पेहती थेन्‍गु दु।The ants are coming out of the hole in swarms.

lay people drink the concluding drink of whisky (after the lama(s)' period of seclusion)—छाम टोहल्‍गे बेले युहल्‍बाॱया टोहल्‍ज्‍याङ थुङ डोएँ।For the conclusion of the lama(s)' period of seclusion the villagers go and have a drink of whisky.

{make} a loop which can be undone by pulling—थाक्‍पा स्‍युहज्‍ये तेहमु ओहङज्‍येला टोहल्‍दी क्‍याहप्‍तोङ!Make a loop which can be undone by pulling so that the cords will easily come off.—लेकामगी थाक्‍पा टोहल्‍दी ताहम्‍ना स्‍युहज्‍ये तेहमु ओहङगे।If you tie the shoe laces in a bow, you can undo them easily.
make a high and soft beating noise—ङा़ टोङ–टोङ माउ दु।The drum is making a high and softly beating noise.

english_meaning: {put on} earrings (ear ornaments set with stones)—टोपासला ठा ल़ूगे।This kind of earring is set with precious stones.
1take out of joint—माहइ स्‍याम्‍बा पेहकेन बेले ङाहइ माहइगी काङबा टोल्‍ग्‍यो।When taking off the skin of the buffalo I broke apart the joints of its legs.
1take out of joint—माहइ स्‍याम्‍बा पेहकेन बेले ङाहइ माहइगी काङबा टोल्‍ग्‍यो।When taking off the skin of the buffalo I broke apart the joints of its legs.
feather—च्‍याहमु टोह।hen's feather.—स्‍योक्‍पागी टोह।feather of wing.—ङामा ङो़गी टोह।feather of tail.
very small settlement on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley, south-west below Syuhnggan-Che
1household—टोहङबा काहजे दुबा?How many households are there?
1household—टोहङबा काहजे दुबा?How many households are there?
1household—टोहङबा काहजे दुबा?How many households are there?
1household—टोहङबा काहजे दुबा?How many households are there?
ant—टो़हमाङॱया ओमले याहर सिहक सिहक पेहती थेन्‍गु दु।The ants are coming out of the hole in swarms.

lay people drink the concluding drink of whisky (after the lama(s)' period of seclusion)—छाम टोहल्‍गे बेले युहल्‍बाॱया टोहल्‍ज्‍याङ थुङ डोएँ।For the conclusion of the lama(s)' period of seclusion the villagers go and have a drink of whisky.

{make} a loop which can be undone by pulling—थाक्‍पा स्‍युहज्‍ये तेहमु ओहङज्‍येला टोहल्‍दी क्‍याहप्‍तोङ!Make a loop which can be undone by pulling so that the cords will easily come off.—लेकामगी थाक्‍पा टोहल्‍दी ताहम्‍ना स्‍युहज्‍ये तेहमु ओहङगे।If you tie the shoe laces in a bow, you can undo them easily.
inlaid precious stone—सितुपला ठा ल़ूएँ।Finger rings are inlaid with precious stones.
hard—दि तोह ठाक्‍पु खालदी दु।This is a hard kind of stone.
be jealous—मोहगी ङाहला ठादोक पेहकु दु।Oh, she is so jealous of me.
{get} a blue bump—गो़ला ठा़म्‍बुर बोगे च्‍य़ेदी!I have a big blue bump on my head!
{make/ become a} bloody mess—ङाहला ङि्य माहङबु छोल्‍ना ठास्‍याल मास्‍याल ज़़े तेर्कें!If you keep aggravating me, I will cut you to pieces!
cubit (18 inches)—ठु काहङ।one cubit.—ठु तोह।two cubits.—ठु सुम।three cubits (etc.).
wash (for clothes, etc.)—नुहमुगी काप्‍च्‍या ठुसीन।The girl washed the clothes.
wash (for one's own body parts)—नुहमु लाहक्‍पा ठुसीन।The girl washed her hands.
make oneself invisible (said of spirit beings)—खो ङ्याम्‍बु ती़ह ठुल्‍दी ओहङ दुबा!A demon followed him.
conflict—ङाहगी पेहकेन ल़ेह लेहमेन मिहगी पेहसीमाना याहम्‍बीज्‍यी पाहर्क्‍यीला ठ़ूगेन।There will be some conflict if somebody else does what I want to do.
sulk angrily—खो ताम फेमाज्‍यीॱला़ ठ़ेउ दु।He is sulking angrily even about little things.
wooden pot used for storing milk and curds
sour (of beer)—छ्याङ ठोक काहल दु।The beer has become too sour.
one moment—ख्‍या देला तेह आ़ह, ङाह ठोप्‍चे काहल्‍दी ल़े ओहङगे।Please stay here, I will be right back.
honoured guest—कार्मागी टाङगा कोहर्मु क्‍याह च्‍यी़गी गोदार ल़ूउ दु, ए ठोम्‍बा–नामॱया!Honoured guests, Karma is putting in one hundred rupees for the wedding gift.
sulk angrily—खो ताम फेमाज्‍यीॱला़ ठ़ेउ दु।He is sulking angrily even about little things.
wooden pot used for storing milk and curds
sour (of beer)—छ्याङ ठोक काहल दु।The beer has become too sour.
one moment—ख्‍या देला तेह आ़ह, ङाह ठोप्‍चे काहल्‍दी ल़े ओहङगे।Please stay here, I will be right back.
honoured guest—कार्मागी टाङगा कोहर्मु क्‍याह च्‍यी़गी गोदार ल़ूउ दु, ए ठोम्‍बा–नामॱया!Honoured guests, Karma is putting in one hundred rupees for the wedding gift.
intimidate—पेहजाॱयाला दार च्‍यिराङ तुहङज्‍ये मेहयोहङगे, नाहम–नाहम डा़न्‍गो तेन कोहएँ।You must not beat the children all the time, sometimes you just need to give them a warning.
small settlement on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley,  below Lhaasisa (which is below ʈohngding, which is below Syuhnggan-Che)
well-matched—स्‍यिङज्‍या पेला डिक्‍पु ल़ू दु।The have put in (i.e. built in) matching wooden furniture.
1money—ओहले ङि्यहनी डिबुॱआङ ज़ो मा़हखु।And we couldn't make any money
interest in form of grains—ङाहगी टाङगा तोङरा सुम डुजोकला ताङसीन।I lent out three thousand rupees to get interest in form of grains.

spirit of sickness which may arrive with a traveler—मिह ङि्यम्‍बु ल्‍हान्‍डीॱआङ ब्र्याङदी ओहङसीमाना, “डुल ओहङसीन, ओहले थाङबु माहयोहङ” माएँ।If a traveler has arrived followed by a spirit of sickness, we say, "A ghost has arrived, that's why I am unwell."

put to use—ङि्यहगी कोहक्‍पा डुल/ युहलॱआङ मेहबे येह।We don't use garlic.—छ्याङ मिहथुङगेन मिहला छ्याङगी डुल लेप्‍सीमाना गो़ सुह क्‍याहप्‍कें, ओ़ह सोङबे ङाह छ्याङगी डुलराङ मेहबे येह।If a total abstainer consumes whisky he gets a headache, so I don't touch it.
coin—डिबु ज़्‍यिगी डेकला आना माएँ।A four-paisaì coin is called 'þana'.
slip—ङाह खावाला डेता क्‍याहप्‍ती बासकी सुहरला रिहल ओहङसीन।Slipping in the snow I fell down beside the bus.
waterpipe (in a mill)—छ्युदागी डेन।the waterpipe of the mill.
deliver (out of hell)—च्‍य़ेन-रेहजीगी स्‍यिम्‍बुला याहर साङग्‍ये-युहलला डेन नाङगेन।TÐeen-Rehzi delivers the spirit of the deceased person (out of hell and sends him) up to heaven.
intensifier for '³kaa-, ¹teh-k'—येहपाराङ ओ़ह ल्‍हा डेप का़एँ साम्‍मा ल्‍हा कोहकेन।And again (we) need a god which is able to stop the other god swiftly.
slouch (sit like a sack of potatoes)
go—ख्‍या म़ेहमे का़हला डोज्‍ये लेह?Where are you going, my friends?

become—ङाहनी स्‍युक म़ेहबा डोएँ।I am becoming weak.—तिङगु खाॱयाला काङबा पा़ना ङ्योक्‍पा मेहन्‍डोएँ?If you step onto the mat, won't it become dirty?—जिङसीमाना ताम फेमाज्‍यीगीॱआङ ठुक्‍पा छि्यम्‍बु डोएँ।If you really quarrel, even because of a small matter the quarrel may become big.
small settlement on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley, below Thaalo
souls of dead people and animals
