be astonished—दि लाहक्‍पागी योहल्‍मु ऱेह–ऱेह सोङ्यीम फुल्‍गेन्‍दीला लामा हाराङ ल़ेहसीन दु लो।The lama was very astonished about this custom of giving only a handful of alms in every place.—खोला थोङदी ङाह हा ल़ेहसीन!He astonished me!
1make a lot of noise for nothing
{make} a trial—दि तोहङस्‍यो हाजे यिहम्‍बा।This is the first trial.—ओहङगे नि मेहयोहङगे, हाजे ताएँ।Let me try if it's alright or not.
do s.th. carelessly—दाङ खोगी ज्‍यिबुराङ ल़ेह हातार हुतुर पेहसीन।Yesterday he did a very careless job.
outbid s.b—खोगी ङाहला हानावार पेहकु दु।I see, he is outbidding me!
1ground full of crevices/ holes—लाहमॱयाला हाप्‍ताङ होप्‍तोङ दु।
be greedy for s.b.'s property/ belongings
greed—मिहगी पेलाला हाराम पेहती क्‍याहल्‍गे।He claims other people's things as his possession.
pair—लाङ हाल सुम।three pairs of oxen yoked for ploughing.—खुङ ङ्यी़ हाल या़हबु डी़उ दु।They are a good pair (for work or business).
hurry up—पेम्‍बागी हुर्ता पेहसीन।Pemba hurried up.—पेम्‍बागी याहम्‍बुला डोज्‍ये हुर्ता पेहसीन।Pemba was in a hurry to go to Kathmandu.
fast (in doing work)—खुङ लाङगो म़ेदी ङि्यह लाङगो हुर्पु येहबा!Our ox works faster than theirs!—चो़मे छ्युदा म़ेदी थो़मे छ्युदा हुर्पु दु।I found out that the upper mill is faster than the lower one.

occasion—ओहले ओ़ह तोहरा छूगे हुल्‍दाङ च्‍यिपुदीनी च्‍यि ता़ह! नाम्‍गा कार्माजेराङ दु!And when (we) started the first time with the dancing circle - well, there were as many as the stars in the sky! (dancers).
spread out—ङि्यहमाला काप्‍च्‍या हेल्‍दी ज़्‍या़गे।I spread out the clothes in the sun.—हे माहरुहल्‍ज्‍यु म़ेदी हेल्‍दी ज़्‍या़क्‍यो।I spread out the potatoes so that they would not rot.
pasture above Kahng-Yuhl on the high ridge between Sermathang and Lha-Kahng Ghyang
there (at quite some distance)—लामा, ख्‍येगी तोर्मा ता य़ूह गुला येह।Lama, your ceremonial statue is up there in that corner.
try to—ङाहगी लु प्री़ह(दी) तासीन, ला़ मा़हखे।I tried to jot down a song, but I was not successful.—दि लु लेहन त़े लेह!Please try to sing this song!
Kesare (village north-west of Sermathang, close to it, just behind the ridge)
1Kutumsang (on the western ridge of the Melamchi valley)
Ningale (a village on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley, the highest one on the ridge south of Sermathang; below it are: Thaalo, ɖomdong, Mehnjya)

along a location—खो क्‍याहपता क्‍याहप काहल्‍सीन।He went along the back.—खुङ ओहराङ थो़दा थो़ डोउ दु!Oh, they are going along above us!—रेहन्‍जा सुहर सुहरतानी च़ूसीन, क्‍यिललानी मा़हचु।Along the edges I planted soya beens, but not in the middle.—फार–ङो़ खाङबा-दाङ छुर–ङो़ खाङबा नाहङ नाहङदा डोसा येहबा, फिला थेन मेहकोहएँ।These two houses are connected inside, you can go from one into the other without going outside.

verb stem nominalizer with fairly neutral meaning—ज़्‍या़दाङ-दाङ कार्ताङ या़हबु येहना माहङबुनी गार मी़हतीएँ।If things are weighed and stored properly not much will be lost.—लाङदाङ चोक्‍पा!What an unruly way of asking for something!—दि योहल्‍मु लुहङबागी मिहन ज़्‍या़दाङ थोप्‍ताङ।How the Helambu region got its name.

1and—खाप-ताङ कुपा ख्‍येरस्‍यो ले!Please bring me a needle and some thread!—ओ़हला ज़्‍यु-दाङ दा प़ेहदी येहबा दु।There a bow and arrow are hidden.—ओहराङ कोहम्‍बाॱयाला दिङबु फुल्‍गेन-दाङ ङ्येन्‍दार फुल्‍गेन-दाङ ताहर्च्‍यो फुल्‍गेन जाती ओहराङ नाहम्‍साला फेन कोहच्‍ये म़ेदी फुल्‍ग्‍यो यिहम्‍बा।The donating of the horizontally and vertically hung up cloths in the temples, and the donating of the prayerflags should benefit our clothing.

horizontally—ङि्यह स्‍यार्पादी मिह स्‍यिसीमाना तोहङले तो़ले याहर ताङे ङ्याल्‍दे ऱेह कार्पुगी उप्‍कें।If among us Sherpas a person dies, we first lay him down up there horizontally and cover him with a white cotton cloth.
sow–नो़हदीगीनी ऩेह ताप्‍सीन दु लो।As to the younger brother, he sowed wheat.
pilgrimage place above the Melamchi River near Naahkote
rug shaped to fit a horse's back or the saddle
1grind (grains, etc.)—तिहरीङ ङ़ेबी था़ डो कोहएँ।Today we have to go and grind (parched) flour.
bring to a conclusion—खुङगी स्‍यिहङगी ताम तिहरीङ था़ च्‍येकें लो।He said that they will bring the matter about the field to a conclusion today.
1far (in distance)—खोगी था़ रिहङबुलेगी तासीन।He watched from far away.
spelling—योहल्‍मो तामगी थाक-छ्या ओहजेबा का़हबु मेहयोहङगेन।The spelling of Yohlmo is not all that difficult.
be tired—ङि्यह च्‍योहज्‍यो थाङ छ्येपा यिहम्‍बा छ़े दु।Our elder brother looks tired.
keep pulling at—बासोॱया ता़ ज़्‍या़ना डोज्‍ये म़ेदी थाङ थाङ पेहकें।When you tie up the cattle they keep pulling at their ropes because they want to go where they like.
rest—लाहमला डोएँमु तोङबा येहना ‘याहम्‍बीज्‍यी थाङ सोएँ’ माएँ।If you are not carrying anything when you walk, you use 'ÿthaÊ þso-en' to stop for resting.
village on the western slope of the Melamchi River valley, below Phungbu-Che
boil over—तिहरीङ ओहमा थाब्‍लो ओहङसीन।Today the milk boiled over.
inferior—खो मिहॱया म़ेदी थामा दु।He is inferior to most people.
all—ङि्यह थाम्‍ज्‍यीराङ देलाराङ येह।We are all here.—लुवाङ थाम्‍ज्‍यी साला ओहङगे।Everybody has a spirit enclosure (at his house).—ङाहला ताम थाम्‍ज्‍यीराङ स्‍येतोङ!Tell me everything!
spread thinly—सेन थाराङ थोरोङज्‍यी क्‍येसीन दु।The seeds sprouted thinly.
piece (used for things and animals)—मिह च्‍यी़गीॱआङ माहइ ङा थाल सोएँ!Even though he/ she is alone, he/ she takes care of 5 buffalos!
village on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley (on the ridge south of Sermathang, below Tap-Kharka)
make the hand gesture which is appropriate for a polite request
any tool used to make sure that things will be straight

join together—काङबागी रेहकोनी ख्‍योक्‍तोङ पेहती थि दु!The bone of the leg had grown together in a crooked way.—मेहन्‍दा सुम थिते थितेराङ स्‍यी़दे नाहले!Three people died, one after the other!—ङाहगी थाक्‍पा थिती!I joined the ropes.—ओहराङ ङ्येहवा थि कोहज्‍ये दुबा!We two must get together for good!
hammer with sharp point used to rough up the mill stones—छ्युदा चो़गे थिउ।the hammer used to rough up the mill stones.
steadiness—खोगी ल़ेहला थिक-चि म़ेहबा, थाप थोप पेहकें।He has no steadiness in his work, he gets restless.—सेम थिक-चि म़ेहबा, थाप थोप पेहकें।He is not settled, he gets restless.
obedient—फेमा येहकेन बेलाले ङाहनी थिङबु येहकेन।I used to be an obedient child.

meditate—ङाटु याहम्‍बीज्‍यी थिन क्‍याङ कोहएँ।In the morning you must meditate a moment.—थिन ताङगेन बेलाले केहलोङ च्‍योहमुॱयागी मिहॱया ङ्याम्‍बु ताम मेहमा दु।I observed that during meditation times the monks and nuns don't talk with people.
seep away—छ्यु साला थिम्‍सीन।The water seeped into the ground.
easy to get along with—ओहले ता़ह च्‍यी़ ङ्यी़ थिम्‍बु मेहयोहङगे, खा फ़ेदी डोएँ।And some don't get along with each other, and they separate.
palm of hand—लाहक्‍पा थिल काहङ पार्च्‍याङ थुङ!Please drink a palmful of ceremonially blessed liquor!
perform the rituals for the post-funeral ceremony for a child, or for various other occasions
drink—ङाहगी च्‍याह थुङसीन।I already drank tea.
be angry—मेहमे थुयाल माहज्‍याङ, दिनी कुर्स्‍या यिहम्‍बा!Please don't get angry, grandfather, this is just a joke!
knowledge—ओ़ह सोङगोराङ नाहरे लामाॱयाला थुयी़ ज्‍यिबु म़ेहबा।Therefore the lamas don't know very much about this now.
{descend} sloping downwards—ओ़ह थुर-च़ूनी फाप्‍च्‍येला खेन्‍दा का़हबु दु।It is very difficult to go down this steep hill.—दि लाहम ज्‍यिबुराङ थुर-च़ू दु।Oh, this path goes down very steeply.
1wind up  (e.g. thread)—ङाहगी कुपा थुल्‍गेन।I wind up the woolen balls (for weaving carpets).
upside down—च्‍याहङला क्‍याहप्‍केन बेले तिहपा थुला डोएँ।When the north wind blows, the smoke falls.
meet—ङाहगी ताहज्‍यी पासाङ थूक्‍यो।I met Pasang a while ago (not by chance).—पेहपा ङि्यहला थ़ूक्‍यो।The Tibetan met us (by chance).
hear s.th. directly—ओहजेलानी आछामगी थ़े दु लो।At that moment Atsham heard it.—ङाहगी नाम्‍ज्‍योला थ़ेसीन।I heard it myself.
hear s.th. indirectly—मोहगी रोह मी़हछीएँ पेहते तेहकु दु लो म़ेदे आछामला थ़े दु लो, माहरला।Atsham, who was down (in his village), heard that her corpse did not burn.
give enlightenment to a departed soul or sometimes also to living people
straight—रेहको थेका पेहती थिको येहबा।The bone will grow together straight.
stretch out s.th. lengthwise—काप्‍च्‍या कान्‍द्राङ कुन्‍द्रुङ काहल्‍सीन दु, ओहले ङाहगी थेन्‍ग्‍यो।The clothes were crumpled up, so I stretched them.

hit a goal—ओ़हला गो़ली थेप दु।Oh, a bullet hit there.—तोह माहर रिहल्‍दे ओहङदे कोहरेला थेप्‍सीन।The stone came rolling from above and hit the cattle shed.—ङाहला तोहगी थेप्‍सीन।The stone hit me.—तेम्‍बु पेहती सुङ, ख्‍येला गो़लीगी थेप्‍कोॱवा!Go carefully, otherwise you might be hit by a bullet!
hold up to give light (lamp, torch)—मिह डोसाला ङाहगी बा़ती थेप तेरक्‍यो।I held up the lamp for the people going by.
only just now—याहम्‍बीज्‍यी क़ूह आ़, ङाह थेरेरे लाहङगो येहबा।Wait a minute, I am only just getting up.
plaster (floors and walls with mud)—खुङगी चिक्‍पा देप सासागी थेल्‍ग्‍यो।They plastered the walls with mud.
hand-span—थो काहङ/ तो़ह/ सुम।one/ two/ three hand-spans.
take (of time)—ओहले लोह माहङबुराङ थो़सीन लो।And it took many years they said.

register in which the gifts given for funerals are recorded—सेम-सो(ल) काहजे तेर्पा थो-यिहला प्री़ह ज़्‍या़एँ।The gifts which are given for a funeral are recorded in the register (especially kept for this purpose, in view of reciprocation).
plough—थोङ ङ्यास्‍यीङला केन्‍गेन।fix the plough to the yoke.
{fry/ cook} vegetable or meat curry (with little sauce)—थोङसे लाम्‍गेमु डा़सीन दु।I see, the curry got scorched when it was fried.
seal s.th—ङि्यहगी डु पाहङाला ल़ूदी आमागी पाहङा खाला थेजी क्‍याहप ज़्‍या़दी येहबा।Having put our grains into the big wooden chest mother put a seal onto the chest.
settlement on the western ridge of the of Yohlmu, before Golbhanjyāng
1mark s.th. as one's property (by piling together 3 stones)
forgive me—ङ्य़ेहबा जोल्‍वा थोलो स्‍या!Please forgive me for any shortcomings and mistakes.
only just now—याहम्‍बीज्‍यी क़ूह आ़, ङाह थेरेरे लाहङगो येहबा।Wait a minute, I am only just getting up.
plaster (floors and walls with mud)—खुङगी चिक्‍पा देप सासागी थेल्‍ग्‍यो।They plastered the walls with mud.
hand-span—थो काहङ/ तो़ह/ सुम।one/ two/ three hand-spans.
take (of time)—ओहले लोह माहङबुराङ थो़सीन लो।And it took many years they said.

register in which the gifts given for funerals are recorded—सेम-सो(ल) काहजे तेर्पा थो-यिहला प्री़ह ज़्‍या़एँ।The gifts which are given for a funeral are recorded in the register (especially kept for this purpose, in view of reciprocation).
plough—थोङ ङ्यास्‍यीङला केन्‍गेन।fix the plough to the yoke.
{fry/ cook} vegetable or meat curry (with little sauce)—थोङसे लाम्‍गेमु डा़सीन दु।I see, the curry got scorched when it was fried.
seal s.th—ङि्यहगी डु पाहङाला ल़ूदी आमागी पाहङा खाला थेजी क्‍याहप ज़्‍या़दी येहबा।Having put our grains into the big wooden chest mother put a seal onto the chest.
settlement on the western ridge of the of Yohlmu, before Golbhanjyāng
1mark s.th. as one's property (by piling together 3 stones)
forgive me—ङ्य़ेहबा जोल्‍वा थोलो स्‍या!Please forgive me for any shortcomings and mistakes.

arrow (for shooting with bow)—दाङ ङि्यहगी दा चेसीन।Yesterday we were shooting with arrows.—क्‍याहल्‍बुगी फेकाला ज़्‍यु-दाङ दा प़ेहदी येहबा दु।It turned out that the king had hidden a bow and arrows in his wide robe.
lick—योहल्‍मो खाङबाला तेहर्मा दा़ज्‍ये ओहङगेन।In a Yohlmo household you are allowed to lick your plate.
pasture above Chhimi on the high ridge between Sermathang and Lha-Kahng Ghyang
settlement on the western slope of the Melamchi River valley, on the hill north of Gohare Khola
chunky and big—स्‍या दागा दोगे।big pieces of meat (the way it comes from the butcher).
bunch of cloths fixed on a stick
some time ago—आज्‍यीनी दा़नु पोहमो ख्‍योङदे ओहङग्‍यो।Some time ago my elder sister came, and she brought her daughter with her.
always—ङाहगी दा़र च्‍यी़राङ ख्‍येला ओ़ह फुक्‍पा नाहङला तो क्‍याल ओहङगे।I will keep coming into that cave to bring food to you.
ceiling beam—ख्‍याती दार्नी ग़ू दु।Oh, your ceiling beam is sagging.
pen (biro)दार्पेन ख्‍य़ूगु दु।Oh, the pen is writing.
win over—ल्‍हागी सिम्‍बुला दाल्‍गेन।The gods win over the giants.
become mushy—खाम्‍बु छ़ेदी दाल्‍सीन दु।The peaches are so ripe that they have become mushy.

proof (of a material kind) {look for/ find}—क्‍याहल्‍बुला सुगी सेक्‍यो म़ेदी दास्‍यी छोल्‍गेराङ येह, ताहन्‍दा साम्‍मानी च्‍य़ेह माहसीहन।They are looking for proof as to who killed the king, but up to now they have not found anything.
this season—आहन्‍डा माना ख्‍ये दि पाहर्क्‍यी याहम्‍बुला डोएँ नि?In that case, are you going to Kathmandu this season?

{paint} wall frescos—खो दिब्री ख़ेबा यिहम्‍बा।He is a fresco painter.—ओहले देपला दिब्रीगी ल़ेहॱया दावा माहङबुला सिहन्‍दा च्‍येसीन।And it took us many months to finish the fresco paintings on the walls.
tremble (not used of people)—रिहॱराङ दिर्कुदु!The earth is trembling!

two ends meet up with each other—मिह थाम्‍ज्‍यीराङ लाहक्‍पाला मेहन्‍दो खादा थो़दी कोहम्‍बा हार्केनलेगी कोहम्‍बा साम्‍मा मिह दी़क्‍यो।Carrying flowers and ceremonial scarfs in their hands, there was a line of people which went quite far from the other side of the temple right up to the temple.—ताम-छी़ सुमला दी़क्‍यो।give a summary.
community—दी़जो कोहम्‍बाला क़ेङ्येर युहल्‍बागी ज़्‍या़गे।For the community temple the villagers hire a person to look after the temple.
usually—दुक्‍तेनी दि मिह या़हबुराङ येहकेन, तिहरीङ नाहङबार स्‍यिहताङ च्‍यी़राङ पेहकु दु।He used to be a nice fellow, but nowadays he is always angry.
village on the western slope of the Melamchi River valley, below Thang-Sying Kahng
sprouts—फार्सीगी दुङदुङ जाम्‍माराङ बुगी स़ेह दुबा!Oh, the worms have eaten up all the pumpkin sprouts!
intensifier for '¹preeh-'—ओ़ह मिहनी दुप प़्रेह दुबा, कुहङमुराङ!That man had swiftly run away, already in the evening!
join up (people)—खुङ टोहङबा च्‍यी़ला दुम्‍ग्‍यो।They joined up in one household.
exactly the same—दि ङ्यी़गी पेहताङ दुर-सुता दु।These two behave exactly the same.
district—ताहङबु ताहङबु प़ेह क्‍याहल–खापला दे–खाम ट़ूह येहबा लो।Formerly there were six districts in the kingdom of Tibet.
go into or be in a trance in which the medium visits the realm of the dead—मेहमे द़े-लोक्‍पा।way to refer to a male medium.—यिबी द़े-लोङमा।way to refer to a female medium.
flat (of sitting)—साला देरे तेह!Sit properly on the floor!
quite big—तोह दोक दोक च्‍यी़ ख्‍येर स्‍यो!Bring quite a big stone!
the whole thing (e.g. pumpkin, bread, etc.)—तेर्ना दि क्‍येक दोगेराङ साह खूएँ!I can eat the whole cake in one go, if you allow me to!
loud and hollow (noise)—क़े दोङ दोङ तेन्‍गु दु।There is a loud, hallow noise coming from somewhere.
upper flow of Tadi Khola west of Yohlmu
small rubber ball—स्‍यो, दोप–दोप चेएँ।Come, let's play with the ball.
{take} a footstep—खो दोम्‍बा रिहङाज्‍यी पा़दी डोउ दु।Oh, he is taking long steps.
small rubber ball—स्‍यो, दोप–दोप चेएँ।Come, let's play with the ball.
{take} a footstep—खो दोम्‍बा रिहङाज्‍यी पा़दी डोउ दु।Oh, he is taking long steps.
conditional—ताहलेन बाइसाकला ओहङना ओहङसीन, मेहयोहङसी मानानी मोह का़हला डोएँ?If she comes this year in Baisakh then that's fine; if she doesn't come, then where will she go?

swear an oath—ना क्‍याल्‍ज्‍येला कोहम्‍बाला डोसीमाना छ्य़ेमी ज़ुङदी ना क्‍याल्‍गे।If you go to the temple for making an oath, you hold on to a butter lamp when giving the oath (taking it as a witness).
be disturbed by noise—क़े माहङबु माहतेन, ङाह ना़ छाउ दु!Don't shout like that, it's getting on my nerves!
pitch black—मुक्‍पा ना़-लोहगे दु।Oh, these clouds are pitch black.
get tired of a certain food—सेहन च्‍यी़राङ स़ेहदे स़ेहदेॱआङ, ता़हनी ङाहला नाल लो़हसीन।We have been eating so much corn mash lately, I tired of it now.
give—ओहले साङग्‍येगी ल्‍हाला का च्‍यी़ नाङसीन।And then Sanggye gave Lha a commandment.

auxiliary verb added to a non-honorific vs or pred.ph. to make it honorific—ओ़ह सोङबे तिहक्‍पागी तेहन्‍दाला नोम्‍साङ ताङ नाङदोङ, ङ्येन्‍जेन ङयेहवाॱयाTherefore reflect about the matter of sin, my friends and relatives.

verb stem nominalizer referring to the appearance of an action or state—काप्‍च्‍या साम्‍बा ज़्‍या़नाङ चोक्‍पा पेहती ज़्‍या़ना निङबा ल़ेहमु थोङगे।If you store new clothes in an unorderly way they will look like old stuff.—काप्‍च्‍या निङबा येहॱनाङ केहन्‍नाङ या़हबु येहना ज्‍यार्पु थोङगे।Even if the clothes are old, if you put them on neatly, they will look nice.
worker (working for wages)—नाङबाॱयाला ला/ ल़ेगा तेर कोहएँ।You have to give the workers wages/ a salary.
all kinds of—खुङ साला तोप्‍च्‍या नाजेराङ साहउ दु।The eat all kinds of different foods.
be fed up with a person—ओ़ह ल़ेहमु तामॱया काहजे म़े तेहकेन यिहम्‍बा? ङि्यहला नान्‍डी स्‍योर्सीन!Who much longer will you talk about such things? We are fed up with it!
bear the consequences of a false oath—छ्या़ छाल्‍दी ना क्‍याल्‍गे बेलाले पा़पी माएँदीदी नाप्‍सीवा डोएँ।The one who swears a false oath bowing down (in the temple) will have to endure hardships.

all kinds of—ओहले सोक्‍च्‍युङ ङि्यहमुदी ङ्युङनेला तेहके मिह थाम्‍ज्‍यीलाराङ तोप्‍च्‍या नामे नाजो स्‍यिहम्‍बु स्‍यिहम्‍बुॱया तेर्कें।And on the second day all sorts of nice food is given to all the participants of the fasting festival.
ear—ख्‍येगी नाम्‍ज्‍यो येहन्‍ग्‍यो दुबा!You must be deaf! (abusive expression)
spin (includes the whole process of preparing yarn)
be chronically ill—खोनी ऩेह यिहन्‍ज्‍यु टा़हॱराङ मा़हखु, नार्तेराङ तेहसीन।Once he had fallen ill, he just couldn't get better; he became chronically ill.
become ill—ङाह ल़ेनु येहकेन बेलाले ङि्यह आमा ज्‍यिबुराङ नाहक्‍यो लो।They tell me that my mother was very ill when I was a small baby.
village on the western side of the Melamchi River, on the slope north of Gohare Khola
black and foreboding—मुक्‍पा ना़ह–लुहङ चोक्‍पा दु!Oh, there are foreboding black clouds!
Naahkote (wide-spread settlement on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley, below Langgyapsa)
inside part—तार्का नाहङगो स्‍यिहम्‍बु - खाम्‍बु फिको स्‍यिहम्‍बु।With walnuts the inside part is tasty - with peaches the outside one.
{put on} short necklace with coral, turquoise, and '¹zi'

precious things (shown only inside)—नाहङदेन फिला तेन्‍दी लेहमेन ग्‍या़ला मेहताङगे, नाहङला च्‍यी़राङ तेन्‍गे।Some precious things are not taken out and sent to other places, they are shown only inside.
precious things (of personal value)—ओ़हदी खोगी मेहतेर्टो, ओ़हदी खुङगी नाहङदेर यिहम्‍बा।He will probably not sell this, this is a precious thing of personal value.

among—रो़ह नाहङदोङ मिताप पेहकेन।Among friends you wink your eyes at each other.—ओ़ह खाङबा सुम नाहङला खुङदी थो़स्‍योदी यिहम्‍बा।Among those three houses theirs is the highest (in size or at the highest altitude).
tomorrow—ङि्यहन्‍दोङ नाहङबारॱया, ओहले ख्‍येराङगी ङोह स्‍य़ेएँ लोह!Listen to it in the days to come, and you will know it yourself!

take a foreordained course (e.g. stars, animals)—थोदोङ कार्च्‍येनगी नाहम्‍सी ज़ुङगेन, ओहले च्‍याहबुगीॱआङ नाहम्‍सी ज़ुङगेन।The big star always appears at the same time, and the cock also crows at his regular time.
festival on which deep-fried bread is distributed to the villagers;
arranged in a row—खुङ पाटी ओ़हला नाहरे दु।Oh, they are standing in a row.—स्‍येल्‍बुङॱया पाहङग्‍याला नाहरे (पेहती) ज़्‍यो़!Put the bottles on the shelf into a row!
small pipe of mountain bamboo (used for smoking the water pipe, cf. '¹tohwa')—तोहवा थुङगे नाहली।the pipe for smoking the water pipe.
emphatic confirmation—ख्‍या छ्युदा का़हला ले? – ङि्यह छ्युदा ता म़ूहला नाहले!Where is your water mill? - Down there, obviously!
or—ख्‍ये तिहरीङ डोएँ नि मेहन्‍डोएँ?Are you going today or not?
settlement above Tap-Kharka, a little more north, just below the ridge
confide in s.b—ङाहगी ल़ेह चोक्‍पा पेहती, ओहले रो़ह थिम्‍बुला निङदाम च्‍योल्‍सीन।I did something wrong, but then I confided what I did to a good friend.
old (of inanimate things)—ङाहती च्‍य़ेन-रेहजीगी थाङगु निङबाज्‍यी येहबा।I have an old thangka of the god of deliverance.
withdraw—काछुवागी गो़ नाहङला नुप्‍कु दु।The turtle is withdrawing its head.

tangle up s.th—पेहजाॱयागी कुपा जाम्‍माराङ नुुर्सीन/ नुहर च्‍य़ूसीन।The children tangled up all the yarn.—प्रुहलगी ओम नाहङलेगी गो़ नुर नुर पेहकु दु।The snake is sticking its head out of the hole and withdrawing it again.
Nuhrpu Kahng (village on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley, on the way to Langgyapsa before Timbu, fairly wide-spread settlements)
sink (e.g. in water)—काछुवागी गो़ला द़ेना गो़ नुहप्‍कें।If you touch a turtle's head it disappears.
woolen back apron of thick quality with broad coloured stripes on black background
1get tangled—कुपा जाम्‍माराङ नुहर्सीन दु!The yarn is all tangled up!
suspect—ख्‍येगी ङाहला कुमेन ऩेक्‍यो?Do you suspect me to be a thief?
seem (i.e. find s.th. to be of a certain quality)—दि खाङबा ङाहला या़हबु ऩेसीन।I like this house.
dwell (used for spirits and gods)—दि तोहलानी लु ऩेगे लो, मा़हन्‍दे रो़ह!A nature spirit lives in this stone, they say, don't touch it!
conceive—केरे, सामे तुहक्‍यो, ऩेगे नि म़ेहनेगे? – ऩेसीन दु।Well, the cow has been with the bull, will she conceive or not? -- She has conceived.
harm s.b—ए़राककी पेहजाॱयाला नेकें।Drinking whisky is harmful for children.
affect badly (mostly used of foods)—दि स़ेह साहसीमाना ङाहला नेकें।Eating this kind of food affects me badly.

original_word: ऩे कोर-/ ऩे कोरा क्‍याह्‌प-/ ऩेगोरला डो-
pronunciation: ⁴nee ⁴kor-/ ⁴nee ⁴kora ¹kyahp-/ ⁴nee-gor-la ²ɖo-

english_meaning: burn a piece of paper with some text and the name of the deceased person on it—ऩेबार ङि्यहमु च्‍याहङबारला स्‍यिम्‍बुगी मिहन प्री़हदी ऩे ताङगे/ पार्कें।On the day of the 'ÿneebar' ceremony the name of the deceased person is written on a certain piece of paper which them is burnt.

arrange the loom in such a way that the threads of the warp can be pulled out alternately—था़ प्रेहन्‍दी ऩे तुह कोहएँ।After fixing the warp, the threads need to be arranged so the they can be pulled out alternately.
lama settlement a short distance west of Taa-Khor (on the western ridge of the Melamchi valley)
lodge—याहम्‍बुला ओहङगेमु का़हन्‍दी ऩेजाङला तेहपा?In which lodge did you stay when you came to Kathmandu?
place of pilgrimage—याङरी काहङ ऩेनाङज्‍यी, उइ रो़ह!Yangri Kahng is a beautiful place of pilgrimage, isn't it, my friend?
spirit of sickness—“नेपा टोल कोहज्‍ये दु” म़ेदी पोहम्‍बोगी माएँ थ़ेक्‍यो।I have heard the shaman say, "The spirit of sickness needs to be pacified."

ceremony performed 1-3 weeks after somebody dies or during '¹kyehwa'—ऩेबार पेहती ऩे माहताङ साम्‍मा स्‍यिक्‍यो मिहगी सेम माहल्‍साला ओहङगे।Until the 'ÿneebar' ceremony takes place the spirit of the deceased person remains in the cloth dummy.
press down—तोहगी खोला नेम्‍सीन।The stone pressed him down.
exhibit malicious pleasure—ङाहगी टाङगा कुमेनगी ख्‍येर्सीन, ओहले खोगी ङाहला नेम्‍जेन ताप्‍कें नाह।My money has been stolen, and I know he is full of malicious pleasure.

{be affected} by harmful food—“नेहपागी ऩेसे साहज्‍ये मेहयोहङगे, ऩेसे स्‍योर्कें!” म़ेदी माएँ।They say that sick people mustn't eat any food which is harmful for their sickness, otherwise they will get worse!"
visit a sick person or a mother with a newborn baby

original_word: नेह्‌ल नेह्‌ल/ ङ्येह्‌ल ङ्येह्‌ल/ नाह्‌ल नाह्‌ल पेह्‌-क
pronunciation: ¹nehl ¹nehl/ ¹ŋyehl ¹ŋyehl/ ¹nahl ¹nahl peh-k
english_meaning: be slimy—क्‍याहब्रे साहएँमु खाला नेहल नेहल पेहकु दु।Oh, buckwheat spinach feels so slimy in your mouth.—पा़हना ङ्येहल ङ्येहल पेहती थेन्‍गु दु।Oh, there is sticky slime coming out.
spoil s.th./ s.b—दि स़ेह ङाहगी नोङॱडाङ माहनोङ, खोराङ नोहङग्‍यो!I didn't spoil this thing, it just broke by itself!

hatch eggs (of hens)—कोहङा क्‍ये सिहन फान्‍ज्‍यो च्‍याहमु नोङमा तेहकें। नोङमा तेह फान्‍ज्‍यो कुकु म़ेदी क़े तेन्‍गे, ओहले कुकु तेहकेंॱआङ माएँ।When the hen has finished laying her eggs she hatches them. When she is hatching eggs, she cackles "kuku", and so we also call it "kuku 'tehken."
{accumulate} wealth; riches—बार्माला काहल्‍देॱआङ ङाहगी नोहर ज़ो मा़हखु।Even though I went to India, I could accumulate no wealth.
sink (e.g. in water)—काछुवागी गो़ला द़ेना गो़ नुहप्‍कें।If you touch a turtle's head it disappears.
woolen back apron of thick quality with broad coloured stripes on black background
1get tangled—कुपा जाम्‍माराङ नुहर्सीन दु!The yarn is all tangled up!
harm s.b—ए़राककी पेहजाॱयाला नेकें।Drinking whisky is harmful for children.
affect badly (mostly used of foods)—दि स़ेह साहसीमाना ङाहला नेकें।Eating this kind of food affects me badly.
spirit of sickness—“नेपा टोल कोहज्‍ये दु” म़ेदी पोहम्‍बोगी माएँ थ़ेक्‍यो।I have heard the shaman say, "The spirit of sickness needs to be pacified."
spirit of sickness—“नेपा टोल कोहज्‍ये दु” म़ेदी पोहम्‍बोगी माएँ थ़ेक्‍यो।I have heard the shaman say, "The spirit of sickness needs to be pacified."
spirit of sickness—“नेपा टोल कोहज्‍ये दु” म़ेदी पोहम्‍बोगी माएँ थ़ेक्‍यो।I have heard the shaman say, "The spirit of sickness needs to be pacified."
spirit of sickness—“नेपा टोल कोहज्‍ये दु” म़ेदी पोहम्‍बोगी माएँ थ़ेक्‍यो।I have heard the shaman say, "The spirit of sickness needs to be pacified."
spirit of sickness—“नेपा टोल कोहज्‍ये दु” म़ेदी पोहम्‍बोगी माएँ थ़ेक्‍यो।I have heard the shaman say, "The spirit of sickness needs to be pacified."
spirit of sickness—“नेपा टोल कोहज्‍ये दु” म़ेदी पोहम्‍बोगी माएँ थ़ेक्‍यो।I have heard the shaman say, "The spirit of sickness needs to be pacified."
press down—तोहगी खोला नेम्‍सीन।The stone pressed him down.

pronunciation: ¹nehl ¹nehl/ ¹ŋyehl ¹ŋyehl/ ¹nahl ¹nahl peh-k

hatch eggs (of hens)—कोहङा क्‍ये सिहन फान्‍ज्‍यो च्‍याहमु नोङमा तेहकें। नोङमा तेह फान्‍ज्‍यो कुकु म़ेदी क़े तेन्‍गे, ओहले कुकु तेहकेंॱआङ माएँ।When the hen has finished laying her eggs she hatches them. When she is hatching eggs, she cackles "kuku", and so we also call it "kuku 'tehken."
{accumulate} wealth; riches—बार्माला काहल्‍देॱआङ ङाहगी नोहर ज़ो मा़हखु।Even though I went to India, I could accumulate no wealth.
miss the right time (to do a certain agricultural job)—हे च़ूज्‍येला याहम्‍बीज्‍यी पा थाल्‍सीन।It's a little bit late to plant to potatoes.
small settlement in the Nuhrpu-Kahng area (near Padap towards the north)
1person from the place to which the suffix is added
boards under the roof protrusion (to keep wind out)
outside—पेहजाॱया नाहङला माहतेह, पाङला चेमु चे सोङ!Children, don't stay inside, go and play outside!
pasture above Kahng-Yuhl on the high ridge between Sermathang and Lha-Kahng Ghyang
fallow ground (flat or sloping)—ओहले क्‍याहल्‍बु कोहम्‍बागी हार–ङ़ेगी पाङगाला हेलीकोप्‍टारले पाहप नाङसीन।And the king descended from the helicopter on the fallow ground this side of the temple.
small settlement on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley, below ʈasyi Thang
back apron (of fine quality, with finely striped pattern)
{make} a pile (of things which can be stacked up)—ङाहदी पाङलेप पाङजी च्‍यी़नी कुमेनगी उहप क्‍या़ ख्‍येर दु।Oh, a thief has snatched away one of my stacks of shingles.
take s.b. along as a witness—किन्‍ज्‍यो पाङबु ज़्‍या़दी ना क्‍याल्‍गेन।perform an oath invoking God as witness.
forest nettle—दि पा़ज्‍यार कुपागी था़क्‍योगी फेङगा यिहम्‍बा।This is fabric made of forest nettle fibres.

{be/ call} a witness (pertaining to the traditional village law)—ङाहगी फास्‍यी कोह कोहज्‍ये येहसीमाना ङाहगी सेम ल़े खाल ख़ूएँ मिहॱयाला पान्‍जा ज़्‍या़एँ।When I have to divide the heritage, I call some trustworthy persons to be witnesses.—पा़हमा पेहकेन बेलाले पान्‍जाॱयागी पी़हमीगी ला़गीला काहम्‍ग्‍या प्रुहएँ।When getting married the witnesses write a marriage agreement for the woman. (An optional custom).
light (lamp, torch, or wood)—मिहॱया फार डोएँमु मी़ थोङज्‍यु म़ेदी बा़ती पार्कें।We light the lamp so that people can see when they go by.
kind (for unusual or bad things)—च्‍यि पा़र काहमज्‍यी उइ!What a funny chest!
be located—तिम्‍बु योहल्‍मुला पा़रती़एँ।Timbu is in Helambu.
end up with—ङि्यहला सेन चोक्‍पा पा़रती़ दु।Oh, we ended up with bad seeds.
ritually blessed liquor or other drinks—ङाहनी छ्याङ माएँदी पार्च्‍याङज्‍यीॱआङ मिहथुङ येह!I do not drink alcoholic beverages, not even the ritually blessed little portion.
from time to time—खाङबागी स्‍यिङज्‍याॱयानी पाहर्क्‍यी पाहर्क्‍यीला पो कोहएँ काहल्‍सीन।The wooden parts of a house have to be exchanged from time to time.
sheet of mountain bamboo (finely woven, flexible)
misfortune—ल्‍हागी पाहर्च्‍ये ताङगेन।the gods send misfortune.
name of the most important day of the '¹naahra' festival
place between hell and heaven where souls are punished for lesser sins—पाहर्तोला खोर्वा ख्‍याम्‍गेन/ आला ख्‍याम्‍गेन।wander around in 'ýpahrto'.
the middle one—पुह/ पोहमो पाहर्पा।the second son/ daughter (if there are more than two sons or daughters).
clear woodland (make arable)—पाल प्रा़हसीम्‍बाराङ सेल सा़गे, ओहले कामे क्‍याहप्‍केन।You cut down all the trees and shrubs and burn everything, and then you do the planting.
1bury s.th. (properly)—गुरीगी क्‍याक्‍पा पाहगेन।Cats bury their feces.
stack up—खोगी नेक्‍यी पा़हउ दु।He is stacking up the pots.
add—खोला तो छ्य़ेमुज्‍यी पा़हदोङ!/ पा़ह तेर!Please add some rice for him!
play a gambling game (with round seeds)
{make} dumplings with wheat flour (to add to a broth with potatoes or other vegetables)
place defiled by birth or death—पाहङला स्‍य़ूहज्‍ये मेहयोहङगे, टिहप फो़गे!You mustn't go into a house which is defiled by birth or death, the spirit of defilement will affect you!

get soaked/ soak s.th—नाम क्‍याहप्‍ते ङाह पाहङसीन।It was raining and I got soaked.—तिहरीङ ङाह नामगी पाहङसीन।Today I got soaked by the rain.—नाम क्‍याहप्‍ते ङाहला नामगी पाहङसीन।I got soaked by the rain.—ङाहगी काप्‍च्‍या पाहङ ज़्‍या़सीन।I put the clothes for soaking.
slime—नाम-ख्‍यूगी पा़हना/ नाम-ख्‍युबुगी पाहनाल।the slime of slugs.

search for the life spirit of a sick person by meditation—पोहम्‍बो नेहपागी ला ख्‍योङज्‍येला पा़हनाङला डोएँ।The shaman goes into a state of concentrated meditation to bring back the life spirit of the sick person.
preparation of beer (prepare cooked grains for the fermenting process)
wedding feast—तिहरीङ पा़हमाला डो कोहएँ।Today we have to go to a wedding feast.
give s.b. a turn—ङाहला ख्‍येगी पाहर लाङ!Give me your turn!—छ्युदाला मिह च्‍यी़ ङ्यी़गी पाहर तेर्कें, च्‍यी़ ङ्यी़गी मेहतेर्कें।At the mill some people give their turn to someone else and some don't.
make abrupt movements (e.g. movements while sleeping)
be best man/ bridesmaid at a wedding

glue (made from animal skins)—को़बा काम्‍बु य़ेदी पिन्‍ज्‍यु ज़ोएँ।Glue is made by cooking dry animal skins.—दि पिन्‍ज्‍यु डी़सीन दु, ता़हनी ङ्या़ह ज़ुङगु दु।This glue is o.k. now; it is sticky enough.
transport s.th. from one place to another—छ्यु पिहन्‍गेन/ पिहङगेन।fetch water.—स्‍यिङ, ली़ह पिहन्‍गेन/ पिहङगेन।transport wood, manure.
1bury s.th. (properly)—पेहजा ल़ेनु स्‍यिसीमाना साला पिहप्‍कें।When small babies die they are buried in the ground.
fife—खोगी लेमु पी़–पी़ म़ेदी फुउ दु।He fifes the bamboo flute.
appearance (the whole appearance of a person)
go into business partnership (with equal shares)—छोङ पी़मा पेहती छोङरो स्‍य़ूह फान्‍ज्‍योला छि्यसाल नाहङसाल पेह कोहएँ।If you go into business partnership, you must be honest.
suffix to form ordinal numbers—च्‍यिपुदी याहर तोङ, ङि्यपुदी माहर तोङ, सुम्‍बुदी फार तोङ!Send the first (person) upwards, the second downwards, and the third one over there!

mustard—यार्का पुङगा ताप्‍कें, ओहले कातीक माङसीरले फार तुहएँ, ओहले मेलला ख्‍येर्ती नुम तेन्‍गे।Mustard is sown during the rainy season, gathered in in October to November, and then taken to the mill to press the oil out.
metal urn with bent spout—पुहम्‍बा पिताल, ङुल, साहङमागी ज़ोएँ।Ceremonial urns are made of brass, silver, or copper.
asthma—खोला प़ूहने नाहजा गोसीन।He has asthma.
thin out (seedlings)—तिहरीङ ओहराङगी छ़ेमा पेल कोहएँ।Today we have to thin out the spinach patch.
hide—ओ़ह क्‍याहल्‍बुगी फेकागी फुदुङ रिहङबु रिहङबु नाहङला ज़्‍यु-दाङ दा प़ेहदी येहबा दु।It turned out that that king had hidden a bow and arrows in the long sleeves of his robe.
be about to—खो डोज्‍ये पेहकु दु।He is about to go.—ङाह याहम्‍बीज्‍यीगी मिहम्‍बा रिहल्‍ज्‍ये पेहक्‍यो।I almost fell over.
do—ता़हनी ङि्यहगी च्‍यि पेहतोङ माएँ?What is your advice, what shall we do now?
use s.th—दि ख्‍या ङ्यी़ सु पेहकें?Who of you two wants to use this? (e.g. a piece of clothing).
shaman who does not make blood sacrifices
burning sensation (of cold limbs warming up)—काङबा पेहर्कु दु।Oh, my feet are burning!
tall tree with leaves similar to holly leaves
leader—ओहले आबागी पोहमोला च्‍याहङ पोम्‍बो छ्युक्‍पु मिहला तेर दु लो।And the the father gave his daughter to a rich fellow, the leader in the north.
1chaff—तुहङग्‍योगी ऩेह योहल्‍दी पोला क्‍युर्कें नाह।I am winnowing the threshed wheat to separate it from the chaff.
{make} a firm lump of dough from '³tsamba'
near—छि्यमीलेगी खावा-लाह पोहलाराङ थोङगु दु।In TÐhimi you get a close-up view of the snow mountains.
{be} a companion to the shaman—पोहम्‍बो ङ्याम्‍बु पोहम्‍डोल पेहती डोएँ।He is the companion of the shaman.
shaman—दि पोहम्‍बो तिक्‍पु दु।This shaman forecasts accurately.
goiter {grow}—ज्‍यिङबाला पोहवा तोबा छि्यम्‍बु, बोगेराङ दु!She has quite a big goiter, it's bulging out!
pour liquids (from one container into another)—दि साहङबुङगी छ्यु छ्यु-साहङला प्‍यो कोहएँ।We must pour the water from the water carrying pot into the water storage vessel.

give a pretext—मोह ला़ मा़हखेबा म़ेदी प्रा़ ग्‍युर्कु दु।She pretends that she was unwell.—खोगी छ्य़े ओ़ कोहच्‍ये येहबा म़ेदी प्रा़ ग्‍युर्कु दु।He gives the pretext that he has to cut cattle fodder.—खो ओहङ माहलाहम्‍बागी छ्य़ेला प्रा़ ग्‍युर्कें नाह।Well, because he doesn't feel like coming he takes the cutting of cattle fodder as a pretext.
meddling in other people's talk and affairs—मोहगी ल़ेहराङ प्रा च्‍योल्‍बु पेहकु दु!All she does is meddling in other people's affairs!
{install} rope-ladder—प्राङ च़ूदी प्राहङजाङगी प्राहङ तेन्‍गे।You install a rope-ladder to get the honey of the wild bees.
slide along on one's stomach (e.g. babies)
Baruwa (Tamang village on the western slope of the upper Indrawati valley, below →yalsa)
cream (on milk)—ओहमागी प्रिगा गुरीइ साहसीन दुबा!Oh, the cat has eaten up the cream!
small settlement below Kharka (inhabited by Magars)
fall down (e.g. wilted flowers, dry leaves from trees, etc.)
langur monkey—रिह नाहङला प्रेकेन फा़ज्‍यीक (च्‍यी़) दु।There is only one langur monkey in the forest.
devour greedily—पेहजाॱयागी तो या़हबु प्रेन्‍सीन, ओहले चेम चे काहल्‍दी!The children devoured the food, and then they went to play!

stick (walking stick, and smallish sticks)—दाङ तिहरीङनी मेहमे प्रेहका च़ूदी डो कोहएँ।Nowadays grandfather has to use a stick for walking.—माहइ ताज्‍येला प्रेहका येह।I have a stick for watching the buffalo.
Salmi (village east of Sermathang, facing Ta-ohngsa)
surface (e.g. foods on liquids)—तिहरीङ ताहरा स्‍याङगेमु स्‍योह प्रोहङदी ओहङदी!When I was churning the buttermilk the curds came up to the surface.
Bhwachosa (settlement on the western slope of the Melamchi River valley, opposite of Phaadungdung Chhyu, above Kwah-Kahng
leader—ओहले आबागी पोहमोला च्‍याहङ पोम्‍बो छ्युक्‍पु मिहला तेर दु लो।And the the father gave his daughter to a rich fellow, the leader in the north.

goiter {grow}—ज्‍यिङबाला पोहवा तोबा छि्यम्‍बु, बोगेराङ दु!She has quite a big goiter, it's bulging out!
pour liquids (from one container into another)—दि साहङबुङगी छ्यु छ्यु-साहङला प्‍यो कोहएँ।We must pour the water from the water carrying pot into the water storage vessel.

give a pretext—मोह ला़ मा़हखेबा म़ेदी प्रा़ ग्‍युर्कु दु।She pretends that she was unwell.—खोगी छ्य़े ओ़ कोहच्‍ये येहबा म़ेदी प्रा़ ग्‍युर्कु दु।He gives the pretext that he has to cut cattle fodder.—खो ओहङ माहलाहम्‍बागी छ्य़ेला प्रा़ ग्‍युर्कें नाह।Well, because he doesn't feel like coming he takes the cutting of cattle fodder as a pretext.
meddling in other people's talk and affairs—मोहगी ल़ेहराङ प्रा च्‍योल्‍बु पेहकु दु!All she does is meddling in other people's affairs!
{install} rope-ladder—प्राङ च़ूदी प्राहङजाङगी प्राहङ तेन्‍गे।You install a rope-ladder to get the honey of the wild bees.
slide along on one's stomach (e.g. babies)
Baruwa (Tamang village on the western slope of the upper Indrawati valley, below →yalsa)
cream (on milk)—ओहमागी प्रिगा गुरीइ साहसीन दुबा!Oh, the cat has eaten up the cream!
small settlement below Kharka (inhabited by Magars)
fall down (e.g. wilted flowers, dry leaves from trees, etc.)
langur monkey—रिह नाहङला प्रेकेन फा़ज्‍यीक (च्‍यी़) दु।There is only one langur monkey in the forest.
devour greedily—पेहजाॱयागी तो या़हबु प्रेन्‍सीन, ओहले चेम चे काहल्‍दी!The children devoured the food, and then they went to play!

stick (walking stick, and smallish sticks)—दाङ तिहरीङनी मेहमे प्रेहका च़ूदी डो कोहएँ।Nowadays grandfather has to use a stick for walking.—माहइ ताज्‍येला प्रेहका येह।I have a stick for watching the buffalo.
Salmi (village east of Sermathang, facing Ta-ohngsa)
surface (e.g. foods on liquids)—तिहरीङ ताहरा स्‍याङगेमु स्‍योह प्रोहङदी ओहङदी!When I was churning the buttermilk the curds came up to the surface.
Bhwachosa (settlement on the western slope of the Melamchi River valley, opposite of Phaadungdung Chhyu, above Kwah-Kahng
father—दि पेहजागी फा का़हन्‍दी यिहम्‍बा, माहगी तेन कोहएँ!The mother has to tell us who the father of this child is!
large wild pig or domesticated pig
small settlement across the stream after Kayn Kahng on the path to Langgyapsa
perform all sorts of magic rituals (in order to cure sickness)
get the measles—ओहजेला ङाहला फागे थेन्‍दी येहकें।At that time I had the measles.
throw away—लामागीॱआङ सोहर फाङगे, पोहम्‍बोगीॱआङ सोहर फाङगे।Both the lama and the shaman throw out the offering called 'üsohr'.
a little way over there—ओहले खो काहल्‍दे फाच्‍ची लेप दु लो।And he went and arrived a little bit further over there.

only one—रिह नाहङला प्रेकेन फा़ज्‍यीक (च्‍यी़) दु।There is only one langur monkey in the forest.—दि सु ङ्याम्‍बुॱआङ मिहथीन्‍गे, ख्‍येराङ फा़ज्‍यीक च्‍यी़ॱराङ तेह!You are not getting along with anybody, so stay by yourself!
{take off/ pull out} s.th. carelessly and leave it disorderly
going to the other side—ओहले फान्‍डो छुन्‍डो खुङगी बातार तेर्कें।And on the way over there and on the way back they give us a meal.
{add} fermenting agent (when making beer)—तो ल्‍हा़माला फाप क्‍याहप ज़्‍या़गे।I will add some fermenting agent to the rice which is left over.
lose (game, struggle)—तिहरीङ ङाह ता़सला फाम्‍दी!Today I lost when we were playing cards!
parents—ङाहगी फामा का़हन्‍दी यिहम्‍बा, तेन्‍दोङ!Show who my parents are! (An illegitimate son might say this when his inheritance is in jeopardy).
former home of a married woman—नामा ज़ुम्‍दी ख्‍योङगेन, ओहले तिङलेॱगा फामीन टोल डोएँ नाहले!You capture the bride and bring her home, and only after that you go and pacify her family.
homeland—ता़हनी क्‍येसा फायुला लो़ह डोएँ।Now I am going back to my birth place in my homeland.

original_word: फार फार फिर फिर छ्योङदी डो-
pronunciation: ⁴phar ⁴phar ⁴phir ⁴phir ³tɕhoŋ-di ²ɖo-

english_meaning: 1about at—नुहप फ़ेले फार।about at midnight.—तिन बाज्‍येले फार।
small silver-lined wooden bowl (used for drinking)
a certain person/ place (an undefined entity)
inheritance (referring primarily to fields)—आबागी फास्‍यी माहङबु येहबा!My father has many fields (to pass down to us).
intensifier for '³kyur ¹ʑaa-, ³ɕor-'—आमागी ओ़ह पेहजाला फि क्‍युर ज़्‍या़ दु!The mother abandoned this child!—कुमेनला सान्‍दी मिहजुङना फि स्‍योर्कें!If the thief is not grabbed firmly he will escape!
be late—ङाह याहम्‍बी फिसीन।I am a little late.
be too late—ता़हनी फिसीन।It's too late now.
inside out (of clothes, etc.)—दि फि–लोक्‍पा केहन्‍ग्‍यो दुबा।You put this on inside-out.
beer of which a small portion has been offered to the house god—छ्येक्‍योङ दावा ट़ूह ट़ूहला फिच्‍याङ तोर कोहएँ।The house god has to be sprinkled with ritually clean beer every six months.
kidney—फिवा ङ्यी़ येहबा।There are two kidneys.
wipe—लाहक्‍पा फी़दोङ!Wipe your hands!—काहमला थाल्‍बी दु, फी़दोङ!Wipe the chest!
cave—फुक्‍पा/ फ़ूगी खा।entrance/ mouth of cave.
come to harm—फुङसुम फो़गे लोहला कुरीम मेहबेसीमाना ङि्यहला फुङडो।If we don't perform a ritual the year you are under a bad star (bad horoscope), we will probably come to harm.
Dhumsisa, Thulodhunga (village on the western slope of the Melamchi River valley, opposite Nuhrpu-Kahng)
big copper pot—फुङस्‍यीला छ्याङ तेन्‍ज्‍येगी दुर ल़ूएँ।Into the big copper pot we put the grain mash for making whisky.
fly—लाहला ल्‍हा नाहम्‍गाला फुर्के।Some gods fly around in the sky.
fountain pen—ख्‍येगी दि फुल–डिनला काहजे तेरक्‍यो?How much did you pay for this fountain pen?
1container used to measure liquids and things measured by volume (e.g. butter; 7.5 dl)
half—ङाहगी दोमाङ फेचे टा़हदी येहकेन।I had read about half of the book called 'ýdomaÊ'.
open—कोहल्‍दाङ फ़ेदोङ!Please open the gate!  (i.e., draw back the bars).

intensifier for '⁴taŋ-, ³tor-, ⁴ʈam-, ³kyur-, ³kyur ¹ʑaa-, ³kyur ⁴taŋ-, ³thal-, ³ɕor-'—स्‍यो़हले खो माहर फे थाल्‍दे, खुङनी कुहङस़ेला लेप्‍ती नाहले!He started going down quickly in the morning, but they arrived there only in the evening!
{put on} robe with wide sleeves for masked dancing
thread or fabric made of forest nettle fibres—फेङगागी फाजी, को़ट, ओहले फोङस्‍यो ल़ेहमु ज़ोएँ।Of the forest nettle fabric sacks, coats, and jackets and other such things are made.
intensifier for '¹ŋahl-, ¹tuhkpu ¹ŋyuhŋ-, ¹teh-k' (with the sense of 'stay on'), '²sah-' (eat a lot)
one and a half—फेदाङ सुम।two and a half.—फेदाङ ज़्‍यि।three and a half.—ङि्यहमा फेदाङ ङ्यी़।one and a half days.—चाम्‍बा काप फेदाङ ङा ल़ू कोहएँ।You must take four and a half cups of flour.
male group—ख्‍या फ़ेबे(ॱया)।you (male group).—खुङ फ़ेबे(ॱया)।they (male group).—ङि्यह फ़ेबे(ॱया)।we (excl.).—ओहराङ फ़ेबे(ॱया)।we (incl.).—ऊह फ़ेबे(ॱया)।we (incl.).
dough of parched flour, butter and sugar
shout—फ़ेरा तेन्‍दी लुहङदेन तेर्केन।teach with a loud voice.—फ़ेरा लाङदेराङ जिङगेन।fight with shouting.
1increase (natural increase of people, animals, field produce)
spill over (used for liquids)—छ्युनी स्‍योहङ स्‍योहङ पेहती फोउ दु।Oh, splashing back and forth the water is spilling over.
take a squatting position—साला (फो) छोगे तेह!Squat on the ground!
sit in groups—मिह माहङबु जोम्‍गेन ग्‍या़ला मिह राहङ राहङ फो–फो पेहती तेहकें।Where many people meet together each one sits in his own group.

intensifier for '¹kyaah- ⁴ter-, ²lehn-, ²tahm-, ¹dzaa- (leak and climb) ¹ɕuuh-, ⁴ʈam-, ¹ʈehmba - ¹tɕeh-k, ²dzol-, ³see-, ¹meh - ³se-k, ¹meh/ ¹mih - ³ɕi-g, ³thal-, ⁴then-, ³kyur ¹ʑaa-, ²ruhl-, ³tshii-, ²gam-'—ङाहगी टाङगा फोक क्‍या़हदी!I squandered my money just like that!
lump (of earth, cooked rice, etc.)—तो फोल्‍दोङ च्‍यी़ साहएँ।I will eat one lump of rice.—सासा(गी) फोल्‍दोङ।lump of earth.
help the soul leave the body (a ritual performed shortly after the passing away of a person)
go without paying attention to anybody
go without looking left or right
kick (of animals)—ताबुगी मिहला फ्रा़ क्‍याहप्‍कें।Horses kick people.
scratch up soil (with fingers or paws)—च्‍याहमुगी साला माहङबु फ्रेकेन।Hens scratch up the soil a lot.
snatch away—तोल-स्‍यु ज्‍यिबा! मिहला ज्‍योरक्‍योदी फ्रो़ ओहङगु दु!What a rascal! He comes and snatches away what somebody else received!
turn up unexpectedly—मोहनी फ्‍लेक फ्‍लेक फ्‍लेक लेप्‍ती तेहकु दु।She keeps turning up unexpectedly.
spill over (used for liquids)—छ्युनी स्‍योहङ स्‍योहङ पेहती फोउ दु।Oh, splashing back and forth the water is spilling over.
take a squatting position—साला (फो) छोगे तेह!Squat on the ground!
sit in groups—मिह माहङबु जोम्‍गेन ग्‍या़ला मिह राहङ राहङ फो–फो पेहती तेहकें।Where many people meet together each one sits in his own group.

go without paying attention to anybody
go without looking left or right
kick (of animals)—ताबुगी मिहला फ्रा़ क्‍याहप्‍कें।Horses kick people.
scratch up soil (with fingers or paws)—च्‍याहमुगी साला माहङबु फ्रेकेन।Hens scratch up the soil a lot.
snatch away—तोल-स्‍यु ज्‍यिबा! मिहला ज्‍योरक्‍योदी फ्रो़ ओहङगु दु!What a rascal! He comes and snatches away what somebody else received!

all sorts of insects and worms—नाम्‍गाङ च्‍य़ेङाला सा कोज्‍ये मेहयोहङगे, बा़-बुॱया सेकें।औंसी (र) पूर्णिमामा माटो खन्‍नु हुँदैन, खन्‍यौं भने किरा-फट्याङग्राहरू मार्छौं।ʷe must not diɡ up the fields at new moon and full moon timeʟ otherwise we will kill worms and other insects.
roughly woven sheet of mountain bamboo—बाक था़दे ख्‍यिम ज़ो कोहके।We had to weave bamboo sheets to make a shelter.
temple and monastery east of Yangri-Kahng
hard ground (stamped earth, inside or outside of house)—बाङला माहतेह, तेङगोला तेह!Don't sit on the bare floor, sit on the mat!
cone-shaped copper bowl—थो़स्‍यो नेक्‍यीदीला बाटा माएँ, ओ़हदीला छ्यु टाहङमु ल़ूएँ।The top vessel (of the destilling device) is called bowl; cold water is put into it.
{sing} middle piece in a song for dancing
meal at a feast or ceremony—बातार तेर्केन।serve a special meal.—बातार साहगेन।eat a special meal.
lamp (ordinary, i.e. not used for religious purposes)

{perform} a masked dance—ओहले कोहम्‍बा छार्तीले छ्या़नेला येहपाराङ छी़ज्‍यु बा़न्‍ज्‍याम क्‍य़ेहबाज्‍यी नाङसीन लो।When the temple was finished and dedicated, they performed an extravagant masked dance.—बा़न्‍ज्‍याम किन्‍ज्‍याम नाङगेन मिहगी फेका नोम्‍गे।The masked dancers put on a costume called 'ÿpheka'. (A long robe with wide sleeves.)
befall (fate)—ङाहला तुहक्‍पु बाप्‍सीन।Trouble befell me.—ओहराङला किन्‍ज्‍योगी का बाप्‍सीन।We received God's word.
1{make} deep-fried pancakes made of rice flour (mainly for '²lohsar')
hand over responsibility—ङाहगी राहङसा पोहम्‍बोला बा़र केन ज़्‍या़दे डो कोहके नाहले।I must hand over the responsibility to another shaman before I go (to India).
equal—मिह छि्यम्‍बुला क़े क्‍याहप्‍ते, “निसाब त़े लाङ, या़हबु पेहते बारा–बारा त़े लाङ!” मा कोहएँ।Leaders must be called, and you must tell them, "Please judge our case, please do it in a fair way!"
rich—ख्‍येनी बारोगी छ्युङमा यिहम्‍बा!You are the wife of a wealthy man!
India, i.e., mainly northern India (and other countries in that general region)—माहरला बार्माला ङि्यह आहन्‍डा पेहते तेह तेहकें, आज्‍यी।Down there in northern India we live like this, sister.
{throw away} small statues made of millet flour—काङसु ताङगेन बेले लामागी बालीङ फाङगे।During the ritual performed to placate the house god the lama throws away millet flour statues.
high altitude bush with small flowers varying from pink to white
sink (e.g. in water)—तोह छ्युला बिङदे!The stone sank in the water!
{blow} mouth organ—बियुल क्‍येप–ख्‍योवागीॱआङ फुएँ, पी़हमीगीॱआङ फुएँ।The mouth organ is played by both, the men and the women.
1worm or insect (usually refers to crawling insects)—बु क्‍याहप्‍केन।
rice when being planted—तिहरीङ खुङगी बु च़ूगे लो!They said that today the will plant rice.
sprout (from cuttings, not from seeds)—स्‍या बी़गेन।there is flesh (or skin) growing.
pendant earrings {put on}—बु-टिलला यु ल़ूगे।The pendant earrings have turquoise stones inlaid.
{have} a stye (small boil near the eye)
{make} a knot in cloth—गार्क्‍याङ बुतुर क्‍याहप्‍केन।knot together a cloth making a bundle.
Boldir (a small settlement on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley, below Langgyapsa and ʈasyi-Thang)
{give/ make} a donation—बुल्‍गा फुल्‍गेन माएँदी, राहङला येहती फुल्‍गे।As to making donations, you give as much as you are able to give.
sprout (from cuttings, not from seeds)—ओहले प्रेहकाला याहल्‍गा-दाङ लोहमा ब़ूसीन दु।And then leaves and branches sprouted from the stick.

birth (used of animals)—दि तोहङस्‍यो बेद यिहम्‍बा।This cow (buffalo, etc.) has given birth to a calf for the first time.—दि बेद ङ्यी़ यिहम्‍बा।यो दोश्रो बेथ हो।This cow (buffalo, etc.) has given birth to a calf for the second time.—तोहङस्‍यो बेदकी पी़हरुfirst-born (animal)
earthen pot (used for making curds)—बाटालेगी ए़राक बेल्‍डुला ताहप्‍कें।From the bowl the whisky drips down into the earthen pot.
crawl—पिहजा बो़दी डोउ दु।Oh, the child is crawling.—नाहरे दि पिहजानी बो़ खूएँ साम्‍मा काहल दु।I see, this child is able to crawl now.

intensifier for '¹ɕuuh-, ³luu-, ³poo-, ¹kahp-, ³ral-, ¹rahl-, ⁴ta-'—ओमला ल़ूएँ, खोनी बोक ल़ू ज़्‍या़एँ।I put him into a whole, I put him there forcefully.—पोहमो नाहङला ब्‍लोक स्‍य़ूह काहल दु लो।The daughter swiftly entered the house.
witch—नाहरे तो़ले याहर बोक्‍स्‍यी ओहङना बोक्‍स्‍यीला मेहङगालगी तेर्केन!Now if from up there a witch comes I will beat her up with a glowing piece of wood!
belching out—चेल्‍बु था़ मा़हस्‍येबागी चेल्‍बु फो़ बोगे थेन दु!Not knowing how to weave properly, the sides of this carrying basket are belching out!
heaped full—तो तेहर्मा बोप बोप तेर!Give a heaped full plate of rice!
1wild growing fruit of about walnut size with big kernel—बोलोङ-स़ेह तोहङबो छि्यम्‍बु ओहङगे।The tree of the bolong-seeh fruit is big.

any wild fruit that grows on trees
follow s.b. obstinately—पेहजाॱयागी आमाला डिबु लाङ म़ेदी ज्‍यिबुरङ ब्र्याङगेन।Children follow their mother obstinately, asking for money.
intensifier for '¹tahp-'—स्‍या ङ्य़ेहदी ख्‍योङग्‍योनी, लाहमलाराङ ब्‍ल्‍याक (रोहवा) ताहप्‍ती!I bought some meat, and on the way home it slipped out and fell flat on the ground!
{collect/ give} a contribution of grain towards the expenses of a religious festival or building

fruit just formingdi ʼpaalo̤ni ʼ̤aw ʼbrelbṳla̤ʼra  sera̤ɡi prup̤ti̋This year the hailstorm tore the apples off the tree when they were just nicely forming!-दि पा़लोनी स्‍याउ ब्रेल्‍बुलाराङ सेरागी प्रुप्‍ती!
any wild fruit that grows on trees
follow s.b. obstinately—पेहजाॱयागी आमाला डिबु लाङ म़ेदी ज्‍यिबुरङ ब्र्याङगेन।Children follow their mother obstinately, asking for money.
intensifier for '¹tahp-'—स्‍या ङ्य़ेहदी ख्‍योङग्‍योनी, लाहमलाराङ ब्‍ल्‍याक (रोहवा) ताहप्‍ती!I bought some meat, and on the way home it slipped out and fell flat on the ground!
upon—साप्‍स्‍यी मा़ला च्‍यिहन्‍ज्‍यी मेहलाप दु।The blessing will not fall upon the earth.

make war—प़ेहला मा़ क्‍याहप्‍केन बेलाले क्‍याहमीॱयागी क्‍याहल्‍सीन।When there was war in Tibet the Chinese won it.—दि ल़ेहनी मा़ ओहङगेन बेलाले ज़्‍यु कुङगेन ठि काहल्‍सीन।This is just as if you curved the bows only when the war is already on. (Do things without thinking ahead.)
wound—ए देला मागा दु। च्‍यि मागा यिहम्‍बा? – टुहगुगी तुप्‍क्‍योगी मागा यिहम्‍बा।Oh, you have a wound. What happened? - It's a cut from a dagger.
Manggengoʈh (saddle and lodge settlement on the western ridge of Yohlmu, between Taa-Khor and Iiring Kahng)
non-lama—दि युहलला माङबादीराङ येहबा।There are only non-lamas in this village.
container used to measure—माना काहङ।one mana.—माना तोह।two manas.—माना सुम, आदी।three manas, etc.

kill for sacrifices—दासाइँला ल्‍हा–थेनॱयाला मा़र माहङबु क्‍याहप्‍कें।At the dasain festival a lot of animals are sacrificed to the gods at the worshipping places.—रोहङबा ठिमला पुजारीॱयागी मार ताङगे, ओहले दासेगी बेलेला जाम्‍मागीराङ मा़र क्‍याहप्‍केँ।According to the custom of the valley dwellers the priest offers blood sacrifices, and at the time of the dasain festival everybody kills animals for sacrifices.
make blood sacrifices—ओ़ह पोहम्‍बोगी मार मेहताङगे।That shaman does not make blood sacrifices.—लामागी मार ताङॱराङ मेहताङगे!The lamas will never make blood sacrifices!

place of consolation—राहङगीराङ ल़ेह नोङ फान्‍ज्‍यो सुॱलाङ मासा (लाहम/ ग्‍या़) म़ेह।When you spoiled things yourself you have no place of consolation.—मिह छे ती़हजी लेप फान्‍ज्‍यो याहङगोॱआङ मासा म़ेह, स्‍यि कोहकेन यिहम्‍बा।When the end of our life span has come, we cannot tarry even for one moment, die we must.

negative for past tenses and the imperative (i.e. for perfect aspect)—पेहजाॱयागी तेहन्‍दाला तुहङाल माहबे!Don't worry about your children!—येहकेन मिह-दाङ नोहर, काप्‍च्‍या, थाम्‍ज्‍यीला सेम मा़हछ्या!Don't remain attached to your loved ones, your possessions and clothes, don't remain attached to anything!
village in the western area of Yohlmu, on a ridge about four hours west of Taa-Khor
a little way down—टाहल म़ेहबा येहना माहच्‍ची ज़्‍य़ू!The ones who are not especially important should sit further down.
excluding—ङाह मा़हथेबाराङ ओ़हला मेहन्‍दा ङाजे येहकेन।Excluding myself there were about five people there.
1stupa or stone wall with stupa(s)—माहनी कोरा क्‍याहप कोहएँ। (योमा ङो़ले डो कोहएँ।)You must walk around the stupa. (This implies you must go on the left side; it is done to accumulate merit.)
village on the western side of the Melamchi River valley, a little distance east of Taa-Khor
the newer temple in Sermathang, upon the saddle
wink one's eye at s.b—खोगी मोहला मिहताप पेहकु दु।Oh, he is winking his eye at her.

master of ceremonies at the wedding—मिहक्‍तेनगी पा़हमाला खादा प्रा साल्‍दी ल़ूगे।On weddings the master of ceremony puts the ceremonial scarf on the couple and announces how much money has been given.
life (span)—मिहजे रिहङ – क़ेहजे थुङ!May (my) life be longer than the short life of the (new) garment. (A wish made when wearing a new garment for the first time.)
lit.: people's friend—च्‍यि केच्‍या लेह, मिहरो लु या़हबा लेहन्‍गे!It's no problem for her, she sings beautiful songs!—मिहरोगी च्‍यि या़हबा सेहसीन दु!How nicely she has recorded it!
alien (people from other places)—मिहसेर जोम्‍बे काहङला – खाम्‍साङ ऱेह-ऱेह च्‍येह स्‍यो!When gathered with people from other places -- let us have fun together!
disparage s.b—तोहङमा पी़हमीगी मी़–ङेन खोगी सेसीन!He disparaged his first wife (by getting a nicer second wife).
1node (in wood)—दि स्‍यिङला मी़ङगोर दु, स्‍या़ज्‍ये का़हबु ओहङगे।There are nodes in this wood, it's hard to split it.
big earthen pot with a ring of small holes at the bottom
white foreigner (used for m. and f.)
be suspicious of one's spouse (with or without reason)—खुङ जाब्राङ ङ्यी़ मी़सेर पेहकेन तामला जिङगु दु।The couple is quarreling because they suspect each other of unfaithfulness.
temple (body part)—याहमागी मुर्चाराङ फोङज्‍ये पेहसीन!This sinusitis makes my temples feel like bursting!

send a message—‘नाहङबार ओहङ मेहकोहएँ’ म़ेदी आमागी ङाहला म़े ताङग्‍यो।Mother sent me to tell you that you don't need to come tomorrow.—‘ख्‍ये पोहमो छ्युक्‍पु डोज्‍ये येहबा लो’ म़ेदी ङाहइ म़े चाहङगु दु म़ेदोङ!Tell him (the father) that I told you that his daughter will become rich!
cry out for help—खोगी ल्‍हा ल्‍हामुला म़े पार्कें।He cries out to the god and goddesses for help.
female spirit which lives in the woods
curse s.b—ओ़ह सोङगोराङ आज्‍यीदीगी म़ेमु क्‍याहप तेहकें मिहन्‍दु लेह!So because of that the older sister keeps cursing (them)!
{put on} shaman's costume—पोहम्‍बोगी जा़तारला डोएँँमु म़ेयोल केहन्‍दी छ्याम्‍गे।When to shaman goes to a festival he puts on his costume and dances.
full to overflowing—साहङबुङला छ्युॱआ़ मेरे ल़ू ज़्‍या़ दु।Oh, the water pot is filled to overflowing.
{give} a blessing (with visible gesture)—लोहसारला छ्येवागी च्‍युङाला मेलाम क्‍याहप्‍कें।During the new year festival the elders bless the younger ones.

negative for non-perfect aspect/ tense—टिहप्‍साला तानानी टिहप्‍सा मेहथोङ दु!When I look for my shadow, there isn't any!—काङबा साला क्‍याहप्‍ती ताना साला च्‍येहलाप म़ेहछ्या दु!When I step on the ground, I don't leave any footprints!
{shine with} a glowing piece of wood
not alike—दि छेन्‍दा मेहन्‍डावाज्‍यी दु!He/ she doesn't look at all like the others!
person (used with numbers)—मेहन्‍दा/ मिहर्कु ङा।five people.
grandfather—मेहमे रिहम्‍बुर-छ्य़े।title for a well-learned lama.
1family (also used for a group of animals)—ख्‍या म़ेहमेॱया काहजे येहबा?How many are there in your family?—ल़ूह/ माहइ म़ेहमेॱया।भेडाहरू/ भैँसीहरू।ɡroup of sheepʔ buffaloes.
blowing pipe (for fire)—मेह मेहस्‍योगी फुगेन।blow the fire with a pipe.
rice or wheat grains used for making a diagnosis by fortune telling {give}
roll s.th. in some substance—या़ मार्चाला मोल्‍दे साहएँ।I am going to eat the arum roots having rolled them in ground chili.
she–मोहगी सेहसीन माक्‍योनी, आज्‍यीइ सेहॱआङ माहसेह दुबा।She said that (she) held out (the microphone), but actually elder sister hadn't.
divination ritual—मोह तिक्‍पु दु/ मोह  थी़उ दु।The divination was accurate.
disparage s.b—तोहङमा पी़हमीगी मी़–ङेन खोगी सेसीन!He disparaged his first wife (by getting a nicer second wife).

cry out for help—खोगी ल्‍हा ल्‍हामुला म़े पार्कें।He cries out to the god and goddesses for help.
female spirit which lives in the woods
curse s.b—ओ़ह सोङगोराङ आज्‍यीदीगी म़ेमु क्‍याहप तेहकें मिहन्‍दु लेह!So because of that the older sister keeps cursing (them)!

{give} a blessing (with visible gesture)—लोहसारला छ्येवागी च्‍युङाला मेलाम क्‍याहप्‍कें।During the new year festival the elders bless the younger ones.
{give} a blessing (with visible gesture)—लोहसारला छ्येवागी च्‍युङाला मेलाम क्‍याहप्‍कें।During the new year festival the elders bless the younger ones.
{give} a blessing (with visible gesture)—लोहसारला छ्येवागी च्‍युङाला मेलाम क्‍याहप्‍कें।During the new year festival the elders bless the younger ones.

roll s.th. in some substance—या़ मार्चाला मोल्‍दे साहएँ।I am going to eat the arum roots having rolled them in ground chili.

divination ritual—मोह तिक्‍पु दु/ मोह  थी़उ दु।The divination was accurate.
divination ritual—मोह तिक्‍पु दु/ मोह  थी़उ दु।The divination was accurate.
she–मोहगी सेहसीन माक्‍योनी, आज्‍यीइ सेहॱआङ माहसेह दुबा।She said that (she) held out (the microphone), but actually elder sister hadn't.
take it!—या, थुङ!Take and drink it!
tarnish—साहङमागी या ङुम्‍बु ओहङगे।The tarnish on copper is greenish.
stag (?)—दि यागी को़बा यिहम्‍बा।This is the skin of big stag.
intimidating—लेहम च्‍यी़ ओ़ह मिहङग्‍युर काहपुला रोप मा़हखुनानी या!Once I am surely going to beat up this fellow called MihÊgyur!
itch—ङाहला याउ दु।Oh, I feel itchy.—ङाह लाहक्‍पा य़ेदी!Oh, my hand is so itchy!
flit back and forth—खो मिह पोहले फार याक याक युक युक पेहकु दु।He flits back and forth in front of the people.

become/ make responsible for next year's festival—साङबे छी़ज्‍युगी याङजी आ पासाङगी खुर्सीन।Brother Pasang has become responsible for next year's 'þtshiiÑu' festival.—युहल्‍बा-दाङ युहलगी गोबाॱया कोहम्‍बाला ओहङगे, ओहले युहल–ठिमगी हिसापकी साङबे छी़ज्‍युगी याङजी काल्‍गे।The villagers and the village leaders assemble in the temple, and make somebody responsible for next year's 'þtshiiÑu' festival.
grain which has not been threshed successfully (because it was moist)—तिहरीङ ङाहगी क्‍याहगार याङजी तुहङसीन।Today I threshed the millet grains which had to be dried again.
cattle shed settlement north of Yangri Kahng, just below Kahngdza Lah
Yangri Khola (the river east of Yangri Kahng)
pasture above Chhimi on the high ridge between Sermathang and Lha-Kahng Ghyang

borrow (belongings, returning the very same item, not the same in kind)—ङाह ख्‍या साला तो़जी च्‍यी़ यार ओहङग्‍यो।I have come to borrow a small hoe from you.—ङाहगी तो़जी च्‍यी़ यार ख्‍योङसीन।I borrowed a small hoe.
apply dabs of butter to s.b. or s.th
1cow or buffalo which has calved the previous year, but not yet this year
little patches here and there—नामला मुक्‍पा याला युले दु।There are some small clouds in the sky.
female forest spirit—मोहलानी —याल्‍मुगी ख्‍येर्सीन दु’ म़ेदी थेन्‍सीन।The divination ritual revealed that the female forest spirit had captured him.
small settlement between Tehn-Thang and Kayn Kahng
none at all—पी़हमी खोलानी या़ह म़ेहबा!He has no wife at all!

prominent, emphatic focus—दि पालीपनी लिहक लिहक पेहकु दु, रोहम्‍डो याहङ!यो छाप्रो त हल्‍लिँदो रहेछ, भत्‍केला नि!—काप्‍च्‍यानी केहन्‍गे याहङ रो़ह!—ङाह खुस्‍यी याहङ, तिहक्‍यानी!Of course we will dress up, friend!ah ʼkhṳi yah , tihkya̤ni̋I am just happy where I am, these days!

double conjunction expressing wavering—याहङनी साहएँ ल़ेहमु, याहङनी मेहसाहएँ ल़ेहमु, साहएँ यिहना तोक तोक सोह, मेहसाहएँ यिहना क्‍युर ज़्‍यो़!Why are you messing around with this food? If you want it, eat it up nicely, if you don't want it, then leave it alone!
a little way upwards—याहच्‍ची याहच्‍ची ज्‍य़ू!Move up, please! (A polite invitation to take the good places.)—ओहले याहच्‍चीला ख्‍यिम च्‍यी़ दु लो।And a little bit further up there was a house.
top beam of a window or door frame
going upward—याहन्‍डोनी रो़ह क्‍याहक्‍चेराङ ज्‍योर्सीन।On the way up I had a lot of companions.
springtime—याहन्‍डो रिह ङुम्‍बु डोएँ।In springtime the landscape becomes green.
porch (covered, with or without wooden railing)
sinusitis—खोला याहमा नाहजा दु।He has got a sinusitis.—खोगी याहमा टा़हसीन।His is healed of his sinusitis.
a moment/ somewhat—याहम्‍बी देला क़ूहदोङ लोह।Wait a moment here.—ल़ेह याहम्‍बी लोपॱसे पेहती पेहतोङ!Work somewhat slower!—तिहरीङ याहम्‍बी ल्‍हाक्‍पु ल़ू!Give me somewhat more rice today!
up—प्‍लास्‍य्‍टार स्‍यी़दीले माहरले याहरकी यिहगी ताङगे।When the cast has been taken off, we will send you a letter from down here up to (where you are).
become prosperous—ख्‍ये नाहमॱआङ याहरक्‍यी ओहङज्‍ये मिहन्‍दु!You will never become prosperous!
branch—तोहङबोगी याहल्‍गाॱया क़ूहसीन दु।Oh I see, the branches of the tree are hanging down.
unripe (only used for fruit, not for vegetables or grains)—खाम्‍बुनी ताहन्‍दा यिन्‍ज्‍युराङ दु।The peaches are still green.
hide—मिहला यिप्‍ती ज़्‍या़गे, पेलादी प़ेहदी ज़्‍या़एँ।For hiding people you usually use 'üyipken', and for hiding things 'ýpeehgen'.

keep bringing up s.th—ङाह ला़ मेहखाएँ बेले आदागी ताक्‍यो, ओहले नामागी, 'ओ़ह ताॱङ्यी' म़ेदी यिह था़गे।My older brother looked after me when I was unwell, and now my sister-in-law keeps reminding him of this.

suddenly—ङि्यह बा़ल छ्याङ थुङदे छो़दे तेह तेहते येहकेननी, यिहङमे थोङमेलाराङ आस्‍याङ का़मी होप लाहङदे छ्युङमादीला डाम्‍ज्‍या ङ्यी़ तेर्ते माएँ रो़ह!I tell you, we were sitting together in a group, drinking beer; and then suddenly uncle Kaami got up and slapped his wife on her cheeks.
since—जाम्‍बुलीङ छ्या़ॱले यिहन्‍ज्‍यु।since the world came into existence.
have power of meditation—खोला यिहन्‍देन येहबा।He has the power of meditation.
hide—प्रुहल खाङबा क्‍याहपला यिहप तेह दु लो।The snake hid behind the house.

go too far unintentionally in doing s.th—ताम मारा़ थाल्‍दी दि केच्‍या चोक्‍पा काहल्‍सीन।This matter has become awkward because somebody said the wrong thing.—क्‍याहक्‍रा़ थाल्‍दी खोगी मी़रङ डुप्‍कयो!He has become so fat that his eyes are not showing anymore!

and—च्‍याह–र्‍हिल राङगा माइदा ताङ चोहङ!Send us a cake of tea leaves and some white flour!—ओहले आबागी ओ़ह च्‍यिहबी माहर्मु राङ ङुम्‍बु ङ्यी़ ख्‍येर दु लो।And then the father took the red and the blue bird along with him.
half naked—ख्‍या़बुला च्‍यि राङगाल रोङगोल ड़े तेहकें यिहम्‍बा?Why are you walking around half-naked in this cold weather?
smash/ get smashed (breakable things like crockery)

perform an activity excessively—मोहगी बार्ता लाङगेन्‍दी ता़हनी माहङ रा़़हसीन!Her scolding has become too much (to bear) now!—ओहले गिटी तुहङ रा़हदी लाहक्‍पालेगी ठा़ थी़बा थेन्‍गे!And breaking up so many stones for gravel your hands will start bleeding!
strap used to carry loads on the back
sponsor a special festival—आस्‍याङगी ज़्‍यिनीङ लोहला स्‍युहङगान-च्‍येला राहङज्‍या छी़ज्‍यु ताङग्‍यो।The year before last year my maternal uncle sponsored a 'tshiiÑu festival in Syuhnggan-Che.
selfishness—राहङदेनगी साम्‍लो ताङगेन।have selfish intentions.—राहङदेन ओहङगेन्‍गी ल़ेह पेहकेन।स्‍वार्थी काम गर्नु।work only for oneʼs own interests.
become aggressive—ङाहगीनी च्‍यिॱआङ मा़हमेबाला खोराङ राहङदोङ क्‍य़ेदी ङाह ङ्याम्‍बु जिङ ओहङ दुबा।I hadn't said anything; he became agressive just like that and started to fight with me.
on one's own (gift developed without teaching)—पोहम्‍बो राहङस्‍यीङ थेन्‍गेन।become shaman on one's own.
uneven in length and not very neat (of things hanging)—याहप्‍सा काहङ काप्‍च्‍या राहना रिहने च्‍याङ ज़्‍या़ दु।All over the porch there are clothes hanging in a disorderly fashion.
wrestle—पेहजाॱया रिहला रा़हमा क्‍याहप्‍केन।The children wrestle with each other in the forest.
blanch—तीतो कारीलला राहम्‍जी क्‍याहप्‍ती प़्रेह याहल्‍गेन।Titekarelas are blanched to take away some of their bitterness.
{celebrate} dedication of '²kohmba' and other religious monuments
chives—राहम्‍बा पा़ला ल़ूगे।Chives are put into curry.

big tree with soft leaves—राहम्‍स्‍याङ तोहङबो रिह क्‍याहप्‍क्‍यो ग्‍या़ला माहङबु ओहङगे, लाहॱलाङ ओहङगे, रोहङॱलाङ ओहङगे।The 'ýrahmÐaÊ' tree usually grows where there has been a landslide; it grows at higher and lower elevations.
wooden gadget carried by the god '¹dʑambe-⁴yaŋ'—ज्‍याम्‍बे–याङगी लाहक्‍पा च्‍यी़ला राहल्‍डी थो़क्‍यो ओहङगे।The god called dÑambe-yaÊ carries a wooden gadget in one of his hands.
blossom of grass-like plants—टा राहल्‍बा रिहन रिहन।unkempt shock of hair.
seed of fleocarpus ganitries tree—मेहमे पोहम्‍बोगी रा़हस्‍या माहङबु ता़ दु!Grandfather shaman is wearing (a necklace with) a lot of rudraksha seeds!
cause to fall over—दि ल़ेहमु थाङना स्‍यार्पानी मेहन्‍दा च्‍यु ओहङॱना़ ङाहइ रिल्‍गेन माएँ!I tell you, even if ten of those wretched Sherpas come, I will throw them over!
small settlement north of Phaa-Dungdung on the upper way to Langgyapsa
spirit (male, lives in the forest, teaches the shaman)
settlement on the western slope of the Melamchi River valley, about opposite of Kayn-Kahng
small settlement on the western ridge of the Yohlmu valley, below Taa-Khor
landslide site—रिहस्‍याल नाहङलाराङ राहम्‍स्‍याङ तोहङबो क्‍य़े दुबा!Oh, even on the landslide site 'ürahmÐaÊ' trees are growing!
mass of a landslide—का़हले फार रिह क्‍याहपा छ़े दु, रिहस्‍योल छि्यम्‍बु ओहङ दु!Somewhere there must have been a landslide, there is a huge mass of debris!
be near—ता़हनी ख्‍या़बु ओहङ रिहङसीन।The cold season is about to come now.
price—दिगी रिहन काहजे पेहकें?How much is the price of this?—दिदीला रिहन काहजे तेर्पा?How much did you pay for this?
settlement on the western slope of the Melamchi River valley, a little to the north of Serka-Thili

each time when performing action of vs—पेला तेन-रिम टाहङमाराङ तोर्कें।Each time he takes this utensil out (of the box) he loses it.—पेहजानी ङि्य छे-रिहम टाहङमाराङ ङुहउ दु।Each time the child wakes up he cries.
whenever performing the action of vs—क्‍या़हबेदी साह–रिहम्‍बागी तेन्‍दोङ।Take out (of the storage) the provisions as you need them.—टाङगा कोह–रिहम्‍बागी चा़ल्‍ती़गे।I will use the money as I need it.
highest rank for lama—ओ़ह च्‍याह-टाल रिहम्‍बुर-छ्य़ेगी तोहम्‍बा स्‍युहक्‍यो।That big lama called 'ütÐahÌa' devotes his life entirely to religious activities.
fall over—ङाह याहम्‍बीज्‍यीगी रिहल्‍ज्‍ये पेहक्‍यो।I almost fell over.

{make} thick soup with mashed potato dumplings—हे य़ेदी पाको स्‍यी़दी तुहङदी, ओहले सोहन्‍दोर कोल्‍दी, ओहले रिहल्‍दो लाहक्‍पागी च्‍येती ल़ूगे।You boil the potatoes, peel them and mash them, and then you make a spiced broth, tear up the mash with your hands and throw the pieces into the broth.
languish away (be sick for a long time)—ङियहगी पाबा री़दी री़दी स्‍यिसीन।Our father died after having been sick for a long time.

all at once—ओहजे मिह रुबे ताहम्‍दे ख्‍येर कोहके!That many people we have to bind (i.e. make invisible to the gods) and take them along together.—पेताम रुबे रुबेराङ टेहम्‍बा च्‍येहतेनी, च्‍यि स़ेज्‍यीॱआङ म़ेहबा!I forgot all the stories, nothing is left!

unite—ङि्यह आदाॱया रुहप फान्‍ज्‍योनी सुगीॱआङ ला़ मेहखाएँ माएँ रो़ह!हाम्रा दाजुभाइहरू हूल बाँधेर आएपछि कसैले पनि उनीहरूसँग टेक लिन सक्‍तैन, है साथी!ɪ tell you, if our brothers unite, nobody will be able to stand aɡainst them̋

particle added to the first syllable of clan names to make them abusive—ओहले ङि्यह च्‍याहबा यिहन्‍गे सोङगोराङ ङि्यहला च्‍याह–रुहल माएँ!And because we are of the 'ütÐahba' clan, they call us rotten birds!
intensifier for '³tup-, ¹praah-, ³tɕe-k, ¹tɕhe-k, ¹muhr-'
one each—खाङबा टाहङमाराङ लुवाङ ऱेह-ऱेह ओहङगे।Every house has one spirit enclosure.

some—ओहजे बेलालाराङ मिह रेहङगागी हाल्‍ला पेहसीन।Just at that time some people started a commotion.—रेहङगानी येहन्‍डाराङॱआङ छि्यम्‍बु छ्य़ेन्‍दो तेन्‍गे नाहले!Some people are arrogant without any reason!

be near—ता़हनी खो ओहङ रेहन्‍ग्‍यो यिहम्‍बा।He must be about to come now.—दि हे क्‍ये रिहन्‍ग्‍यो यिहम्‍बा, नाम माहक्‍याहप्‍पागी क्‍ये मा़हखु।These potatoes are about to sprout, but because there was no rain they have not quite made it yet.
soya beans—रेहन्‍जा रोलीगी तुहङगे।Soya beans are threshed with a piece of wood.
excrements of goats, sheep and forest animals

some—रेहरे मिहनी आहन्‍डाराङॱआङ स्‍यिहताङ लाहङगेन।Some people get angry for nothing.—रेहरे काहक्‍काला नेपा चोक्‍पा क्‍याहप्‍क्‍यो ओहङगेन।Some (people) are possessed by a very evil spirit of sickness.—रेहरे सुर च्‍यी़गीॱआङ डोएँ।Some (spirits of sickness) just leave when you give them a food offering.
rope for tying up animals—रेहल जो़बुरला केन्‍गे।The rope for tying up the cattle is slipped over a pole.
bear fruit (trees)—ताहलेननी स्‍याउ रो़ॱराङ मा़हरो।This year there were no apples at all.

gradual increase in the stated quality or quantity—सेम्‍बा ज़्‍येन्‍डो ज़्‍येन्‍डो - ली़हबु केहन्‍डो केहन्‍डो!The older the body becomes - the younger the heart!—दि क्‍यिबुनी ङि्यहमा टाहङमाराङ चोक्‍टो चोक्‍टो डोउ दु!This dog gets meaner every day!—ङि्यहमा टाहङमाराङ छ्येरो छ्येरो काहल्‍दी ओहङसीन।They became bigger each day.—स्‍युगु माहङबु ज़ोक्‍यो सोङगोराङ स्‍युगु तुहङबुॱया ङ्युहङरो ङ्युहङरो काहल दु।Because much paper was made, the paper bush has gradually decreased.
threshing stick—कोसा छ्याप्‍क्‍याराङ काम्‍ग्‍योगी ताम्रा रोलीगी तुहङगे।Beans dried in the pods are threshed with a piece of wood.

help with the activity expressed by the preceding stem—ङाहला पेलाॱया रूरो़ह पेह लाङ!Help me gather up these things!—दि खुर्पुॱया याहम्‍बी क्‍या़रूह पेहतोङ!Please help lift these loads!—ख्‍ये ल़ेह म़ेहना दि चेम्‍बुॱया स्‍यी़रूह पेहतोङ!If you have nothing to do, please help undoing these seems!
beat—मेहमेगी पेहजाॱयाला रोहल्‍दी!Grandfather beat up the children!
alike—स्‍य़ेज्‍ये रोहवा लुज्‍यी लेहन कोहच्‍ये दुबा।We must sing a song which we know.
a brief moment—र्‍हाप र्‍हिप क़ूहदोङ लेह।Please wait just a little moment.
a brief moment—मेहमेला, “र्‍हिपचेज्‍यी फेप्‍तोङ” म़ेदी सुङ नाङदोङ!Please tell grandfather to come just for a little moment!
intensfier for '¹ŋyahl, ³ŋyi ²looh-'—थाङ छ्येती ओहङग्‍यो यिहम्‍बा छ़े दु, खो र्‍हिले ङ्याहल्‍सीन।'I see, he was tired when he came, he lied down right away.
round (of ball shape)—खो गो़नी र्‍हिल्‍मु म़ेहबा, क्‍योङमो दु।His head is not round, it's quite longish (oval-shaped).

life spirit/ life substance of a person

wages—खोगी ला क्‍याहप्‍ती पेहजा म़ेहमेॱया सोएँ।He brings up his family by working on daily wages.—दावागी हिसाप मिहम्‍बादीला ला क्‍याहप्‍केन माएँ।If it is not on a monthly basis, you talk of working for wages.
according to—ख्‍येला प़ेबे ङाहगीॱआङ ल़ेह पेह ओहङ नाह!I will pay it back to you!—मिहला प़ेबेगी सो़ह!(The quality of) the merchandise matches (the quality of) the person!
person who has been working or traveling in a foreign country—लाउरे ओहङसीन!A well traveled person has come back!
step-like shelves at the rear of the fireplace
very small settlement on the eastern slope of the Melamchi River valley, near Kayn Kahng, below Sindir Kahng
{have} a skin disease—मोह(गी) तोहङबा काहङग्‍या लाङस्‍यु थेन दु।Her face is full of white patches from a skin disease.
be completely drunk—छ्याङगी लाटुलॱराङ ज़िक्‍यो यिहना खो टेहम्‍बा सिहन्‍गु दु।Even though he is completely drunk he has not lost his senses.
odd bits and pieces of flattish things (like cloth, paper, etc.)—पेहजाॱयाला काप्‍च्‍या लाप्‍टा लेप्‍टाॱया ङ्योह काहल्‍ग्‍यो।I went to buy some odd clothes for the children.
head lama's house or settlement—लामा लाब्राङ नाहङला येह।The lama is in his house.
lama (Buddhist priest)—लामाला चा़ छि्यम्‍बु पेहकें।The lamas are greatly honoured.
polite attitude—च्‍यि नोम्‍साङ ताङ नाङगो येहबा, मेहमे ला़ह?What are you thinking about, grandfather?
necklace or bracelet with beads—लाहक्‍ची बुदी।jewelry beads.
gesticulate—खोगी राहङगी लाहक्‍पा लाहदाप पेहती ताम मासीन।He talked gesticulating with his hands.
adze (used to scrape off wood splinters)—स्‍यिङ ज़्‍यार्केन ला़हदी।the adze used to scrape off wood.

get mountain sickness—ङाहनी लाहदो लाहङगे म़ेदी ज्‍यी़ॱआङ ज्‍यी़उ दु।Moreover I am afraid I might get mountain sickness.—लाहला डोएँमु लाहला मिहला लाहदो लाहङगे।Some people get mountain sickness when they go into the high hills.
pile of stones on mountain passes and cross-roads
leaf—ओहले केरा लाहप्‍ती काप्‍कु दुबा।And they make the roof of banana leaves.
feel like doing s.th—ङाह मा़हन्‍गुला तेह लाहम्‍गेन।I like to stay in Maahngu.
guide—ङि्यहला लाहम्‍ग्‍यी पेह रो़ह!Be our guide, friend!
Lamdo (small settlement quite high up on the western slope of the Melamchi River valley, about opposite of Timbu)
pasture above Kahng-Yuhl on the high ridge between Sermathang and Lha-Kahng Ghyang

some—लाहला मिहगी छ्याङ मिहथुङगेन।Some people don't drink alcoholic beverages.—ताम लाहला टेहम्‍बा च्‍येहसीन।I forgot some of it.—नेहपा काहल्‍दी स्‍यिज्‍ये पेहकेन बेलाले लाहलादी टेहम्‍बा सिहन्‍गे, लाहलादी मिहसीहन्‍गे।When people are sick and about to die, some are still alert to things around them, and some aren't.
receive—खुङला या़हबु ऩेक्‍यो नामा खुङगी ला़हलाराङ थेप्‍सीन।They received the daughter-in-law they liked.
thin mixture of parched flour and salt tea (or hot water)
polite response: alright, okay—लाहसो ला़ह, ता़ह देजेराङ यिहम्‍बा ला़ह!My honoured listeners, that's all now!
feed (a child)—पेहजाला लि तेर आ़।Here, feed the child!
world—पोहमो ङि्यहमा डावा – लिङस्‍यी कोर्ने ज्‍याल डो।A young lady looking like the sun! Let us go and walk through the world and worship.
feed s.b—नेहपाला साहज्‍ये लिन कोहउ दु।The patient has to be fed.
horoscope calendar (used by lamas)—लोह फो़उ येहबा नि म़ेदी लामाॱयागी लिहदोला ताएँ।The lamas look up the horoscope calendar to see whether you are under a bad star or not.

shake back and forth—लोमाला ब़्रेबी लिहप्‍ती ज्‍याम्‍बु क्‍योहङबु खा फ़ेएँ।Shaking the rice flour back and forth in the bamboo tray you separate the coarsely ground flour from the finely ground flour.
brass urn with very narrow neck

tradition—रोहङबागी लिहयुला च्‍याहमु सेती ल्‍हाला ठा़ ल़ूएँAccording to the tradition of the valley dwellers (hindus), chicken are killed, and the blood is applied to the idols.—ताहङबु च्‍यि माएँ लो मासीमाना, ओ़हदी पोहम्‍बोगी लिहउले थेन्‍दे ओहङगे।To tell you what was said formerly, we learn from the tradition of the shamans, they said.
kind of leech—पाहलाङगी नार्कोला स्‍य़ूहएँगी ली़।the leech which goes into the nostrils of cows.

lama or shaman placates the spirits of sickness and misfortune with little statues made of flour—लामाॱयागी ली़ कोहङगे, ओहले ङोक्‍यो स्‍वा़ ली़ला ल़्‌ूगे, ओहले ली़ क्‍युर्कें।The lamas form the millet flour statues, and put roasted rice grains onto them, and then they throw them out.
small settlement on the western ridge of the Yohlmu valley, below Taa-Khor
spirit residing in an object of nature

verb stem nominalizer for way of doing s.th—ङाहगी डोलुज्‍यीराङ, मिहॱया क़ेहमु क्‍याहप्‍लुज्‍यी!People have to laugh when they just see my way of walking!—खोगी तो योलु या़हबा!How well he cooks!—तेहलु डोलु।behaviour; life style.
small enclosure made of stones with a stone in the middle
have time for the activity stated in the verb stem—तिहरीङ ङाहला डो–लुहङ म़ेह।I don't have time to go today.—तिहरीङ ङि्यहला तो साह–लुहङॱआङ माहयोहङ!Today we didn't even have time to eat!
rift valley (large or small, with or without stream)
carrying rings on big metal vessels
sheep—ल़ूह/ लुह म़ेहमेॱया।a group of sheep.

from (directional)—ओहजे बेलालेनी ङाहराङ बासले थेन्‍दी लाहमगी थो़ ङो़ले डोॱराङ डोउ येहकेन।At that time I myself, having come out of the bus, was walking along it above the road.—योहल काहङले ल्‍हा़मानी सुगीॱआ़ माहतेर दु लो।Nobody gave him more than a handful.—ओहले छि्यमी कोहम्‍बागी यिल्‍दोलेगी फार डोएँमु तो योएँगी मेहङगाङला लेप्‍सीन।And going over from the courtyard of the temple in TÐhimi I arrived at the cooking shed.
trust s.b. (having experienced that he is trustworthy)—ङाहगी खुङला ल़े खाल्‍गोराङ खुङगीॱआङ ङाहला ल़े खाल्‍गेन।Because I trust them they also trust me.

trust s.b. firmly—ख्‍योवागी पी़हमीला ल़े च्‍योल कोहएँ, पी़हमीगी ख्‍योवाला ल़े च्‍योल कोहएँ, ओहलेॱगा टोहङबा स्‍यिह–तेम्‍बु ओहङगे।The husband must trust his wife, and the wife must trust her husband, and then the marriage will be strong.

wages {pay/ receive)—स्‍यिङ ट़ूहना, सा कोना, हे च़ूना ल़ेगा ज्‍योर्कें।For gathering fire wood, digging up the fields, and planting potatoes you get wages.—ङि्यहमा काहङ ल़ेह पेहती ऩेह मा़ना ङ्यी़ पेहक्‍योज्‍यीॱगा ल़ेगा!Only two maanas of wheat for a day's wages!
flattish piece of s.th—स्‍यिङ/ तोह लेप्‍टाज्‍यी।a flat piece of wood/ stone.—ओ़ह लेप्‍टादी ङाहला लाङ, ङाह तेहच्‍ये!Give me that flat thing there, I want to sit on it!
{blow} bamboo flute—लेमु क़े चाङगा दु।The bamboo flute has a high sound.
1of course I would do what the verb stem expresses
mirror which records all our works

"kill" a little statue of millet flour mash—छी़ज्‍यु बेलाले लामाॱयागी लेहङगा टाहल कोहएँ।At the 'þtshiiÑu' festival the lamas must ward off the evil spirits by performing the "ýlehÊga üÌahl-gen" ritual.
{pursue} occupation—खोदी ल़ेहज्‍यु या़हबु दु लो।He has a good occupation they said.
smearing—ना़जा लेहटे पेटे क्‍यूगेन।ink smears.—कालाम लेहटे पेटे ख्‍य़ूगेन।the ink flows plentifully in this pen.—पेहजागी लेहटे पेटे थेल्‍दी साहगेन।the child smears himself with his food.
side (of paper, path, clothing, etc.)—लेहप च्‍यी़ ङो़ले थोवा काहल दु।Oh, it is higher on one side.
slow (in doing work)—दि लाङगो लेहपुज्‍यी दु।This ox is slow.
flattened shape—पेहजा बेललाले थाम्‍ज्‍यीदीराङ ना़सुम लेहप्‍ता ओहङगे।At a young age everybody has a flat nose.

otherwise—ताम च्‍यी़ ङ्यी़ मिहम्‍बा मेहथेन्‍गे, लेहमेन ङाहगी ल़ूज्‍ये येहकें नाह, मोहला रिहक्‍पा लोप्‍च्‍ये म़ेदे।I can't say anything, except one or two phrases, otherwise I would put something (on the tape recorder) to teach her proper behaviour.—सा़ब म़ेदे खोला काहकाला छि्यम्‍बु मिह ज़़ेदे ज़्‍या़ कोहएँ, लेहमेननी ङि्यहला बार्ता लाङगे!We must call him Sir and deal with him as with an important person, otherwise he scolds us sharply.
tick—पाहलाङला लेहम्‍बागी आ़ ताप्‍कु दु।Oh, the ticks are biting the cows.
small wooden poles stuck into the ground on which to fasten the loom
non-lama clan (='⁴maŋba')—खुङ क्‍यिहपा लो यिहम्‍बा।They are of the 'ÿlo' clan.

reported information particle—ख्‍येगी पोहमो प्राङबु मिह ङि्यम्‍बु पा़हमा पेह तेर्तोङ लो, ओहले ख्‍ये पोहमो छ्युक्‍पु डोज्‍ये येहबा लो, ङाहइ सोदीगी येहन्‍डा म़े चाहङगु दुबा।He said that you should marry your daughter to a poor person, and then she would become rich; my good fortune told me to tell you this.
whooping cough—खोला लोने नाहजा येहबा।He has whooping cough.
learn—ताह–लोह ङाह टाहम्‍ङ्येन लोप्‍च्‍ये।This year I want to learn to play the string instrument.—था़ था़ज्‍ये ङाहराङ लोप्‍क्‍यो।I learnt to weave myself.
teach—पोहमोला था़ था़गेन लोप्‍तोङ!Teach your daughter to weave!—पोहमोला तोप्‍च्‍या ज़ोज्‍ये लोप्‍तोङ!Teach your daughter to cook!
note—स्‍यिका च्‍यी़गी लोर।a one rupee note.—क्‍याह–लोर च्‍यी़।a one hundred rupee note.—ङाप्‍क्‍यागी लोर च्‍यी़।a five hundred rupee note.—तोङ–लोर च्‍यी़।a one thousand rupee note.

be fond of—मिहगी मिहला लोर्चा मेहन्‍डोसीमाना दि रोहवा ताम चोक्‍पा माएँ।If people don't like somebody they say such evil things.—दि खाङबा जोप्‍ता ङाहला लोर्चा माहकाहल।I certainly don't like the design of this house.
year—बाम ज्‍यिल्‍लाला ङाह लोह च्‍यी़ तेहक्‍यो।I stayed one year in Bam Jilla.
friendly spurring on—तिहरीङ ओहराङ ङ्यी़ माङसीर च्‍य़ेङागी जा़तारला डोएँ लोह!Today let's go together to the full moon festival of (the month of) Mangsir.
side (of piece of clothing, paper, house)—लो़ह च्‍यी़ ङो़ले ल्‍ह़ेदोङ, लेहमेन लो़ह ङो़ले क्‍युर ज़्‍या़दोङ।Continue knitting only on one side now and leave the other side till later.
lama leading the singing of mantras at funeral ceremony
okay—दि ङाहइ ख्‍येर्कें लोह?– लोह/ लोहउ, ख्‍येर्तोङ।Can I take this? - Okay, take it.
1come out even (with money, measuring food items, etc.)
obtain back (money, means)—ङाहइ खार्चा काहजे पेहक्‍यो, ओहजे ङाहला लोहङ कोहज्‍ये!I must at least obtain as much as I spent!
big iron pot with low rims and a flat bottom
other—ङाहगी थो़ला ल्‍हा लो़हलो छि्यम्‍बु म़ेह!There is no other god greater than I am!
{celebrate} new year—दि पा़लोनी लोहसार याहम्‍बुलाराङ साहएँ।This year we will celebrate New Year in Kathmandu.
worshipping place lying a small distance to the north-east of the saddle at the foot of Yangri Kahng
be stiff and aching with cold—खावा नाहङला डोसीमाना काङबा ल्‍हाङगेन।If you walk (barefoot) in snow your feet get stiff and aching.
small calendar used to look up fortune {look up}
after—खो साहगे ल्‍हामो, ङाहराङ साहगेन।I will eat after him.
rituals performed for wishing good luck and success
paint gods (on paper or walls, etc.)—ङाहनी सेर्माथाङगी कोहम्‍बागी ल्‍हारीला डो कोहएँ काहल्‍सीन।As to me, I had to go to paint gods onto the walls of the temple in Sermathang.
very small settlement below ʈohngding (which is below Syuhnggan-Che)
loose (i.e. not tight, of clothes, knitting)—दि छ्युबा याहम्‍बीज्‍यी ल्‍हेपु दु।Oh, this Tibetan dress is a little loose.
