intensifier for '²looh-, ⁴loo-'—ल्‍हा़बागी काप्‍च्‍या ल्‍होक लो़गे!The wind turns over the clothes completely.

place of an activity—ओ़ह च़ूसा ग्‍या़ ग्‍या़ला प्रेहका ऱेह–ऱेह तिहप ज़्‍या़ कोहकें!Into each planting hole you must put a stick!—कास्‍यीर मासाला।in the place called KaÐir.—दि पाहरला आछाम ज्‍य़ूसा ज़़े!Make room here in the middle for Atsham to sit down!—जाम्‍माराङ ल़ूज्‍ये यिहन्‍सीमानानी फिला मी़ थोङसाला माज्‍ये!If we are going to record everything, then let's go outside where we can see!
whitewash—योहल्‍मुला लाहलागी खाङबाला सागार क्‍याहप्‍कें।In Yohlmu some people whitewash their houses.
clear up—नाम साङसीन।The weather cleared up.
go to heaven—स्‍यी़देले ओहराङ साङ क्‍य़ेहएँ नाह।After dying we go to heaven of course.
heaven and earth—देला साज्‍या नाम्‍ज्‍या छ्या़गेगी छ्या़बेनी ङाहला म़ेह।I do not have a book here, which talks about how the world came into existence.
truth—किन्‍ज्‍योगी सात माहक्‍युर्पा पेहती दि छ्य़ेदी माहङबु टा़हदी सोङ!Don't forsake the truth of God and keep reading this book a lot!
be borne out—ङाहगी माएँगी ताम सातार काहल्‍सीन।What I said was borne out (proved to be true) later.

thinking—खुमा नाहङगी डिबु, याङडाङ साम्‍ने ज़्‍या़क्‍यो, था़ रिहङ ज़्‍युङगी आदा, छ्येटो साम्‍ने ज़्‍या़क्‍यो।The money in the bag, taking them as lucky coins, I put them into it; in the same way, the older brother from a distant place, I take him as my heart-friend.
think about—साम्‍लो मेहताङ टाहक्‍पा, ताङना सेम्‍बा छेरा!It's better not to think about it, if I think about it, I get discouraged!
consonant—क, ख, ग, ङ माएँ यिहगीॱयाला साल-च्‍येह माएँ।k, kh, g, Ê - these letters are called consonants.
consult with each other—खोगी ङाहला लेहमा च्‍यी़ साल्‍दाप पेहपा यिहना ङाह स्‍यिहताङ लाहङज्‍ये म़ेहबा येहकें।If he had consulted with me once, I would not have become angry.
large, wide and rather shallow copper pot used for cooking
big commotion—मिह साहङ–सिहङ दु।There are a lot of people, all moving around.
copper or brass urn for carrying water—साहङबुङ तोङबा।empty water urn.—काहङ पेहक्‍योगी साहङबुङ।full water urn.
small silver-lined wooden bowl (used for drinking)
nearby—ओहले ङि्यहगी साहर-सुहरकी लामाॱया फेप स्‍यी़हदी कुरीम पेहसीन।And then we called the lamas from nearby and had them perform a ceremony.
{put on} amulet made of rudraksha seeds—सिउ ज़्‍या़गेमु पोहम्‍बोगी पेहजाला रा़हस्‍या ता़ तेर्कें।When giving a siw-amulet the shaman fastens rudraksha seeds to the child.

original_word: सिङग्‍यी कार्पु/ सिङग्‍यी कार्मु/ सिङग्‍यी ता़
pronunciation: ³siŋgyi ⁴karpu/ ³siŋgyi ⁴karmu/ ³siŋgyi ⁴taa
english_meaning: lion—सिङग्‍यी कार्पु ङुहर्कें/ ङोहर्कें/ ङाहर्कें।Lions roar.
small settlement near Kayn Kahng, below Yalmu Neesa
red powder used in worship—पोहम्‍बोगी रोहङबा ल्‍हाॱयाला छ्येपा तेर्केन बेले सिन्‍द्रीङ कोहएँ।The shaman needs red powder when he worships the gods of the Hindus.
strip off  (e.g. leaves off branches, bean plants off poles)—सुल्‍गार याहच्‍ची सिर्केन।pull up the pant legs.

dew—काप्‍च्‍या कुहङमु फिला लिहसीमाना सिलगी पाहङगे।If the clothes are left outside at night, the dew will make them wet.—फिला ङ्याहल्‍सीमाना ज़ुबुला सिल क्‍याहप्‍कें।If you sleep outside dew will fall on you.

wash (for one's own body parts)—छामला ज़्‍य़ूगेमु स्‍याहल्‍दोङ सिल्‍दे क्‍युर्नानी च्‍यिहलाप याल्‍गे म़ेदे स्‍याहल्‍दोङ सिल्‍ज्‍ये मेहयोहङगे।During lama retreats they are not to wash their faces for fear that some blessing would get lost when they throw away the water they used for washing.

weeds and stalks left in a field after harvesting—सा फोल्‍गेमु सिल तेन्‍दी काम्‍दी रुहप पेहती सा़गे।When the earth clods are broken up the weeds and stalks are pulled out, left to dry, and then gathered together and burnt.
part of wedding ceremony in order to kill the '¹kehk' (an evil spirit)
tremble violently (in trance)—ङि्यहगी सिहक सिहक पाहप च्‍य़ूक्‍योॱया नाहरेराङ कायाम ज़ोएँ!The ones trembling violently (because of spirit possession) we bring under control in one moment.
densely lined—प्रालाला ङ्येर्माङ सिहगे दु।His forehead is densely lined with wrinkles.
bustling movement—मिह सिहङ सिहङ दु।There is a bustling crowd.
be finished—तिङले ङि्यहमा सुमला राहम्‍ने सिहन्‍ग्‍यो।Later, after three days the dedication (of the religious monument) was finished.

finish s.th—ख्‍येगी दि ब्रुम्‍सी नाहम सिहन्‍गेन यिहम्‍बा?When are you going to finish this carpet?—दि छ्योवा ङि्यहगी गान्‍टा ङ्यी़ नाहङला सिहन च्‍य़ूगेन।We are going to finish this worship ceremony within two hours.
finish an action—ओहले थो़ला टिन काप्‍ती कोह कार्कुङॱया जाम्‍माराङ ज़ो सिहन्‍सीन।And they put on the tin roof and finished making all the doors and windows.
definitely finish an action—तिहरीङ चिक्‍पा ची़ज्‍ये सिहन्‍दा च्‍येकेँ लो।They said they will definitely finish erecting the wall today.
densely (of plants)—ङि्यहतीनी च्‍याङदा सिहप सिहप सिहप येहबा!Our barley stands very densely.—चाॱया सिहप सिहप सिहप क्‍य़े दु!Oh, the grass has sprouted very densely!
simmer (of s.th. coming to a boil)
Kiul (a settlement on the eastern side of the Melamchi River valley)
neutral past—ओहलेनी छ्य़े या़हबु पेहती लोप्‍सीन।And then (I) learned the religious books well.

{make offerings} to the house god—खाङबा टाहङमाराङ (छ्येक्‍योङ) सुङमा ओहङगे, ओहले सुङमा क्‍योङ कोहएँ। मेहक्‍योङना मिहला ने क्‍याल्‍गे।Near each house there is a house god, and he has to be satisfied. If we don't satisfy him, he will harm the people of the house.

help with the butchering of an animal and take ones share for helping—स्‍याम्‍बा स्‍युएँमु खुङगी सुती पेहक्‍यो।Skinning the animal each one took his share for the help given.—माहइ सेसाला ङाहॱआङ सुतीला स्‍य़ूहक्‍यो।When they slaughtered the buffalo I helped with the work and received my share for helping.
push s.th. into a crack or hole—पेहजाॱया, क़े माहङबु तेन्‍ना खा नाहङला मेहङगाल सुर तेर्कें!Hey kids, if you keep shouting like that I am going to push a burning piece of wood into your mouths!
placate the spirit of a deceased person
pierce—ङाहला छेर्माङ(गी) स़ूहदी!Oh, I got pricked by a thorn!
mix—मोहगी चाम्‍बा ना ङ्यी़ येहकेन फोक स़ेसीन दु।Oh, she quickly mixed up the two kinds of flour!
formal greeting of juniors to seniors—नुहमुगी मेहमेला स़े स्‍यी़हदी, ओहले मेहमेगी मेलाम क्‍याहप नाङग्‍यो।The girl formally greeted grandfather, and he blessed her.
{give} payment to s.b. for raising one's child or animal
failures—दि स़ेङ्यीॱया! स़ेदी फेन्‍दोप म़ेहबा!These failures! Having brought them up they haven't turned out well at all!
survive (used only of plants)—सेन च़ू फान्‍ज्‍यो रेहङगा स्‍यिएँ, रेहङगा सेन्‍गेन।When you plant seedlings, some will die, and some will survive.
village on the western side of the Melamchi River valley, on the slope north of Gohare Khola (above Ichok)
relative—मिहगी मिहन स्‍यार्क्‍याङ मेहथेन्‍गे, स़ेनु त़ेदी मिहला क़े क्‍याहप्‍कें।You don't address people by their names right away, you address them by the kinship term.
give a ceremonial welcome—दा़नु डुपा रिहम्‍बुर-छ्य़ेला सेन्‍डाङ पेहती फेप स्‍युहसीन।Some time ago the big lama called Íupa was ceremonially welcomed.

be astonished—दि लाहक्‍पागी योहल्‍मु ऱेह–ऱेह सोङ्यीम फुल्‍गेन्‍दीला लामा हाराङ ल़ेहसीन दु लो।The lama was very astonished about this custom of giving only a handful of alms in every place.—खोला थोङदी ङाह हा ल़ेहसीन!He astonished me!
1make a lot of noise for nothing
{make} a trial—दि तोहङस्‍यो हाजे यिहम्‍बा।This is the first trial.—ओहङगे नि मेहयोहङगे, हाजे ताएँ।Let me try if it's alright or not.
do s.th. carelessly—दाङ खोगी ज्‍यिबुराङ ल़ेह हातार हुतुर पेहसीन।Yesterday he did a very careless job.
outbid s.b—खोगी ङाहला हानावार पेहकु दु।I see, he is outbidding me!
1ground full of crevices/ holes—लाहमॱयाला हाप्‍ताङ होप्‍तोङ दु।
be greedy for s.b.'s property/ belongings
greed—मिहगी पेलाला हाराम पेहती क्‍याहल्‍गे।He claims other people's things as his possession.
pair—लाङ हाल सुम।three pairs of oxen yoked for ploughing.—खुङ ङ्यी़ हाल या़हबु डी़उ दु।They are a good pair (for work or business).
hurry up—पेम्‍बागी हुर्ता पेहसीन।Pemba hurried up.—पेम्‍बागी याहम्‍बुला डोज्‍ये हुर्ता पेहसीन।Pemba was in a hurry to go to Kathmandu.
fast (in doing work)—खुङ लाङगो म़ेदी ङि्यह लाङगो हुर्पु येहबा!Our ox works faster than theirs!—चो़मे छ्युदा म़ेदी थो़मे छ्युदा हुर्पु दु।I found out that the upper mill is faster than the lower one.

occasion—ओहले ओ़ह तोहरा छूगे हुल्‍दाङ च्‍यिपुदीनी च्‍यि ता़ह! नाम्‍गा कार्माजेराङ दु!And when (we) started the first time with the dancing circle - well, there were as many as the stars in the sky! (dancers).
spread out—ङि्यहमाला काप्‍च्‍या हेल्‍दी ज़्‍या़गे।I spread out the clothes in the sun.—हे माहरुहल्‍ज्‍यु म़ेदी हेल्‍दी ज़्‍या़क्‍यो।I spread out the potatoes so that they would not rot.
pasture above Kahng-Yuhl on the high ridge between Sermathang and Lha-Kahng Ghyang
